register.name: IoCfgAddr
register.desc: IO-Space Configuration Address. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. IO::IoCfgAddr, and IO::IoCfgData are used to access system configuration space, as defined by the PCI specification. IO::IoCfgAddr provides the address register and IO::IoCfgData provides the data port. Software sets up the configuration address by writing to IO::IoCfgAddr. Then, when an access is made to IO::IoCfgData, the processor generates the corresponding configuration access to the address specified in IO::IoCfgAddr. See 2.1.8 [Configuration Space]. IO::IoCfgAddr may only be accessed through aligned, DW IO Reads and Writes; otherwise, the accesses are passed to the appropriate IO link. Accesses to IO::IoCfgAddr and IO::IoCfgData received from an IO link are treated as all other IO transactions received from an IO link. IO::IoCfgAddr and IO::IoCfgData in the processor are not accessible from an IO link.

field.name: ConfigEn
field.desc: Configuration space enable. Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=IO Read and Write accesses are passed to the appropriate IO link and no configuration access is generated. 1=IO Read and Write accesses to IO::IoCfgData are translated into configuration cycles at the configuration address specified by this register.

field.name: ExtRegNo
field.desc: Extended register number. Read-write. Reset: 0h. ExtRegNo provides bits[11:8] and RegNo provides bits[7:2] of the byte address of the configuration register.

field.name: BusNo
field.desc: Bus number. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Specifies the bus number of the configuration cycle.

field.name: Device
field.desc: Device number. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Specifies the device number of the configuration cycle.

field.name: Function
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Specifies the function number of the configuration cycle.

field.name: RegNo
field.desc: Register address. Read-write. Reset: 00h. See IO::IoCfgAddr[ExtRegNo].

register.name: IoCfgData
register.desc: IO-Space Configuration Data Port. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: Data
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. See IO::IoCfgAddr.

register.name: TrapOffset
register.desc: SMM IO Trap Offset. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h. If the assertion of SMI is recognized on the boundary of an IO instruction, Core::X86::Smm::TrapOffset contains information about that IO instruction. For example, if an IO access targets an unavailable device, the system can assert SMI and trap the IO instruction. Core::X86::Smm::TrapOffset then provides the SMI handler with information about the IO instruction that caused the trap. After the SMI handler takes the appropriate action, it can reconstruct and then re-execute the IO instruction from SMM. Or, more likely, it can use Core::X86::Smm::IoRestart to cause the core to re-execute the IO instruction immediately after resuming from SMM.

field.name: Port
field.desc: Trapped IO port address. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000h. This provides the address of the IO instruction.

field.name: BPR
field.desc: IO breakpoint match. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0h.

field.name: TF
field.desc: EFLAGS TF value. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0.

field.name: SZ32
field.desc: Size 32 bits. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Port access was 32 bits.

field.name: SZ16
field.desc: Size 16 bits. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Port access was 16 bits.

field.name: SZ8
field.desc: Size 8 bits. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Port access was 8 bits.

field.name: REP
field.desc: Repeated port access. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0.

field.name: STR
field.desc: String-based port access. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0.

field.name: V
field.desc: IO trap word valid. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 0=The other fields of this offset are not valid. 1=The core entered SMM on an IO instruction boundary; all information in this offset is valid.

field.name: RW
field.desc: Port access type. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 0=IO Write (OUT instruction). 1=IO Read (IN instruction).

register.name: LocalSmiStatus
register.desc: Local SMI Status. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h. This offset stores status bits associated with SMI sources local to the core. For each of these bits, 1=The associated mechanism generated an SMI.

field.name: MceRedirSts
field.desc: Machine check exception redirection status. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. This bit is associated with the SMI source specified in Core::X86::Msr::McExcepRedir[RedirSmiEn].

field.name: IoTrapSts
field.desc: IO trap status. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0h. Each of these bits is associated with each of the respective SMI sources specified in Core::X86::Msr::SMI_ON_IO_TRAP.

register.name: IoRestart
register.desc: IO Restart Byte. Read-write. Reset: 00h. If the core entered SMM on an IO instruction boundary, the SMI handler may write this to FFh. This causes the core to re-execute the trapped IO instruction immediately after resuming from SMM. The SMI handler should only write to this byte if Core::X86::Smm::TrapOffset[V] == 1; otherwise, the behavior is undefined. If a second SMI is asserted while a valid IO instruction is trapped by the first SMI handler, the core services the second SMI prior to re-executing the trapped IO instruction. Core::X86::Smm::TrapOffset[V] == 0 during the second entry into SMM, and the second SMI handler must not rewrite this byte. If there is a simultaneous SMI IO instruction trap and debug breakpoint trap, the processor first responds to the SMI and postpones recognizing the debug exception until after resuming from SMM. If debug registers other than DR6 and DR7 are used while in SMM, they must be saved and restored by the SMI handler. If Core::X86::Smm::IoRestart is set to FFh when the RSM instruction is executed, the debug trap does not occur until after the IO instruction is re-executed.

field.name: RST
field.desc: SMM IO Restart Byte. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

register.name: AutoHalt
register.desc: Auto Halt Restart Offset. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: HLT
field.desc: Halt restart. Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=Entered SMM on a normal x86 instruction boundary. 1=Entered SMM from the Halt state. Upon SMM entry, this bit indicates whether SMM was entered from the Halt state. Before returning from SMM, this bit can be written by the SMI handler to specify whether the return from SMM should take the processor back to the Halt state or to the instruction-execution state specified by the SMM state save area (normally, the instruction after the halt). Clearing this bit the returns to the instruction specified in the SMM save state. Setting this bit returns to the halt state. If the return from SMM takes the processor back to the Halt state, the HLT instruction is not refetched and re-executed. However, the Halt special bus cycle is broadcast and the processor enters the Halt state.

register.name: NmiMask
register.desc: NMI Mask. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: NmiMask
field.desc: NMI Mask. Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=NMI not masked. 1=NMI masked. Specifies whether NMI was masked upon entry to SMM.

register.name: SvmState
register.desc: SMM SVM State. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. This offset stores the SVM state of the processor upon entry into SMM.

field.name: HostEflagesIF
field.desc: Host EFLAGS IF. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0.

field.name: SvmState
field.desc: Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0h. ValidValues: 0h - SMM entered from a non-guest state. 1h - Reserved. 2h - SMM entered from a guest state. 5h-3h - Reserved. 6h - SMM entered from a guest state with nested paging enabled. 7h - Reserved.

register.name: SmmRevID
register.desc: SMM Revision Identifier. Read-only. Reset: 0003_0064h. This offset stores the SVM state of the processor upon entry into SMM.

field.name: BRL
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=Base relocation supported.

field.name: IOTrap
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=IO trap supported.

field.name: Revision
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0064h.

register.name: SmmBase
register.desc: SMM Base Address. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. This offset stores the base of the SMM-State of the processor upon entry into SMM.

field.name: SmmBase
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h. See Core::X86::Msr::SMM_BASE[SmmBase].

register.name: ApicId
register.desc: APIC ID. Read-only.

field.name: ApicId
field.desc: APIC ID. Read-only. Reset: XXh. The reset value varies based on core number. See 2.1.14.2.1.3 [ApicId Enumeration Requirements].

register.name: ApicVersion
register.desc: APIC Version. Read-only.

field.name: ExtApicSpace
field.desc: Extended APIC register space present. Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=Indicates the presence of extended APIC register space starting at Core::X86::Apic::ExtendedApicFeature.

field.name: DirectedEoiSupport
field.desc: Directed EOI support. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. 0=Directed EOI capability not supported.

field.name: MaxLvtEntry
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: XXh. Specifies the number of entries in the local vector table minus one.

field.name: Version
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 10h. Indicates the version number of this APIC implementation.

register.name: TaskPriority
register.desc: Task Priority. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: Priority
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. This field is assigned by software to set a threshold priority at which the core is interrupted.

register.name: ArbitrationPriority
register.desc: Arbitration Priority. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: Priority
field.desc: Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 00h. Indicates the current priority for a pending interrupt, or a task or interrupt being serviced by the core. The priority is used to arbitrate between cores to determine which accepts a lowest-priority interrupt request.

register.name: ProcessorPriority
register.desc: Processor Priority. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: Priority
field.desc: Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 00h. Indicates the core's current priority servicing a task or interrupt, and is used to determine if any pending interrupts should be serviced. It is the higher value of the task priority value and the current highest in-service interrupt.

register.name: EndOfInterrupt
register.desc: End of Interrupt. Write-only. This register is written by the software interrupt handler to indicate the servicing of the current interrupt is complete.

register.name: RemoteRead
register.desc: Reserved. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h. Remote Read is deprecated.

register.name: LocalDestination
register.desc: Logical Destination. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: Destination
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 00h. This APIC's destination identification. Used to determine which interrupts should be accepted.

register.name: DestinationFormat
register.desc: Destination Format. Read-write. Reset: F000_0000h. Only supported in xAPIC mode.

field.name: Format
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Fh. Controls which format to use when accepting interrupts with a logical destination mode. ValidValues: 0h - Cluster destinations are used. Eh-1h - Reserved. Fh - Flat destinations are used.

register.name: SpuriousInterruptVector
register.desc: Spurious-Interrupt Vector. Reset: 0000_00FFh.

field.name: FocusDisable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. 1=Disable focus core checking during lowest-priority arbitrated interrupts.

field.name: APICSWEn
field.desc: APIC software enable. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 0=SMI, NMI, INIT, LINT[1:0], and Startup interrupts may be accepted; pending interrupts in Core::X86::Apic::InService and Core::X86::Apic::InterruptRequest are held, but further fixed, lowest-priority, and ExtInt interrupts are not accepted. All LVT entry mask bits are set and cannot be cleared.

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: FFh. The vector that is sent to the core in the event of a spurious interrupt.

register.name: InService
register.desc: In-Service. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h. The in-service registers provide a bit per interrupt to indicate that the corresponding interrupt is being serviced by the core. The first 16 InServiceBits of the first Core::X86::Apic::InService register are Reserved.

field.name: InServiceBits
field.desc: Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h. These bits are set when the corresponding interrupt is being serviced by the core.

register.name: TriggerMode
register.desc: Trigger Mode. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h. The trigger mode registers provide a bit per interrupt to indicate the assertion mode of each interrupt. The first 16 TriggerModeBits of the each thread's APIC[1F0:180] registers are Reserved.

field.name: TriggerModeBits
field.desc: Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h. The corresponding trigger mode bit is updated when an interrupt is accepted. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. ValidValues: Bit [0] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [1] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [2] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [3] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [4] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [5] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [6] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [7] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [8] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [9] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [10] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [11] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [12] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [13] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [14] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [15] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [16] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [17] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [18] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [19] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [20] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [21] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [22] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [23] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [24] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [25] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [26] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [27] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [28] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [29] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [30] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [31] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt.

register.name: InterruptRequest
register.desc: Interrupt Request. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h. The interrupt request registers provide a bit per interrupt to indicate that the corresponding interrupt has been accepted by the APIC. The first 16 RequestBits of the first Core::X86::Apic::InterruptRequest register are Reserved.

field.name: RequestBits
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h. The corresponding request bit is set when the an interrupt is accepted by the APIC. ValidValues: Bit [0] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [1] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [2] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [3] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [4] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [5] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [6] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [7] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [8] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [9] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [10] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [11] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [12] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [13] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [14] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [15] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [16] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [17] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [18] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [19] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [20] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [21] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [22] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [23] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [24] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [25] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [26] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [27] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [28] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [29] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [30] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set. [31] - 0=Request bit not set. 1=Request bit set.

register.name: ErrorStatus
register.desc: Error Status. Writes to this register trigger an update of the register state. The value written by software is arbitrary. Each write causes the internal error state to be loaded into this register, clearing the internal error state. Consequently, a second write prior to the occurrence of another error causes the register to be overwritten with cleared data.

field.name: IllegalRegAddr
field.desc: Illegal register address. Read-write. Reset: 0. This bit indicates that an access to a nonexistent register location within this APIC was attempted. Can only be set in xAPIC mode.

field.name: RcvdIllegalVector
field.desc: Received illegal vector. Read-write. Reset: 0. This bit indicates that this APIC has received a message with an illegal vector (00h to 0Fh for fixed and lowest priority interrupts).

field.name: SentIllegalVector
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. This bit indicates that this APIC attempted to send a message with an illegal vector (00h to 0Fh for fixed and lowest priority interrupts).

field.name: RcvAcceptError
field.desc: Receive accept error. Read-write. Reset: 0. This bit indicates that a message received by this APIC was not accepted by this or any other APIC.

field.name: SendAcceptError
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. This bit indicates that a message sent by this APIC was not accepted by any APIC.

register.name: InterruptCommandLow
register.desc: Interrupt Command Low. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: DestShrthnd
field.desc: Destination shorthand. Read-write. Reset: 0h. Description: Provides a quick way to specify a destination for a message. If all including self or all excluding self is used, then destination mode is ignored and physical is automatically used. ValidValues: 0h - No shorthand (Destination field). 1h - Self. 2h - All including self. 3h - All excluding self (This sends a message with a destination encoding of all 1s, so if lowest priority is used the message could end up being reflected back to this APIC).

field.name: RemoteRdStat
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0h. ValidValues: 0h - Read was invalid. 1h - Delivery pending. 2h - Delivery complete and access was valid. 3h - Reserved.

field.name: TM
field.desc: Trigger mode. Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=Edge triggered. 1=Level triggered. Indicates how this interrupt is triggered.

field.name: Level
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=De-asserted. 1=Asserted.

field.name: DS
field.desc: Interrupt delivery status. Read-only. Reset: 0. 0=Idle. 1=Send pending. In xAPIC mode this bit is set to indicate that the interrupt has not yet been accepted by the destination core(s). Software may repeatedly write Core::X86::Apic::InterruptCommandLow without polling the DS bit; all requested IPIs are delivered.

field.name: DM
field.desc: Destination mode. Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=Physical. 1=Logical.

field.name: MsgType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. The message types are encoded as follows: ValidValues: 0h - Fixed. 1h - Lowest Priority. 2h - SMI. 3h - Reserved. 4h - NMI. 5h - INIT. 6h - Startup. 7h - External interrupt.

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. The vector that is sent for this interrupt source.

register.name: InterruptCommandHigh
register.desc: Interrupt Command High. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: DestinationField
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. The destination encoding used when Core::X86::Apic::InterruptCommandLow[DestShrthnd] is 00b.

register.name: TimerLvtEntry
register.desc: LVT Timer. Reset: 0001_0000h.

field.name: Mode
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=One-shot. 1=Periodic.

field.name: Mask
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. 0=Not masked. 1=Masked.

field.name: DS
field.desc: Interrupt delivery status. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 0=Idle. 1=Send pending. (Indicates that the interrupt has not yet been accepted by the core.).

field.name: MsgType
field.desc: Message type. Read-write. Reset: 0h. See2.1.14.2.1.14 [Generalized Local Vector Table].

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Interrupt vector number.

register.name: ThermalLvtEntry
register.desc: LVT Thermal Sensor. Reset: 0001_0000h.

field.name: Mask
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. 0=Not masked. 1=Masked.

field.name: DS
field.desc: Interrupt delivery status. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 0=Idle. 1=Send pending. (Indicates that the interrupt has not yet been accepted by the core.).

field.name: MsgType
field.desc: Message type. Read-write. Reset: 0h. See2.1.14.2.1.14 [Generalized Local Vector Table].

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Interrupt vector number.

register.name: PerformanceCounterLvtEntry
register.desc: LVT Performance Monitor. Reset: 0001_0000h. Interrupts for this local vector table are caused by overflows of: 1) Core::X86::Msr::PERF_LEGACY_CTL0..3(Performance Event Select [3:0]). 2) Core::X86::Msr::PERF_CTL0..5(Performance Event Select [5:0]).

field.name: Mask
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. 0=Not masked. 1=Masked.

field.name: DS
field.desc: Interrupt delivery status. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 0=Idle. 1=Send pending. (Indicates that the interrupt has not yet been accepted by the core.).

field.name: MsgType
field.desc: Message type. Read-write. Reset: 0h. See2.1.14.2.1.14 [Generalized Local Vector Table].

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Interrupt vector number.

register.name: LVTLINT
register.desc: LVT LINT[1:0]. Reset: 0001_0000h.

field.name: Mask
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. 0=Not masked. 1=Masked.

field.name: TM
field.desc: Trigger mode. Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=Edge. 1=Level.

field.name: RmtIRR
field.desc: Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. If trigger mode is level, remote Core::X86::Apic::InterruptRequest is set when the interrupt has begun service. Remote Core::X86::Apic::InterruptRequest is cleared when the end of interrupt has occurred.

field.name: DS
field.desc: Interrupt delivery status. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 0=Idle. 1=Send pending. (Indicates that the interrupt has not yet been accepted by the core.).

field.name: MsgType
field.desc: Message type. Read-write. Reset: 0h. See2.1.14.2.1.14 [Generalized Local Vector Table].

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Interrupt vector number.

register.name: ErrorLvtEntry
register.desc: LVT Error. Reset: 0001_0000h.

field.name: Mask
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. 0=Not masked. 1=Masked.

field.name: DS
field.desc: Interrupt delivery status. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 0=Idle. 1=Send pending. (Indicates that the interrupt has not yet been accepted by the core.).

field.name: MsgType
field.desc: Message type. Read-write. Reset: 0h. See 2.1.14.2.1.14 [Generalized Local Vector Table].

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Interrupt vector number.

register.name: TimerInitialCount
register.desc: Timer Initial Count. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: Count
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h. The value copied into the current count register when the timer is loaded or reloaded.

register.name: TimerCurrentCount
register.desc: Timer Current Count. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: Count
field.desc: Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h. The current value of the counter.

register.name: TimerDivideConfiguration
register.desc: Timer Divide Configuration. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: Div[3:0]
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Div[2] is unused. ValidValues: 0h - Divide by 2. 1h - Divide by 4. 2h - Divide by 8. 3h - Divide by 16. 7h-4h - Reserved. 8h - Divide by 32. 9h - Divide by 64. Ah - Divide by 128. Bh - Divide by 1. Fh-Ch - Reserved.

register.name: ExtendedApicFeature
register.desc: Extended APIC Feature. Read-only. Reset: 0004_0007h.

field.name: ExtLvtCount
field.desc: Extended local vector table count. Read-only. Reset: 04h. This specifies the number of extended LVT registers (Core::X86::Apic::ExtendedInterruptLvtEntries) in the local APIC.

field.name: ExtApicIdCap
field.desc: Extended APIC ID capable. Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=The processor is capable of supporting an 8-bit APIC ID, as controlled by Core::X86::Apic::ExtendedApicControl[ExtApicIdEn].

field.name: SeoiCap
field.desc: Specific end of interrupt capable. Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=The Core::X86::Apic::SpecificEndOfInterrupt is present.

field.name: IerCap
field.desc: Interrupt enable register capable. Read-only. Reset: 1. This bit indicates that the Core::X86::Apic::InterruptEnable are present. See2.1.14.2.1.8 [Interrupt Masking].

register.name: ExtendedApicControl
register.desc: Extended APIC Control. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: ExtApicIdEn
field.desc: Extended APIC ID enable. Read-write. Reset: 0. 1=Enable 8-bit APIC ID; Core::X86::Apic::ApicId[ApicId] supports an 8-bit value; an interrupt broadcast in physical destination mode requires that the IntDest[7:0] == 1111_1111b (instead of XXXX_1111b); a match in physical destination mode occurs when (IntDest[7:0] == ApicId[7:0]) instead of (IntDest[3:0] == ApicId[3:0]).

field.name: SeoiEn
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. 1=Enable SEOI generation when a Write to Core::X86::Apic::SpecificEndOfInterrupt is received.

field.name: IerEn
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. 1=Enable writes to the interrupt enable registers.

register.name: SpecificEndOfInterrupt
register.desc: Specific End Of Interrupt. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: EoiVec
field.desc: End of interrupt vector. Read-write. Reset: 00h. A Write to this field causes an end of interrupt cycle to be performed for the vector specified in this field. The behavior is undefined if no interrupt is pending for the specified interrupt vector.

register.name: InterruptEnable
register.desc: Interrupt Enable. Read-write. Reset: FFFF_FFFFh.

field.name: InterruptEnableBits
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: FFFF_FFFFh. The interrupt enable bits can be used to enable each of the 256 interrupts.

register.name: ExtendedInterruptLvtEntries
register.desc: Extended Interrupt Local Vector Table. Reset: 0001_0000h. Assignments conventions: 1) APIC500 provides a local vector table entry for IBS. 2) APIC510 provides a local vector table entry for error thresholding. See Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg[ThresholdLvtOffset]. 3) APIC520 provides a local vector table entry for Deferred errors. See MCi_CONFIG[DeferredIntType].

field.name: Mask
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. 0=Not masked. 1=Masked.

field.name: DS
field.desc: Interrupt delivery status. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 0=Idle. 1=Send pending. (Indicates that the interrupt has not yet been accepted by the core.).

field.name: MsgType
field.desc: Message type. Read-write. Reset: 0h. See2.1.14.2.1.14 [Generalized Local Vector Table].

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Interrupt vector number.

register.name: LargFuncNum

field.name: LFuncStd
field.desc: Largest standard function. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0010h. The largest CPUID standard function input value supported by the processor implementation.

register.name: ProcVendEbx
register.desc: Processor Vendor (ASCII Bytes [3:0]). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,6874_7541h. Core::X86::Cpuid::ProcVendEbx and Core::X86::Cpuid::ProcVendExtEbx return the same value.

field.name: Vendor
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,6874_7541h. ASCII Bytes [3:0] ("h t u A") of the string "AuthenticAMD".

register.name: ProcVendEcx
register.desc: Processor Vendor (ASCII Bytes [11:8]). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,444D_4163h. Core::X86::Cpuid::ProcVendEcx and Core::X86::Cpuid::ProcVendExtEcx return the same value.

field.name: Vendor
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,444D_4163h. ASCII Bytes [11:8] ("D M A c") of the string "AuthenticAMD".

register.name: ProcVendEdx
register.desc: Processor Vendor (ASCII Bytes [7:4]). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,6974_6E65h. Core::X86::Cpuid::ProcVendEdx and Core::X86::Cpuid::ProcVendExtEdx return the same value.

field.name: Vendor
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,6974_6E65h. ASCII Bytes [7:4] ("i t n e") of the string "AuthenticAMD".

register.name: FamModStep
register.desc: Family, Model, Stepping Identifiers. Read-only. Core::X86::Cpuid::FamModStep and Core::X86::Cpuid::FamModStepExt return the same value. Family: Is an 8-bit value and is defined as: Family[7:0]=({0000b,BaseFamily[3:0]}+ExtendedFamily[7:0]). 1) E.g., If BaseFamily[3:0] == Fh and ExtendedFamily[7:0] == 08h, then Family[7:0] = 17h. Model: Is an 8-bit value and is defined as: Model[7:0]={ExtendedModel[3:0],BaseModel[3:0]}. 1) E.g., If ExtendedModel[3:0] == 1h and BaseModel[3:0] == 8h, then Model[7:0] = 18h. 2) Model numbers vary with product. Model numbers are are assigned a letter, 0h = "A", 1h = "B", and so on. Model and Stepping form the Revision. E.g., B1.

field.name: ExtFamily
field.desc: Extended family. Read-only. Reset: 0Ah. See Family above.

field.name: ExtModel
field.desc: Extended model. Read-only. Reset: 0h. See Model above.

field.name: BaseFamily
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fh. See Family description above.

field.name: BaseModel
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Xh. Model numbers vary with product.

field.name: Stepping
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Xh. Processor stepping (revision) for a specific model.

register.name: ExtTopEnumEax0
register.desc: Extended Topology Enumeration. Read-only. Enable: (Core::X86::Cpuid::ExtTopEnumEbx0 > 0). CPUID Fn0000_000B_E[D,C,B,A]X_x[2:0] specifies the hierarchy of logical cores from the SMT level through the processor socket level. Software determines the presence of CPUID Fn0000_000B if (CPUID Fn0000_000B_EBX_x0[31:0] != 0). Software reads CPUID Fn0000_000B_E[C,B,A]X for ascending values of ECX until (CPUID Fn0000_000B_EBX[LogProcAtThisLevel] == 0).

field.name: XFeatureSupportedMask[31:0]
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0207h. Each set bit indicates the corresponding bit in register XCR0[31:0] is settable. ValidValues: Bit [0] (X87) - X87 Support. [1] (SSE) - 128-bit SSE Support. [2] (AVX) - 256-bit AVX support. [8:3] - Reserved. [9] (MPK) - Memory Protection Keys. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEcx0[PKU] for the. [31:10] - Reserved.

register.name: ProcExtStateEnumEbx00
register.desc: Processor Extended State Enumeration. Read-only,Volatile.

field.name: XFeatureEnabledSizeMax
field.desc: Read-only,Volatile. Reset: XXXX_XXXXh. Description: Size in bytes of an uncompacted XSAVE/XRSTOR area for all features enabled in the XCR0 register. IF (XCR0[MPK] == 1) Return EBX=0000_0988h // legacy header + X87/SSE + AVX + MPK size ELSIF (XCR0[AVX] == 1) Return EBX = 0000_0340h // legacy header + X87/SSE + AVX size ELSIF (XCR0[SSE] == 1) Return EBX = 0000_0240h // legacy header + X87/SSE size ELSIF (XCR0[X87] == 1) Return EBX=0000_0240h END.

field.name: CounterSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,00h. Encode counter width offset from bit[24]. ValidValues: 00h - Family/Model/Stepping should be used to determine counter size; 44-bits for this product. 26h-01h - <Value>+24-bit counters. FFh-27h - Reserved.

register.name: RsrcDirTechAllocEnumEax0
register.desc: Resource Director Technology Allocation Enumeration. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0000h. Enable: (Core::X86::Cpuid::RsrcDirTechAllocEnumEbx0 > 0). Software determines the presence of CPUID Fn0000_0010 if (CPUID Fn0000_0010_EBX_x0[31:0] != 0). Software reads CPUID Fn0000_0010_E[D,C,B,A]X for ascending values of ECX until (CPUID Fn0000_0010_EBX[LogProcAtThisLevel] == 0).

field.name: AllocUnits

register.desc: Resource Director Technology L3 Allocation Enumeration. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0004h. Enable: (Core::X86::Cpuid::RsrcDirTechAllocEnumEbx1 > 0).

field.name: CDP
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Code and data prioritization.

register.name: RsrcDirTechAllocEnumEdx1
register.desc: Resource Director Technology L3 Allocation Enumeration. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_000Fh. Enable: (Core::X86::Cpuid::RsrcDirTechAllocEnumEbx1 > 0).

field.name: HCS
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,000Fh. Highest COS supported.

register.name: LargExtFuncNum
register.desc: Largest Extended Function Number. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,8000_0023h.

field.name: LFuncExt
field.desc: Largest extended function. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,8000_0023h. The largest CPUID extended function input value supported by the processor implementation.

register.name: ProcVendExtEbx
register.desc: Processor Vendor (ASCII Bytes [3:0]). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,6874_7541h. Core::X86::Cpuid::ProcVendEbx and Core::X86::Cpuid::ProcVendExtEbx return the same value.

field.name: Vendor
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,6874_7541h. ASCII Bytes [3:0] ("h t u A") of the string "AuthenticAMD".

register.name: ProcVendExtEcx
register.desc: Processor Vendor (ASCII Bytes [11:8]). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,444D_4163h. Core::X86::Cpuid::ProcVendEcx and Core::X86::Cpuid::ProcVendExtEcx return the same value.

field.name: Vendor
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,444D_4163h. ASCII Bytes [11:8] ("D M A c") of the string "AuthenticAMD".

register.name: ProcVendExtEdx
register.desc: Processor Vendor (ASCII Bytes [7:4]). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,6974_6E65h. Core::X86::Cpuid::ProcVendEdx and Core::X86::Cpuid::ProcVendExtEdx return the same value.

field.name: Vendor
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,6974_6E65h. ASCII Bytes [7:4] ("i t n e") of the string "AuthenticAMD".

register.name: FamModStepExt
register.desc: Family, Model, Stepping Identifiers. Read-only. Core::X86::Cpuid::FamModStep and Core::X86::Cpuid::FamModStepExt return the same value. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FamModStep.

field.name: ExtFamily
field.desc: Extended family. Read-only. Reset: 0Ah. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FamModStep description of Family.

field.name: ExtModel
field.desc: Extended model. Read-only. Reset: 0h. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FamModStep description of ExtModel.

field.name: BaseFamily
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fh. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FamModStep description of Family.

field.name: BaseModel
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Xh. Model numbers vary with product.

field.name: Stepping
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1h. Processor stepping (revision) for a specific model.

register.name: BrandId
register.desc: BrandId Identifier. Read-only.

field.name: PkgType
field.desc: Package type. Read-only. Reset: Xh. Specifies the package type.

register.name: FeatureExtIdEcx
register.desc: Feature Identifiers. Read-only. These values can be over-written by Core::X86::Msr::CPUID_ExtFeatures.

field.name: AdMskExtn
field.desc: Address mask extension support for instruction breakpoint. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Indicates support for address mask extension (to 32 bits and to all 4 DRs) for instruction breakpoints.

field.name: MwaitExtended
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: !Core::X86::Msr::HWCR[MonMwaitDis]. 1=MWAITX and MONITORX capability is supported.

field.name: PerfCtrExtLLC
field.desc: Last Level Cache performance counter extensions. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=Indicates support for Core::X86::Msr::ChL3PmcCfg and Core::X86::Msr::ChL3Pmc L3 performance counter extensions. L3 performance counter extensions support. See 2.1.17.5 [L3 Cache Performance Monitor Counters] and 2.1.17 [Performance Monitor Counters].

field.name: PerfTsc
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. Performance time-stamp counter supported.

field.name: DataBreakpointExtension
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=Indicates data breakpoint support for Core::X86::Msr::DR0_ADDR_MASK, Core::X86::Msr::DR1_ADDR_MASK, Core::X86::Msr::DR2_ADDR_MASK and Core::X86::Msr::DR3_ADDR_MASK.

field.name: PerfCtrExtDF
field.desc: Data fabric performance counter extensions support. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=Indicates support for Core::X86::Msr::DF_PERF_CTL and Core::X86::Msr::DF_PERF_CTR.

field.name: PerfCtrExtCore
field.desc: Core performance counter extensions support. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=Indicates support for Core::X86::Msr::PERF_CTL0 - 5 and Core::X86::Msr::PERF_CTR. See See 2.1.17.4 [Core Performance Monitor Counters] and 2.1.17 [Performance Monitor Counters].

field.name: TopologyExtensions
field.desc: Topology extensions support. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=Indicates support for Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0 and Core::X86::Cpuid::ExtApicId.

field.name: TCE
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Translation cache extension.

field.name: FMA4
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. Four-operand FMA instruction support.

field.name: LWP
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. Lightweight profiling support.

field.name: WDT
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Watchdog timer support.

field.name: SKINIT
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. SKINIT and STGI support.

field.name: XOP
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. Extended operation support.

field.name: IBS
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Instruction Based Sampling.

field.name: OSVW
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. OS Visible Work-around support.

field.name: ThreeDNowPrefetch
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Prefetch and PrefetchW instructions.

field.name: MisAlignSse
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Misaligned SSE Mode.

field.name: SSE4A
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. EXTRQ, INSERTQ, MOVNTSS, and MOVNTSD instruction support.

field.name: ABM
field.desc: Advanced bit manipulation. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. LZCNT instruction support.

field.name: AltMovCr8
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. LOCK MOV CR0 means MOV CR8.

field.name: ExtApicSpace
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Extended APIC register space.

field.name: SVM
field.desc: Secure Virtual Mode feature. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Indicates support for: VMRUN, VMLOAD, VMSAVE, CLGI, VMMCALL, and INVLPGA.

field.name: CmpLegacy
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,(Core::X86::Cpuid::SizeId[NC] > 0). 0=Single core product (Core::X86::Cpuid::SizeId[NC] == 0). 1=Multi core product (Core::X86::Cpuid::SizeId[NC] !=0 ). Core multi-processing legacy mode.

field.name: LahfSahf
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. LAHF and SAHF instruction support in 64-bit mode.

register.name: FeatureExtIdEdx
register.desc: Feature Identifiers. Read-only. These values can be over-written by Core::X86::Msr::CPUID_ExtFeatures.

field.name: ThreeDNow
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. 3DNow! instructions.

field.name: ThreeDNowExt
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. AMD extensions to 3DNow! instructions.

field.name: LM
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Long Mode.

field.name: RDTSCP
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. RDTSCP instruction.

field.name: Page1GB
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1-GB large page support.

field.name: FFXSR
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. FXSAVE and FXRSTOR instruction optimizations.

field.name: FXSR
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. FXSAVE and FXRSTOR instructions.

field.name: MMX
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. MMX instructions.

field.name: MmxExt
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. AMD extensions to MMX instructions.

field.name: NX
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. No-execute page protection.

field.name: PSE36
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Page-size extensions.

field.name: PAT
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Page attribute table.

field.name: CMOV
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Conditional move instructions, CMOV, FCOMI, FCMOV.

field.name: MCA
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Machine check architecture, MCG_CAP.

field.name: PGE
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Page global extension, CR4.PGE.

field.name: MTRR
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Memory-type range registers.

field.name: SysCallSysRet
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. SYSCALL and SYSRET instructions.

field.name: APIC
field.desc: Advanced programmable interrupt controller (APIC) exists and is enabled. Read-only. Reset: X. Reset is Core::X86::Msr::APIC_BAR[ApicEn].

field.name: CMPXCHG8B
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. CMPXCHG8B instruction.

field.name: MCE
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Machine Check Exception, CR4.MCE.

field.name: PAE
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Physical-address extensions (PAE).

field.name: MSR
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Model-specific registers (MSRs), with RDMSR and WRMSR instructions.

field.name: TSC
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Time stamp counter, RDTSC/RDTSCP instructions, CR4.TSD.

field.name: PSE
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Page-size extensions (4 MB pages).

field.name: DE
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Debugging extensions, IO breakpoints, CR4.DE.

field.name: VME
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Virtual-mode enhancements.

field.name: FPU
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. x87 floating-point unit on-chip.
