register.name: L1Tlb2M4M
register.desc: L1 TLB 2M/4M Identifiers. Read-only. This function provides the processor's first level cache and TLB characteristics for each core.

field.name: L1DTlb2and4MAssoc
field.desc: Data TLB associativity for 2-MB and 4-MB pages. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,FFh. See Core::X86::Cpuid::L1DcId[L1DcAssoc].

field.name: L1DTlb2and4MSize
field.desc: Data TLB number of entries for 2-MB and 4 MB-pages. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,64. The value returned is for the number of entries available for the 2-MB page size; 4-MB pages require two 2-MB entries, so the number of entries available for the 4-MB page size is one-half the returned value.

field.name: L1ITlb2and4MAssoc
field.desc: Instruction TLB associativity for 2-MB and 4 MB-pages. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,FFh. See Core::X86::Cpuid::L1DcId[L1DcAssoc].

field.name: L1ITlb2and4MSize
field.desc: Instruction TLB number of entries for 2-MB and 4-MB pages. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,64. The value returned is for the number of entries available for the 2-MB page size; 4-MB pages require two 2-MB entries, so the number of entries available for the 4-MB page size is one-half the returned value.

register.name: L1Tlb4K
register.desc: L1 TLB 4K Identifiers. Read-only. See Core::X86::Cpuid::L1Tlb2M4M.

field.name: L1DTlb4KAssoc
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,FFh. Data TLB associativity for 4-KB pages. See Core::X86::Cpuid::L1DcId[L1DcAssoc].

field.name: L1DTlb4KSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,64. Data TLB number of entries for 4-KB pages.

field.name: L1ITlb4KAssoc
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,FFh. Instruction TLB associativity for 4-KB pages. See Core::X86::Cpuid::L1DcId[L1DcAssoc].

field.name: L1ITlb4KSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,64. Instruction TLB number of entries for 4-KB pages.

register.name: L1DcId
register.desc: L1 Data Cache Identifiers. Read-only. This function provides first level cache characteristics for each core.

field.name: L1DcSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,32. L1 data cache size in KB.

field.name: L1DcAssoc
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,8. L1 data cache associativity. ValidValues: 00h - Reserved. 01h - 1 way (direct mapped). 02h - 2 way. 03h - 3 way. FEh-04h - <Value> way. FFh - Fully associative.

field.name: L1DcLinesPerTag
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,01h. L1 data cache lines per tag.

field.name: L1DcLineSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,64. L1 data cache line size in bytes.

register.name: L1IcId
register.desc: L1 Instruction Cache Identifiers. Read-only. This function provides first level cache characteristics for each core.

field.name: L1IcSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,32. L1 instruction cache size KB.

field.name: L1IcAssoc
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,8. L1 instruction cache associativity. ValidValues: 00h - Reserved. 01h - 1 way (direct mapped). 02h - 2 way. 03h - 3 way. 04h - 4 way. FEh-05h - <Value> way. FFh - Fully associative.

field.name: L1IcLinesPerTag
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,01h. L1 instruction cache lines per tag.

field.name: L1IcLineSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,64. L1 instruction cache line size in bytes.

register.name: L2Tlb2M4M
register.desc: L2 TLB 2M/4M Identifiers. Read-only. This function provides the processor's second level cache and TLB characteristics for each core.

field.name: L2DTlb2and4MAssoc
field.desc: L2 data TLB associativity for 2-MB and 4-MB pages. Read-only. Reset: Xh.

field.name: L2DTlb2and4MSize
field.desc: L2 data TLB number of entries for 2-MB and 4-MB pages. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,2048. The value returned is for the number of entries available for the 2-MB page size; 4-MB pages require two 2-MB entries, so the number of entries available for the 4-MB page size is one-half the returned value.

field.name: L2ITlb2and4MAssoc
field.desc: L2 instruction TLB associativity for 2-MB and 4-MB pages. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,2. ValidValues: 1h-0h - Reserved. 2h - 2 ways. Fh-3h - Reserved.

field.name: L2ITlb2and4MSize
field.desc: L2 instruction TLB number of entries for 2-MB and 4-MB pages. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,512. The value returned is for the number of entries available for the 2-MB page size; 4-MB pages require two 2-MB entries, so the number of entries available for the 4-MB page size is one-half the returned value.

register.name: L2Tlb4K
register.desc: L2 TLB 4K Identifiers. Read-only. This function provides the processor's second level cache and TLB characteristics for each core.

field.name: L2DTlb4KAssoc
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 6h. L2 data TLB associativity for 4-KB pages. ValidValues: 5h-0h - Reserved. 6h - 8 ways. Fh-7h - Reserved.

field.name: L2DTlb4KSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,2048. L2 data TLB number of entries for 4-KB pages.

field.name: L2ITlb4KAssoc
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,4. L2 instruction TLB associativity for 4-KB pages. ValidValues: 3h-0h - Reserved. 4h - 4 ways. Fh-5h - Reserved.

field.name: L2ITlb4KSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,512. L2 instruction TLB number of entries for 4-KB pages.

register.name: L2CacheId
register.desc: L2 Cache Identifiers. Read-only. This function provides second level cache characteristics for each core.

field.name: L2Size
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0200h. L2 cache size in KB. ValidValues: 00FFh-0000h - Reserved. 0100h - 256-KB. 01FFh-0101h - Reserved. 0200h - 512-KB. 03FFh-0201h - Reserved. 0400h - 1-MB. 07FFh-0401h - Reserved. 0800h - 2-MB. FFFFh-0801h - Reserved.

field.name: L2Assoc
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,6. L2 cache associativity. ValidValues: 0h - Disabled. 1h - 1 way (direct mapped). 2h - 2 ways. 3h - Reserved. 4h - 4 ways. 5h - Reserved. 6h - 8 ways. 7h - Reserved. 8h - 16 ways. 9h - Reserved. Ah - 32 ways. Bh - 48 ways. Ch - 64 ways. Dh - 96 ways. Eh - 128 ways. Fh - Fully associative.

field.name: L2LinesPerTag
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1h. L2 cache lines per tag.

field.name: L2LineSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,64. L2 cache line size in bytes.

register.name: L3CacheId
register.desc: L3 Cache Identifiers. Read-only. This function provides third level cache characteristics shared by all cores of a processor.

field.name: L3Size
field.desc: L3 cache size. Read-only. Reset: XXXXh. The L3 cache size in 512 KB units. ValidValues: 0000h - Disabled. 3FFFh-0001h - (<Value> *0.5) MB.

field.name: L3Assoc
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,9h. There are insufficient available encodings to represent all possible L3 associativities. Please refer to Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx3[CacheNumWays]. ValidValues: 8h-0h - Reserved. 9h - Invalid, not reported here. Fh-Ah - Reserved.

field.name: L3LinesPerTag
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1h. L3 cache lines per tag.

field.name: L3LineSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,64. L3 cache line size in bytes.

register.name: ProcFeedbackCap
register.desc: Reserved. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0000h.

register.name: RasCap
register.desc: RAS Capabilities. Read-only.

field.name: ScalableMca
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 0=Scalable MCA is not supported. 1=Scalable MCA is supported. See 3.1.1.2 [Machine Check Architecture Extensions] and MCA_CONFIG[McaX] for the respective bank.

field.name: HWA
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. Hardware assert supported.

field.name: SUCCOR
field.desc: Software uncorrectable error containment and recovery capability. Read-only. Reset: X. The processor supports software containment of uncorrectable errors through context synchronizing data poisoning and deferred error interrupts; MSR Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg, MCA_STATUS[Deferred] and MCA_STATUS[Poison] exist.

field.name: McaOverflowRecov
field.desc: MCA overflow recovery support. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 0= MCA overflow conditions require software to shutdown the system. 1=MCA overflow conditions (MCi_STATUS[Overflow] == 1) are not fatal; software may safely ignore such conditions. See 3.1 [Machine Check Architecture].

register.name: ApmInfoEcx
register.desc: Advanced Power Management Information. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0000h.

field.name: CpuPwrSampleTimeRatio
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0000h. Specifies the ratio of the compute unit power accumulator sample period to the TSC counter period.

register.name: ApmInfoEdx
register.desc: Advanced Power Management Information. Read-only. This function provides advanced power management feature identifiers.

field.name: RAPL
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Running average power limit.

field.name: ConnectedStandby
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Connected Standby.

field.name: ProcPowerReporting
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. Core power reporting interface supported.

field.name: ProcFeedbackInterface
field.desc: Processor feedback interface. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. 1=Indicates support for processor feedback interface; Core::X86::Cpuid::ProcFeedbackCap.

field.name: EffFreqRO
field.desc: Read-only effective frequency interface. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Indicates presence of Core::X86::Msr::MPerfReadOnly and Core::X86::Msr::APerfReadOnly.

field.name: CPB
field.desc: Core performance boost. Read-only. Reset: X. 1=Indicates presence of Core::X86::Msr::HWCR[CpbDis] and support for core performance boost.

field.name: TscInvariant
field.desc: TSC invariant. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. The TSC rate is invariant.

field.name: HwPstate
field.desc: Hardware P-state control. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Core::X86::Msr::PStateCurLim, Core::X86::Msr::PStateCtl and Core::X86::Msr::PStateStat exist.

field.name: OneHundredMHzSteps
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. 100 MHz multiplier Control.

field.name: TM
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Hardware thermal control (HTC).

field.name: TTP
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. THERMTRIP.

field.name: TS
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Temperature sensor.

register.name: LongModeInfo
register.desc: Long Mode Address Size Identifiers. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_3030h. This provides information about the maximum physical and linear address width supported by the processor.

field.name: GuestPhysAddrSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,00h. Maximum guest physical byte address size in bits. ValidValues: 00h - The maximum guest physical address size defined by PhysAddrSize. FFh-01h - The maximum guest physical address size defined by GuestPhysAddrSize.

field.name: LinAddrSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,30h. Maximum linear byte address size in bits.

field.name: PhysAddrSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,30h. Maximum physical byte address size in bits.

register.name: FeatureExtIdEbx
register.desc: Extended Feature Extensions ID EBX. Read-only.

field.name: BranchSample
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=Branch sampling feature supported. See Core::X86::Msr::SAMP_BR_FROM, Core::X86::Msr::SAMP_BR_TO and Core::X86::Msr::DbgExtnCfg.

field.name: PSFD
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Predictive Store Forward Disable. See Core::X86::Msr::SPEC_CTRL[PSFD].

field.name: CPPC
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0. Collaborative Processor Performance Control.

field.name: SSBD
field.desc: Speculative Store Bypass Disable. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1.

field.name: PPIN
field.desc: PPIN support. Read-only. Reset: X. 0=PPIN capability is not supported; Core::X86::Msr::PPIN_CTL and Core::X86::Msr::PPIN are treated as RAZ. 1=Indicates that Protected Processor Inventory Number (PPIN) capability can be enabled for privileged system inventory agent to read PPIN from Core::X86::Msr::PPIN. Protected Processor Inventory Number support.

field.name: TlbFlushNested
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. Flushing includes all nested translations for guest. Allows setting RAX[5] for INVLPGB.

field.name: EferLmsleUnsupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=Core::X86::Msr::EFER[LMSLE] is not supported, and MBZ.

field.name: IbrsProvidesSameModeProtection
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. IBRS provides Same Mode Protection.

field.name: IbrsPreferred
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=IBRS is preferred over software solution.

field.name: StibpAlwaysOn
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. Single Thread Indirect Branch Prediction Mode has Enhanced Performance and May be left Always On.

field.name: STIBP
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. Single Thread Indirect Branch Prediction.

field.name: IBRS
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. Indirect Branch Restricted Speculation.

field.name: INT_WBINVD
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. Interruptible WBINVD,WBNOINVD.

field.name: IBPB
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. Indirect Branch Prediction Barrier.

field.name: WBNOINVD
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. WBNOINVD writes all modified cache lines in the internal caches of the processor back to memory leaving the line valid (clean) in the internal caches.

field.name: MCOMMIT
field.desc: Memory commit. Read-only. Reset: 1. Memory commit instruction support. See Core::X86::Msr::EFER[MCOMMIT].

field.name: MBE
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Memory Bandwidth Enforcement.

field.name: RDPRU
field.desc: Read processor register at user level. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. RDPRU instruction allows reading MPERF and APERF at user level.

field.name: INVLPGB
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. INVLPGB instruction broadcasts a TLB invalidate to all threads in the system.

field.name: RstrFpErrPtrs
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=FXSAVE, XSAVE, FXSAVEOPT, XSAVEC, XSAVES always save error pointers and FXRSTOR, XRSTOR, XRSTORS always restore error pointers is supported.

field.name: InstRetCntMsr
field.desc: Instructions retired count support. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=Core::X86::Msr::IRPerfCount supported.

field.name: CLZERO
field.desc: Clear Zero Instruction. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. CLZERO instruction zero's out the 64-byte cache line specified in RAX. Note: CLZERO instruction operations are cache-line aligned and RAX[5:0] is ignored.

register.name: SizeId
register.desc: Size Identifiers. Read-only. This provides information about the number of threads supported by the processor.

field.name: PerfTscSize
field.desc: Performance time-stamp counter size. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0h.

field.name: ApicIdSize
field.desc: APIC ID size. Read-only. Reset: Xh. The number of bits in the initial Core::X86::Apic::ApicId[ApicId] value that indicate thread ID within a package.

field.name: NC
field.desc: Number of threads - 1. Read-only. Reset: XXh. The number of threads in the package is NC + 1 (e.g., if NC == 0, then there is one thread).

register.name: FeatureExtSizeEdx
register.desc: Feature Extended Size Edx. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0001_0007h.

field.name: RdpruMax
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,01h. RDPRU Instruction max input supported.

field.name: InvlpgbCountMax
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0007h. Maximum count for INVLPGB instruction.

register.name: SvmRevFeatIdEax
register.desc: SVM Revision and Feature Identification. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0001h. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[SVM].

field.name: SvmRev
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,01h. SVM revision.

register.name: SvmRevFeatIdEbx
register.desc: SVM Revision and Feature Identification. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_8000h. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[SVM]. This provides SVM revision and feature information.

field.name: NASID
field.desc: Number of address space identifiers (ASID). Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_8000h.

register.name: SvmRevFeatIdEdx
register.desc: SVM Revision and Feature Identification. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,009B_94FFh. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[SVM]. This provides SVM feature information.

field.name: HOST_MCE_OVERRIDE
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=If hCR4:MCE == 1 and gCR4:MCE == 0, machine check exceptions (#MC) in guest do not cause shutdown and are always intercepted.

field.name: GuestSpecCtrl
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=Indicates support for Guest SPEC_CTRL.

field.name: SupervisorShadowStack
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Supervisor Shadow Stack.

field.name: GMET
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Guest Mode Execute Trap.

field.name: vGIF
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Virtualized GIF.

field.name: V_VMSAVE_VMLOAD
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Virtualized VMLOAD and VMSAVE.

field.name: AVIC
field.desc: AMD virtual interrupt controller. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. 1=Support indicated for SVM mode virtualized interrupt controller; Indicates support for Core::X86::Msr::AvicDoorbell.

field.name: PauseFilterThreshold
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. PAUSE filter threshold.

field.name: PauseFilter
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Pause intercept filter.

field.name: DecodeAssists
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Decode assists.

field.name: FlushByAsid
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Flush by ASID.

field.name: VmcbClean
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. VMCB clean bits.

field.name: TscRateMsr
field.desc: MSR based TSC rate control. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=Indicates support for TSC ratio Core::X86::Msr::TscRateMsr.

field.name: NRIPS
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. NRIP Save.

field.name: SVML
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. SVM lock.

field.name: LbrVirt
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. LBR virtualization.

field.name: NP
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Nested Paging.

register.name: L1Tlb1G
register.desc: L1 TLB 1G Identifiers. Read-only. This function provides first level TLB characteristics for 1-GB pages.

field.name: L1DTlb1GAssoc
field.desc: L1 data TLB associativity for 1-GB pages. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,Fh. See Core::X86::Cpuid::L2CacheId[L2Assoc].

field.name: L1DTlb1GSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,64. L1 data TLB number of entries for 1-GB pages.

field.name: L1ITlb1GAssoc
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,Fh. L1 instruction TLB associativity for 1-GB pages. See Core::X86::Cpuid::L2CacheId[L2Assoc].

field.name: L1ITlb1GSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,64. L1 instruction TLB number of entries for 1-GB pages.

register.name: L2Tlb1G
register.desc: L2 TLB 1G Identifiers. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,F040_0000h.

field.name: L2DTlb1GAssoc
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,Fh. L2 data TLB associativity for 1-GB pages.

field.name: L2DTlb1GSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,040h. L2 data TLB number of entries for 1-GB pages.

field.name: L2ITlb1GAssoc
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0h. L2 instruction TLB associativity for 1-GB pages.

field.name: L2ITlb1GSize
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,000h. L2 instruction TLB number of entries for 1-GB pages.

register.name: PerfOptId
register.desc: Performance Optimization Identifiers. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0006h. This function returns performance related information.

field.name: FP256
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 256-bit AVX instructions are executed with full-width internal operations and pipelines rather than decomposing them into internal 128-bit suboperations.

field.name: MOVU
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. MOVU SSE instructions are more efficient and should be preferred to SSE MOVL/MOVH. MOVUPS is more efficient than MOVLPS/MOVHPS. MOVUPD is more efficient than MOVLPD/MOVHPD.

field.name: FP128
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. 128-bit SSE (multimedia) instructions are executed with full-width internal operations and pipelines rather than decomposing them into internal 64-bit suboperations.

register.name: IbsIdEax
register.desc: Instruction Based Sampling Identifiers. Read-only. This function returns IBS feature information.

field.name: IbsOpData4
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. IBS op data 4 MSR supported.

field.name: IbsFetchCtlExtd
field.desc: IBS fetch control extended MSR supported. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Indicates support for Core::X86::Msr::IC_IBS_EXTD_CTL.

field.name: OpBrnFuse
field.desc: Fused branch op indication supported. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Indicates support for Core::X86::Msr::IBS_OP_DATA[IbsOpBrnFuse].

field.name: RipInvalidChk
field.desc: Invalid RIP indication supported. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Indicates support for Core::X86::Msr::IBS_OP_DATA[IbsRipInvalid].

field.name: OpCntExt
field.desc: IbsOpCurCnt and IbsOpMaxCnt extend by 7 bits. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Indicates support for Core::X86::Msr::IBS_OP_CTL[IbsOpCurCnt[26:20],IbsOpMaxCnt[26:20]].

field.name: BrnTrgt
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Branch target address reporting supported.

field.name: OpCnt
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Op counting mode supported.

field.name: RdWrOpCnt
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Read/Write of op counter supported.

field.name: OpSam
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. IBS execution sampling supported.

field.name: FetchSam
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: X. IBS fetch sampling supported.

field.name: IBSFFV
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. IBS feature flags valid.

register.name: CachePropEax0
register.desc: Cache Properties (DC). Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0 reports topology information for the DC.

field.name: NumSharingCache
field.desc: Number of logical processors sharing cache. Read-only. Reset: XXXh. The number of logical processors sharing this cache is NumSharingCache + 1.

field.name: FullyAssociative
field.desc: Fully associative cache. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. 1=Cache is fully associative.

field.name: SelfInitialization
field.desc: Cache is self-initializing. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=Cache is self initializing; cache does not need software initialization.

field.name: CacheLevel
field.desc: Cache level. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1h. Identifies the cache level. ValidValues: 0h - Reserved. 1h - Level 1. 2h - Level 2. 3h - Level 3. 7h-4h - Reserved.

field.name: CacheType
field.desc: Cache type. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,01h. Identifies the type of cache. ValidValues: 00h - Null; no more caches. 01h - Data cache. 02h - Instruction cache. 03h - Unified cache. 1Fh-04h - Reserved.

register.name: CachePropEax1
register.desc: Cache Properties (IC). Read-only. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax1 reports topology information for the IC. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: NumSharingCache
field.desc: Number of logical processors sharing cache. Read-only. Reset: XXXh. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[NumSharingCache].

field.name: FullyAssociative
field.desc: Fully associative cache. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[FullyAssociative].

field.name: SelfInitialization
field.desc: Cache is self-initializing. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[SelfInitialization].

field.name: CacheLevel
field.desc: Cache level. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1h. Identifies the cache level. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[CacheLevel].

field.name: CacheType
field.desc: Cache type. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,02h. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[CacheType].

register.name: CachePropEax2
register.desc: Cache Properties (L2). Read-only. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax2 reports topology information for the L2. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: NumSharingCache
field.desc: Number of logical processors sharing cache. Read-only. Reset: XXXh. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[NumSharingCache].

field.name: FullyAssociative
field.desc: Fully associative cache. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[FullyAssociative].

field.name: SelfInitialization
field.desc: Cache is self-initializing. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[SelfInitialization].

field.name: CacheLevel
field.desc: Cache level. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,2h. Identifies the cache level. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[CacheLevel].

field.name: CacheType
field.desc: Cache type. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,03h. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[CacheType].

register.name: CachePropEax3
register.desc: Cache Properties (L3). Read-only. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax3 reports topology information for the L3.

field.name: NumSharingCache
field.desc: Number of logical processors sharing cache. Read-only. Reset: XXXh. The number of logical processors sharing this cache is NumSharingCache + 1.

field.name: FullyAssociative
field.desc: Fully associative cache. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[FullyAssociative].

field.name: SelfInitialization
field.desc: Cache is self-initializing. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[SelfInitialization].

field.name: CacheLevel
field.desc: Cache level. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,3h. Identifies the cache level. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[CacheLevel].

field.name: CacheType
field.desc: Cache type. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,03h. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[CacheType].

register.name: CachePropEax4
register.desc: Cache Properties Null. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0000h. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax4 reports done/null. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: CacheType
field.desc: Cache type. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,00h. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0[CacheType].

register.name: CachePropEbx0
register.desc: Cache Properties (DC). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,01C0_003Fh. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx0 reports topology information for the DC. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: CacheNumWays
field.desc: Cache number of ways. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,007h. Cache number of ways is CacheNumWays + 1.

field.name: CachePhysPartitions
field.desc: Cache physical line partitions. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,000h. Cache partitions is CachePhysPartitions + 1.

field.name: CacheLineSize
field.desc: Cache line size in bytes. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,03Fh. Cache line size in bytes is CacheLineSize + 1.

register.name: CachePropEbx1
register.desc: Cache Properties (IC). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,01C0_003Fh. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx1 reports topology information for the IC. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: CacheNumWays
field.desc: Cache number of ways. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,007h. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx0[CacheNumWays].

field.name: CachePhysPartitions
field.desc: Cache physical line partitions. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,000h. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx0[CachePhysPartitions].

field.name: CacheLineSize
field.desc: Cache line size in bytes. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,03Fh. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx0[CacheLineSize].

register.name: CachePropEbx2
register.desc: Cache Properties (L2). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,01C0_003Fh. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx2 reports topology information for the L2. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: CacheNumWays
field.desc: Cache number of ways. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,007h. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx0[CacheNumWays].

field.name: CachePhysPartitions
field.desc: Cache physical line partitions. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,000h. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx0[CachePhysPartitions].

field.name: CacheLineSize
field.desc: Cache line size in bytes. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,03Fh. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx0[CacheLineSize].

register.name: CachePropEbx3
register.desc: Cache Properties (L3). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,03C0_003Fh. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx3 reports topology information for the L3. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: CacheNumWays
field.desc: Cache number of ways. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,00Fh. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx0[CacheNumWays].

field.name: CachePhysPartitions
field.desc: Cache physical line partitions. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,000h. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx0[CachePhysPartitions].

field.name: CacheLineSize
field.desc: Cache line size in bytes. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,03Fh. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEbx0[CacheLineSize].

register.name: CachePropEbx4
register.desc: Cache Properties Null. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0000h. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax4 reports done/null. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

register.name: CachePropEcx0
register.desc: Cache Properties (DC). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_003Fh. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEcx0 reports topology information for the DC. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: CacheNumSets
field.desc: Cache number of sets. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_003Fh. Cache number of sets is CacheNumSets + 1.

register.name: CachePropEcx1
register.desc: Cache Properties (IC). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_003Fh. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEcx1 reports topology information for the IC. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: CacheNumSets
field.desc: Cache number of sets. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_003Fh. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEcx0[CacheNumSets].

register.name: CachePropEcx2
register.desc: Cache Properties (L2). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_03FFh. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEcx2 reports topology information for the L2. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: CacheNumSets
field.desc: Cache number of sets. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_03FFh. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEcx0[CacheNumSets].

register.name: CachePropEcx3
register.desc: Cache Properties (L3). Read-only. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEcx3 reports topology information for the L3.

field.name: CacheNumSets
field.desc: Cache number of sets. Read-only. Reset: 0000_XXXXh. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEcx0[CacheNumSets]. ValidValues: 0000_3FFEh-0000_0000h - Reserved. 0000_3FFFh - 16384 L3 Cache Sets. 0000_7FFEh-0000_4000h - Reserved. 0000_7FFFh - 32768 L3 Cache Sets. FFFF_FFFFh-0000_8000h - Reserved.

register.name: CachePropEcx4
register.desc: Cache Properties Null. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0000h. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax3 reports done/null. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: CacheNumSets
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0000h. Cache number of sets.

register.name: CachePropEdx0
register.desc: Cache Properties (DC). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0000h. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEdx0 reports topology information for the DC. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: CacheInclusive
field.desc: Cache inclusive. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. 0=Cache is not inclusive of lower cache levels. 1=Cache is inclusive of lower cache levels.

field.name: WBINVD
field.desc: Write-Back Invalidate/Invalidate. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. 0=WBINVD/INVD invalidates all lower level caches of non-originating cores sharing this cache. 1=WBINVD/INVD not ensured to invalidate all lower level caches of non-originating cores sharing this cache.

register.name: CachePropEdx1
register.desc: Cache Properties (IC). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0000h. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEdx1 reports topology information for the IC. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: CacheInclusive
field.desc: Cache inclusive. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEdx0[CacheInclusive].

field.name: WBINVD
field.desc: Write-Back Invalidate/Invalidate. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. 0=WBINVD/INVD invalidates all lower level caches of non-originating cores sharing this cache. 1=WBINVD/INVD may not invalidate all lower level caches of non-originating cores sharing this cache. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEdx0[WBINVD].

register.name: CachePropEdx2
register.desc: Cache Properties (L2). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0002h. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEdx2 reports topology information for the L2. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: CacheInclusive
field.desc: Cache inclusive. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEdx0[CacheInclusive].

field.name: WBINVD
field.desc: Write-Back Invalidate/Invalidate. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. 0=WBINVD/INVD invalidates all lower level caches of non-originating cores sharing this cache. 1=WBINVD/INVD may not invalidate all lower level caches of non-originating cores sharing this cache.

register.name: CachePropEdx3
register.desc: Cache Properties (L3). Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0001h. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEdx3 reports reports topology information for the L3. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

field.name: CacheInclusive
field.desc: Cache inclusive. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEdx0[CacheInclusive].

field.name: WBINVD
field.desc: Write-Back Invalidate/Invalidate. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 0=WBINVD/INVD invalidates all lower level caches of non-originating cores sharing this cache. 1=WBINVD/INVD may not invalidate all lower level caches of non-originating cores sharing this cache.

register.name: CachePropEdx4
register.desc: Cache Properties Null. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0000_0000h. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax3 reports done/null. See Core::X86::Cpuid::CachePropEax0.

register.name: ExtApicId
register.desc: Extended APIC ID. Read-only. Enable: (Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions] && Core::X86::Msr::APIC_BAR[ApicEn]). If Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions] == 0 then CPUID Fn8000001E_E[D,C,B,A]X are Reserved. If (Core::X86::Msr::APIC_BAR[ApicEn] == 0) then Core::X86::Cpuid::ExtApicId[ExtendedApicId] is Reserved.

field.name: ExtendedApicId
field.desc: Extended APIC ID. Read-only. See 2.1.14.2.1.3 [ApicId Enumeration Requirements]. Reset: (Core::X86::Msr::APIC_BAR[ApicEn] && Core::X86::Msr::APIC_BAR[x2ApicEn]) ? Core::X86::Msr::APIC_ID[ApicId[31:0]] : Core::X86::Msr::APIC_BAR[ApicEn] ? {00_0000h , Core::X86::Apic::ApicId[ApicId]} : 0000_0000h.

register.name: CoreId
register.desc: Core Identifiers. Read-only. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::ExtApicId.

field.name: ThreadsPerCore
field.desc: Threads per core. Read-only. Reset: XXh. The number of threads per core is ThreadsPerCore + 1.

field.name: CoreId
field.desc: Core ID. Read-only. Reset: XXh. Identifies the logical core unit ID.

register.name: NodeId
register.desc: Node Identifiers. Read-only. Enable: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[TopologyExtensions].

field.name: NodesPerProcessor
field.desc: Node per processor. Read-only. Reset: XXXb. ValidValues: 0h - 1 node per processor. 7h-1h - Reserved.

field.name: NodeId
field.desc: Node ID. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,XXh.

register.name: SecureEncryptionEax
register.desc: AMD Secure Encryption EAX. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,0101_FC3Fh.

field.name: VMSARegProt
field.desc: VMSA Register Protection. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Support for VMSA register protection.

field.name: VTE
field.desc: Virtual Transparent Encryption for SEV. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. The Virtual Transparent Encryption feature can be enabled to force all memory accesses within an SEV guest to be encrypted with the guest's key. When enabled the hardware pretends that the C-bits for all guest mode accesses are 1 regardless of the actual guest page tables.

field.name: PreventHostIBS
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Prevent host IBS for a SEV-ES guest.

field.name: DebugStateSwap
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=DR0-3 and DR0-3_MASK can be saved/restored on world switches.

field.name: AlternateInjection
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=SEV-ES guests can use an encrypted vmcb field for event injection.

field.name: RestrictInjection
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=SEV-ES guests can refuse all event-injections except #HV.

field.name: Req64BitHypervisor
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Require 64-bit Hypervisor.

field.name: CoherencyEnforced
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Hardware enforces cache coherency.

field.name: VMPL
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Multiple SNP guests can share memory using differing permissions.

field.name: SNP
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. RMP table can be enabled to protect memory even from hypervisor.

field.name: SevEs
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Secure Encrypted ES.

field.name: VmPgFlush
field.desc: VM Page Flush MSR is supported. Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. See Core::X86::Msr::VMPAGE_FLUSH.

field.name: SEV
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Secure Encrypted Virtualization supported.

field.name: SME
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. Secure Memory Encryption supported.

register.name: SecureEncryptionEbx
register.desc: AMD Secure Encryption EBX. Read-only.

field.name: VmplSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,4. Number of VMPLs supported.

field.name: MemEncryptPhysAddWidth
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 000XXXb. Reduction of physical address space in bits when memory encryption is enabled (0 indicates no reduction). ValidValues: 00h - Physical Address width is not reduced. 01h - Physical Address width is reduced by one. 02h - Physical Address width is reduced by two. 03h - Physical Address width is reduced by three. 04h - Physical Address width is reduced by four. 05h - Physical Address width is reduced by five. 3Fh-06h - Reserved.

field.name: CBit
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 33h. Page table bit number used to enable memory encryption.

register.name: SecureEncryptionEcx
register.desc: AMD Secure Encryption ECX. Read-only.

field.name: NumEncryptedGuests
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: XXXX_XXXXh. Indicates the maximum ASID value that may be used for an SEV-enabled guest.

register.name: SecureEncryptionEdx
register.desc: Minimum ASID. Read-only.

field.name: MinimumSEVASID
field.desc: Minimum SEV enabled, SEV-ES disabled ASID. Read-only. Reset: 0000_000Xh. Indicates the minimum ASID value that must be used for an SEV-enabled, SEV-ES-disabled guest.

register.name: PqeBandwidthEax0
register.desc: Platform QoS Enforcement for Memory Bandwidth. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: PqeBandwidthEbx0
register.desc: Platform QoS Enforcement for Memory Bandwidth. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0002h.

field.name: MBE
field.desc: Memory bandwidth enforcement. Read-only. Reset: 1. Memory bandwidth enforcement.

register.name: PqeBandwidthEcx0
register.desc: Platform QoS Enforcement for Memory Bandwidth. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: PqeBandwidthEdx0
register.desc: Platform QoS Enforcement for Memory Bandwidth. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: PqeBandwidthEax1
register.desc: Platform QoS Enforcement for Memory Bandwidth. Read-only. Reset: 0000_000Bh.

field.name: BW_LEN
field.desc: QOS Memory Bandwidth Enforcement Limit Size. Read-only. Reset: 0000_000Bh. Size of the QOS Memory Bandwidth Enforcement Limit.

register.name: PqeBandwidthEbx1
register.desc: Platform QoS Enforcement for Memory Bandwidth. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: PqeBandwidthEcx1
register.desc: Platform QoS Enforcement for Memory Bandwidth. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: PqeBandwidthEdx1
register.desc: Platform QoS Enforcement for Memory Bandwidth. Read-only. Reset: 0000_000Fh.

field.name: NumClassService
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0000_000Fh. Number of classes of service.

register.name: FeatureExt2Eax
register.desc: Extended Feature 2 EAX. Read-only.

field.name: PrefetchCtlMsr
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=Prefetch control MSR supported. See Core::X86::Msr::PrefetchControl.

field.name: NullSelectorClearsBase
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=Null Selector Clears Base.

field.name: SmmPgCfgLock
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=SMM paging configuration lock supported.

field.name: LFenceAlwaysSerializing
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. LFENCE is always serializing.

field.name: NoNestedDataBp
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Fixed,1. New data-breakpoints are ignored while switching to data-breakpoint handler.

register.name: TSC
register.desc: Time Stamp Counter. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. The TSC uses a common reference for all sockets, cores and threads.

field.name: TSC
field.desc: Time stamp counter. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. The TSC increments at the P0 frequency. The TSC counts at the same rate in all P-states, all C states, S0, or S1. A read of this MSR in guest mode is affected by Core::X86::Msr::TscRateMsr. The value (TSC/TSCRatio) is the TSC P0 frequency based value (as if TSCRatio == 1.0) when (TSCRatio != 1.0).

register.name: APIC_BAR
register.desc: APIC Base Address.

field.name: ApicBar[47:12]
field.desc: APIC base address register. Read-write. Reset: 0_000F_EE00h. Specifies the base address, physical address [47:12], for the APICXX register set in xAPIC mode. See 2.1.14.2.1.2 [APIC Register Space].

field.name: ApicEn
field.desc: APIC enable. Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=Disable Local APIC. 1=Local APIC is enabled in xAPIC mode. See 2.1.14.2.1.2 [APIC Register Space].

field.name: x2ApicEn
field.desc: Extended APIC enable. Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=Disable Extended Local APIC. 1=Extended Local APIC is enabled in x2APIC mode.

field.name: BSC
field.desc: Boot strap core. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: X. 0=The core is not the boot core of the BSP. 1=The core is the boot core of the BSP.

register.name: EBL_CR_POWERON
register.desc: Cluster ID. Writes to this register result in a GP fault with error code 0.

field.name: ClusterID
field.desc: Read,Error-on-write. Reset: 0h. The field does not affect hardware.

register.name: SPEC_CTRL
register.desc: Speculative Control.

field.name: PSFD
field.desc: Predictive Store Forwarding Disable. Read-write. Reset: 0. 1=Disable predictive store forwarding.

field.name: SSBD
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Speculative Store Bypass Disable.

field.name: STIBP
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Single thread indirect branch predictor.

field.name: IBRS
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Indirect branch restriction speculation.

register.name: PRED_CMD
register.desc: Prediction Command.

field.name: IBPB
field.desc: Indirect branch prediction barrier. Write-only,Error-on-read. Reset: 0. Supported if Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEbx[IBPB] == 1.

register.name: PATCH_LEVEL
register.desc: Patch Level. Read,Error-on-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: PatchLevel
field.desc: Read,Error-on-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h. This returns an identification number for the microcode patch that has been loaded. If no patch has been loaded, this returns 0.

register.name: MPERF
register.desc: Max Performance Frequency Clock Count. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: MPERF
field.desc: Maximum core clocks counter. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Incremented by hardware at the P0 frequency while the core is in C0. This register does not increment when the core is in the stop-grant state. In combination with Core::X86::Msr::APERF, this is used to determine the effective frequency of the core. A read of this MSR in guest mode is affected by Core::X86::Msr::TscRateMsr. This field uses software P-state numbering. See Core::X86::Msr::HWCR[EffFreqCntMwait], 2.1.6 [Effective Frequency].

register.name: APERF
register.desc: Actual Performance Frequency Clock Count. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: APERF
field.desc: Actual core clocks counter. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. This register increments in proportion to the actual number of core clocks cycles while the core is in C0. The register does not increment when the core is in the stop-grant state. See Core::X86::Msr::MPERF.

register.name: MTRRcap
register.desc: MTRR Capabilities. Read,Error-on-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0508h.

field.name: MtrrCapWc
field.desc: Write-combining memory type. Read,Error-on-write. Reset: 1. 1=The write combining memory type is supported.

field.name: MtrrCapFix
field.desc: Fixed range register. Read,Error-on-write. Reset: 1. 1=Fixed MTRRs are supported.

field.name: MtrrCapVCnt
field.desc: Variable range registers count. Read,Error-on-write. Reset: 08h. Specifies the number of variable MTRRs supported.

register.name: SYSENTER_CS
register.desc: SYSENTER CS. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: SysEnterCS
field.desc: SYSENTER target CS. Read-write. Reset: 0000h. Holds the called procedure code segment.

register.name: SYSENTER_ESP
register.desc: SYSENTER ESP. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: SysEnterESP
field.desc: SYSENTER target SP. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. Holds the called procedure stack pointer.

register.name: SYSENTER_EIP
register.desc: SYSENTER EIP. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: SysEnterEIP
field.desc: SYSENTER target IP. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. Holds the called procedure instruction pointer.

register.name: MCG_CAP
register.desc: Global Machine Check Capabilities.

field.name: McgCtlP
field.desc: MCG_CTL register present. Read-only,Error-on-write. Reset: Fixed,1. 1=The machine check control registers (MCi_CTL) are present. See 3.1 [Machine Check Architecture].

field.name: Count
field.desc: Read-only,Error-on-write,Volatile. Reset: XXh. Indicates the number of error reporting banks visible to the core. This value may differ from core to core.

register.name: MCG_STAT
register.desc: Global Machine Check Status. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. See 3.1 [Machine Check Architecture].

field.name: MCIP
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=A machine check is in progress. Machine check in progress.

field.name: EIPV
field.desc: Error instruction pointer valid. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The instruction pointer that was pushed onto the stack by the machine check mechanism references the instruction that caused the machine check error.

field.name: RIPV
field.desc: Restart instruction pointer valid. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 0=The interrupt was not precise and/or the process (task) context may be corrupt; continued operation of this process may not be possible without intervention, however system processing or other processes may be able to continue with appropriate software clean up. 1=Program execution can be reliably restarted at the EIP address on the stack.

register.name: MCG_CTL
register.desc: Global Machine Check Exception Reporting Control. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. This register controls enablement of the individual error reporting banks; see 3.1 [Machine Check Architecture] and 3.1.2.1 [Global Registers]. When a machine check register bank is not enabled in MCG_CTL, errors for that bank are not logged or reported, and actions enabled through the MCA are not taken; each MCi_CTL register identifies which errors are still corrected when MCG_CTL[i] is disabled.

field.name: MCnEn
field.desc: Configurable. Reset: 000_0000_0000_0000h. Description: 1=The MC machine check register bank is enabled. Width of this field is SOC implementation and configuration specific. See 3.1.2.1 [Global Registers].

field.name: MCnEnCore
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. 1=The MC machine check register bank is enabled. ValidValues: Bit [0] - EnableMCAfor LSDC. [1] - Enable MCA for ICBP. [2] - Enable MCA for L2. [3] - Enable MCA for DE. [4] - Reserved. [5] - Enable MCA for SCEX. [6] - Enable MCA for FP.

register.name: DBG_CTL_MSR
register.desc: Debug Control. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: PB
field.desc: Performance monitor pin control. Read-write. Reset: 0h. This field does not control any hardware.

field.name: BTF
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. 1=Enable branch single step.

field.name: LBR
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. 1=Enable last branch record.

register.name: BR_FROM
register.desc: Last Branch From IP. Read,Error-on-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: LastBranchFromIP
field.desc: Read,Error-on-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Loaded with the segment offset of the branch instruction.

register.name: BR_TO
register.desc: Last Branch To IP. Read,Error-on-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: LastBranchToIP
field.desc: Read,Error-on-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Holds the target RIP of the last branch that occurred before an exception or interrupt.

register.name: LastExcpFromIp
register.desc: Last Exception From IP. Read,Error-on-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: LastIntFromIP
field.desc: Read,Error-on-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Holds the source RIP of the last branch that occurred before the exception or interrupt.

register.name: LastExcpToIp
register.desc: Last Exception To IP. Read,Error-on-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: LastIntToIP
field.desc: Read,Error-on-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Holds the target RIP of the last branch that occurred before the exception or interrupt.

register.name: MtrrVarBase
register.desc: Variable-Size MTRRs Base. Each MTRR (Core::X86::Msr::MtrrVarBase, Core::X86::Msr::MtrrFix_64K through Core::X86::Msr::MtrrFix_4K_7, or Core::X86::Msr::MTRRdefType) specifies a physical address range and a corresponding memory type (MemType) associated with that range. Setting the memory type to an unsupported value results in a #GP. The variable-size MTRRs come in pairs of base and mask registers (MSR0000_0200 and MSR0000_0201 are the first pair, etc.). Variables MTRRs are enabled through Core::X86::Msr::MTRRdefType[MtrrDefTypeEn]. A core access--with address CPUAddr--is determined to be within the address range of a variable-size MTRR if the following equation is true: CPUAddr[47:12] & PhyMask[47:12] == PhyBase[47:12] & PhyMask[47:12]. For example, if the variable MTRR spans 256 KB and starts at the 1-MB address the PhyBase would be set to 0_0010_0000h and the PhyMask to F_FFFC_0000h (with zeros filling in for bits[11:0]). This results in a range from 0_0010_0000h to 0_0013_FFFFh.

field.name: PhyBase
field.desc: Base address. Read-write. Reset: X_XXXX_XXXXh. Physical base address.

field.name: MemType
field.desc: Memory type. Read-write. Reset: XXXb. Address range from 00000h to 0FFFFh. ValidValues: 0h - UC or uncacheable. 1h - WC or write combining. 3h-2h - Reserved. 4h - WT or write through. 5h - WP or write protect. 6h - WB or write back. 7h - Reserved.

register.name: MtrrVarMask
register.desc: Variable-Size MTRRs Mask.

field.name: PhyMask
field.desc: Address mask. Read-write. Reset: X_XXXX_XXXXh. Physical address mask.

field.name: Valid
field.desc: Valid. Read-write. Reset: X. 1=The variable-size MTRR pair is enabled.

register.name: MtrrFix_64K
register.desc: Fixed-Size MTRRs. See Core::X86::Msr::MtrrVarBase for general MTRR information. Fixed MTRRs are enabled through Core::X86::Msr::MTRRdefType[MtrrDefTypeFixEn,MtrrDefTypeEn]. For addresses below 1-MB, the appropriate Fixed MTRRs override the default access destination. Each fixed MTRR includes two bits, RdDram and WrDram, that determine the destination based on the access type. Writing Reserved MemType values causes an error-on-write.

register.name: MtrrFix_4K_0
register.desc: Fixed-Size MTRRs. See Core::X86::Msr::MtrrVarBase for general MTRR information. Fixed MTRRs are enabled through Core::X86::Msr::MTRRdefType[MtrrDefTypeFixEn,MtrrDefTypeEn]. For addresses below 1-MB, the appropriate Fixed MTRRs override the default access destination. Each fixed MTRR includes two bits, RdDram and WrDram, that determine the destination based on the access type. Writing Reserved MemType values causes an error-on-write.

register.name: MtrrFix_4K_3
register.desc: Fixed-Size MTRRs. See Core::X86::Msr::MtrrVarBase for general MTRR information. Fixed MTRRs are enabled through Core::X86::Msr::MTRRdefType[MtrrDefTypeFixEn,MtrrDefTypeEn]. For addresses below 1-MB, the appropriate Fixed MTRRs override the default access destination. Each fixed MTRR includes two bits, RdDram and WrDram, that determine the destination based on the access type. Writing Reserved MemType values causes an error-on-write.

register.name: MtrrFix_4K_6
register.desc: Fixed-Size MTRRs. See Core::X86::Msr::MtrrVarBase for general MTRR information. Fixed MTRRs are enabled through Core::X86::Msr::MTRRdefType[MtrrDefTypeFixEn,MtrrDefTypeEn]. For addresses below 1-MB, the appropriate Fixed MTRRs override the default access destination. Each fixed MTRR includes two bits, RdDram and WrDram, that determine the destination based on the access type. Writing Reserved MemType values causes an error-on-write.

field.desc: Read DRAM. 0=Read accesses to the range are marked as MMIO. 1=Read accesses to the range are marked as destined for DRAM. Address range from F8000h to F8FFFh. Core::X86::Msr::SYS_CFG[MtrrFixDramEn,MtrrFixDramModEn] masks reads of the stored value. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::SYS_CFG[MtrrFixDramModEn] ? Read-write : Read,Error-on-write-1. Reset: Core::X86::Msr::SYS_CFG[MtrrFixDramModEn] ? X : Fixed,0.

field.name: WrDram_4K_F8000
field.desc: Write DRAM. 0=Write accesses to the range are marked as MMIO. 1=Write accesses to the range are marked as destined for DRAM. Address range from F8000h to F8FFFh. Core::X86::Msr::SYS_CFG[MtrrFixDramEn,MtrrFixDramModEn] masks reads of the stored value. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::SYS_CFG[MtrrFixDramModEn] ? Read-write : Read,Error-on-write-1. Reset: Core::X86::Msr::SYS_CFG[MtrrFixDramModEn] ? X : Fixed,0.

field.name: MemType_4K_F8000
field.desc: Memory type. Read-write. Reset: XXXb. Address range from F8000h to F8FFFh. ValidValues: 0h - UC or uncacheable. 1h - WC or write combining. 3h-2h - Reserved. 4h - WT or write through. 5h - WP or write protect. 6h - WB or write back. 7h - Reserved.

register.name: PAT
register.desc: Page Attribute Table. This register specifies the memory type based on the PAT, PCD, and PWT bits in the virtual address page tables.

field.name: PA7MemType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Default UC. MemType for {PAT, PCD, PWT} = 7h. ValidValues: 0h - UC or uncacheable. 1h - WC or write combining. 3h-2h - Reserved. 4h - WT or write through. 5h - WP or write protect. 6h - WB or write back. 7h - Reserved.

field.name: PA6MemType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 7h. Default UC. MemType for {PAT, PCD, PWT} = 6h. ValidValues: 0h - UC or uncacheable. 1h - WC or write combining. 3h-2h - Reserved. 4h - WT or write through. 5h - WP or write protect. 6h - WB or write back. 7h - Reserved.

field.name: PA5MemType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 4h. Default WT. MemType for {PAT, PCD, PWT} = 5h. ValidValues: 0h - UC or uncacheable. 1h - WC or write combining. 3h-2h - Reserved. 4h - WT or write through. 5h - WP or write protect. 6h - WB or write back. 7h - Reserved.

field.name: PA4MemType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 6h. Default WB. MemType for {PAT, PCD, PWT} = 4h. ValidValues: 0h - UC or uncacheable. 1h - WC or write combining. 3h-2h - Reserved. 4h - WT or write through. 5h - WP or write protect. 6h - WB or write back. 7h - Reserved.

field.name: PA3MemType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Default UC. MemType for {PAT, PCD, PWT} = 3h. ValidValues: 0h - UC or uncacheable. 1h - WC or write combining. 3h-2h - Reserved. 4h - WT or write through. 5h - WP or write protect. 6h - WB or write back. 7h - Reserved.

field.name: PA2MemType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 7h. Default UC. MemType for {PAT, PCD, PWT} = 2h. ValidValues: 0h - UC or uncacheable. 1h - WC or write combining. 3h-2h - Reserved. 4h - WT or write through. 5h - WP or write protect. 6h - WB or write back. 7h - Reserved.

field.name: PA1MemType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 4h. Default WT. MemType for {PAT, PCD, PWT} = 1h. ValidValues: 0h - UC or uncacheable. 1h - WC or write combining. 3h-2h - Reserved. 4h - WT or write through. 5h - WP or write protect. 6h - WB or write back. 7h - Reserved.

field.name: PA0MemType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 6h. MemType for {PAT, PCD, PWT} = 0h. ValidValues: 0h - UC or uncacheable. 1h - WC or write combining. 3h-2h - Reserved. 4h - WT or write through. 5h - WP or write protect. 6h - WB or write back. 7h - Reserved.

register.name: MTRRdefType
register.desc: MTRR Default Memory Type. See Core::X86::Msr::MtrrVarBase for general MTRR information.

field.name: MtrrDefTypeEn
field.desc: Variable and fixed MTRR enable. Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=Fixed and variable MTRRs are not enabled. 1=Core::X86::Msr::MtrrVarBase, and Core::X86::Msr::MtrrFix_64K through Core::X86::Msr::MtrrFix_4K_7 are enabled.

field.name: MtrrDefTypeFixEn
field.desc: Fixed MTRR enable. Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=Core::X86::Msr::MtrrFix_64K through Core::X86::Msr::MtrrFix_4K_7 are not enabled. 1=Core::X86::Msr::MtrrFix_64K through Core::X86::Msr::MtrrFix_4K_7 are enabled. This field is ignored (and the fixed MTRRs are not enabled) if Core::X86::Msr::MTRRdefType[MtrrDefTypeEn] == 0.

field.name: MemType
field.desc: Memory type. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: If MtrrDefTypeEn == 1, then MemType specifies the memory type for memory space that is not specified by either the fixed or variable range MTRRs. If MtrrDefTypeEn == 0, then the default memory type for all of memory is UC. Valid encodings are {00000b, Core::X86::Msr::MtrrFix_64K through Core::X86::Msr::MtrrFix_4K_7[2:0]}. Other write values cause a GP(0).

register.name: U_CET
register.desc: User CET. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: WRSHSTKEN
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Enables the WRSS instruction in User Mode.

field.name: SHSTKEN
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When Set Shadow stack is enabled in User mode.

register.name: S_CET
register.desc: Supervisor CET. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: WRSHSTKEN
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Enables the WRSS instruction in Supervisor Mode.

field.name: SHSTKEN
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When Set Shadow stack is enabled in Supervisor mode.

register.name: PL0Ssp
register.desc: PL0 Shadow Stack Pointer. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: UserLinAddress
field.desc: PL0 user top of SSP. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. UserLinAddress[63:32] must be zero in 32-bit mode.

register.name: PL1Ssp
register.desc: PL1 Shadow Stack Pointer. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: UserLinAddress
field.desc: PL1 user top of SSP. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. UserLinAddress[63:32] must be zero in 32-bit mode.

register.name: PL2Ssp
register.desc: PL2 Shadow Stack Pointer. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: UserLinAddress
field.desc: PL2 user top of SSP. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. UserLinAddress[63:32] must be zero in 32-bit mode.

register.name: PL3Ssp
register.desc: PL3 Shadow Stack Pointer. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: UserLinAddress
field.desc: PL3 user top of SSP. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. UserLinAddress[63:32] must be zero in 32-bit mode.

register.name: IstSspAddr
register.desc: Interrupt SSP Table Address. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: IntrLinTableAddress
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Shadow Stack Pointer interrupt table.

register.name: APIC_ID
register.desc: APIC ID.

field.name: ApicId[31:0]
field.desc: APIC ID[31:0]. Reset: XXXX_XXXXh. Local x2APIC ID register. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: MsrApicVersion
register.desc: APIC Version.

field.name: ExtApicSpace
field.desc: Extended APIC register space present. Reset: 1. 1=Indicates the presence of extended APIC register space starting at Core::X86::Msr::ExtendedApicFeature. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: DirectedEoiSupport
field.desc: Directed EOI support. Reset: 0. 0=Directed EOI capability not supported. 1=Directed EOI capability supported. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: MaxLvtEntry
field.desc: Reset: XXh. Specifies the number of entries in the local vector table minus one. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: Version
field.desc: Reset: 10h. Indicates the version number of this APIC implementation. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: TPR
register.desc: Task Priority.

field.name: Priority
field.desc: Reset: 00h. This field is assigned by software to set a threshold priority at which the core is interrupted. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: MsrArbitrationPriority
register.desc: Arbitration Priority. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: Priority
field.desc: Reset: 00h. Indicates the current priority for a pending interrupt, or a task or interrupt being serviced by the core. The priority is used to arbitrate between cores to determine which accepts a lowest-priority interrupt request. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: MsrProcessorPriority
register.desc: Processor Priority. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: Priority
field.desc: Reset: 00h. Indicates the core's current priority servicing a task or interrupt, and is used to determine if any pending interrupts should be serviced. It is the higher value of the task priority value and the current highest in-service interrupt. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: EOI
register.desc: End Of Interrupt. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: EOI
field.desc: Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. A write zero to this field indicates the end of interrupt processing the currently in service interrupt. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Write-0-only,Error-on-read,Error-on-write-1 : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: LDR
register.desc: Logical Destination Register. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: ClusterDestination
field.desc: Reset: 0000h. Specifies cluster's destination identification. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: LogicalDestination
field.desc: Reset: 0000h. Specifies one of up to sixteen x2APICs within the cluster specified by ClusterDestination. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write. ValidValues: Bit [0] - x2APIC[0]. [1] - x2APIC[1]. [2] - x2APIC[2]. [3] - x2APIC[3]. [4] - x2APIC[4]. [5] - x2APIC[5]. [6] - x2APIC[6]. [7] - x2APIC[7]. [8] - x2APIC[8]. [9] - x2APIC[9]. [10] - x2APIC[10]. [11] - x2APIC[11]. [12] - x2APIC[12]. [13] - x2APIC[13]. [14] - x2APIC[14]. [15] - x2APIC[15].

register.name: SVR
register.desc: Spurious Interrupt Vector.

field.name: FocusDisable
field.desc: Reset: 0. 1=Disable focus core checking during lowest-priority arbitrated interrupts. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: APICSWEn
field.desc: APIC software enable. Reset: 0. All LVT entry mask bits are set and cannot be cleared. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Reset: FFh. The vector that is sent to the core in the event of a spurious interrupt. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: ISR
register.desc: In Service Register. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Interrupt In Service status bits[255:0] accessible through 8 ISR registers.

field.name: InServiceBits
field.desc: Reset: 0000_0000h. These bits are set when the corresponding interrupt is being serviced by the core. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: TMR
register.desc: Trigger Mode Register. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Trigger Mode status bits[255:0] accessible through 8 TMR registers.

field.name: TriggerModeBits
field.desc: Reset: 0000_0000h. The corresponding trigger mode bit is updated when an interrupt is accepted. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write. ValidValues: Bit [0] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [1] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [2] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [3] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [4] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [5] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [6] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [7] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [8] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [9] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [10] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [11] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [12] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [13] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [14] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [15] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [16] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [17] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [18] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [19] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [20] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [21] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [22] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [23] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [24] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [25] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [26] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [27] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [28] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [29] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [30] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt. [31] - 0=Edge-triggered interrupt. 1=Level-triggered interrupt.

register.name: IRR
register.desc: Interrupt Request Register. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Interrupt Request status bits[255:0] accessible through 8 IRR registers.

field.name: RequestBits
field.desc: Reset: 0000_0000h. The corresponding request bit is set when the an interrupt is accepted by the x2APIC. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: ESR
register.desc: Error Status Register. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: IllegalRegAddr
field.desc: Illegal register address. Reset: 0. This bit indicates that an access to a nonexistent register location within this APIC was attempted. Can only be set in xAPIC mode. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: RcvdIllegalVector
field.desc: Received illegal vector. Reset: 0. This bit indicates that this APIC has received a message with an illegal vector (00h to 0Fh for fixed and lowest priority interrupts). AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: SentIllegalVector
field.desc: Reset: 0. This bit indicates that this x2APIC attempted to send a message with an illegal vector (00h to 0Fh for fixed and lowest priority interrupts). AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: RcvAcceptError
field.desc: Receive accept error. Reset: 0. This bit indicates that a message received by this APIC was not accepted by this or any other x2APIC. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: SendAcceptError
field.desc: Reset: 0. This bit indicates that a message sent by this APIC was not accepted by any x2APIC. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: InterruptCommand
register.desc: Interrupt Command. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: DestinationField
field.desc: Reset: 0000_0000h. The destination encoding used when Core::X86::Msr::InterruptCommand[DestShrthnd] is 00b. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: DestShrthnd
field.desc: Destination shorthand. Reset: 0h. Provides a quick way to specify a destination for a message. If all including self or all excluding self is used, then destination mode is ignored and physical is automatically used. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write. ValidValues: 0h - No shorthand (Destination field). 1h - Self. 2h - All including self. 3h - All excluding self. (This sends a message with a destination encoding of all 1s, so if lowest priority is used the message could end up being reflected back to this APIC.).

field.name: TM
field.desc: Trigger mode. Reset: 0. 0=Edge triggered. 1=Level triggered. Indicates how this interrupt is triggered. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: Level
field.desc: Reset: 0. 0=Deasserted. 1=Asserted. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: DM
field.desc: Destination mode. Reset: 0. 0=Physical. 1=Logical. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: MsgType
field.desc: Reset: 0h. The message types are encoded as follows: AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write. ValidValues: 0h - Fixed. 1h - Lowest Priority. 2h - SMI. 3h - Reserved. 4h - NMI. 5h - INIT. 6h - Startup. 7h - External interrupt.

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Reset: 00h. The vector that is sent for this interrupt source. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: Mode
field.desc: Reset: 0. 0=One-shot. 1=Periodic. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: Mask
field.desc: Reset: 1. 0=Not masked. 1=Masked. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: DS
field.desc: Interrupt delivery status. Reset: 0. 0=Idle. 1=Send pending. (Indicates that the interrupt has not yet been accepted by the core.) AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Reset: 00h. Interrupt vector number. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: MsrThermalLvtEntry
register.desc: LVT Thermal Sensor. Reset: 0000_0000_0001_0000h.

field.name: Mask
field.desc: Reset: 1. 0=Not masked. 1=Masked. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: DS
field.desc: Interrupt delivery status. Reset: 0. 0=Idle. 1=Send pending. (Indicates that the interrupt has not yet been accepted by the core.) AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: MsgType
field.desc: Message type. Reset: 0h. See2.1.14.2.1.14 [Generalized Local Vector Table]. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Reset: 00h. Interrupt vector number. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: MsrPerformanceCounterLvtEntry
register.desc: LVT Performance Monitor. Reset: 0000_0000_0001_0000h. Interrupts for this local vector table are caused by overflows of: 1) Core::X86::Msr::PERF_LEGACY_CTL0..3 (Performance Event Select [3:0]). 2) Core::X86::Msr::PERF_CTL0..5 (Performance Event Select [5:0]).

field.name: Mask
field.desc: Reset: 1. 0=Not masked. 1=Masked. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: DS
field.desc: Interrupt delivery status. Reset: 0. 0=Idle. 1=Send pending. Indicates that the interrupt has not yet been accepted by the core. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: MsgType
field.desc: Message type. Reset: 0h. See2.1.14.2.1.14 [Generalized Local Vector Table]. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Reset: 00h. Interrupt vector number. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: Mask
field.desc: Reset: 1. 0=Not masked. 1=Masked. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: TM
field.desc: Trigger mode. Reset: 0. 0=Edge. 1=Level. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: RmtIRR
field.desc: Reset: 0. If trigger mode is level, remote Core::X86::Msr::IRR is set when the interrupt has begun service. Remote Core::X86::Msr::IRR is cleared when the end of interrupt has occurred. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: DS
field.desc: Interrupt delivery status. Reset: 0. 0=Idle. 1=Send pending. (Indicates that the interrupt has not yet been accepted by the core.) AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: MsgType
field.desc: Message type. Reset: 0h. See2.1.14.2.1.14 [Generalized Local Vector Table]. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Reset: 00h. Interrupt vector number. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: Mask
field.desc: Reset: 1. 0=Not masked. 1=Masked. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: DS
field.desc: Interrupt delivery status. Reset: 0. 0=Idle. 1=Send pending. (Indicates that the interrupt has not yet been accepted by the core.) AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: MsgType
field.desc: Message type. Reset: 0h. See 2.1.14.2.1.14 [Generalized Local Vector Table]. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Reset: 00h. Interrupt vector number. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: MsrTimerInitialCount
register.desc: Timer Initial Count. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: Count
field.desc: Reset: 0000_0000h. The value copied into the current count register when the timer is loaded or reloaded. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: MsrTimerCurrentCount
register.desc: Timer Current Count. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: Count
field.desc: Reset: 0000_0000h. The current value of the counter. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read,Error-on-write,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: MsrTimerDivideConfiguration
register.desc: Timer Divide Configuration. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: Div[3:0]
field.desc: Reset: 0h. Div[2] is unused. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write. ValidValues: 0h - Divide by 2. 1h - Divide by 4. 2h - Divide by 8. 3h - Divide by 16. 7h-4h - Reserved. 8h - Divide by 32. 9h - Divide by 64. Ah - Divide by 128. Bh - Divide by 1. Fh-Ch - Reserved.

register.name: SelfIPI
register.desc: Self IPI. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. The self IPI register provides a perforamnce optimized path for sending self IPI's. A self IPI is semantically identical to an inter-processor interrupt sent via the ICR, with a Destination Shorthand of Self, Trigger Mode equal to Edge, and a Delivery Mode equal to Fixed.

field.name: Vector
field.desc: Reset: 00h. Interrupt vector number. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Write-only,Error-on-read : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: MsrExtendedApicFeature
register.desc: Extended APIC Feature. Reset: 0000_0000_0004_0007h.

field.name: ExtLvtCount
field.desc: Extended local vector table count. Reset: 04h. This specifies the number of extended LVT registers (Core::X86::Msr::ExtendedInterruptLvtEntries) in the local APIC. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: ExtApicIdCap
field.desc: Extended APIC ID capable. Reset: 1. 1=The processor is capable of supporting an 8-bit APIC ID, as controlled by Core::X86::Msr::ExtendedApicControl[ExtApicIdEn]. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: SeoiCap
field.desc: Specific end of interrupt capable. Reset: 1. 1=The Core::X86::Msr::SpecificEndOfInterrupt is present. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: IerCap
field.desc: Interrupt enable register capable. Reset: 1. This bit indicates that the Core::X86::Msr::InterruptEnable0 - 7 are present. See 2.1.14.2.1.8 [Interrupt Masking]. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-only,Error-on-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: MsrExtendedApicControl
register.desc: Extended APIC Control. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: ExtApicIdEn
field.desc: Extended APIC ID enable. Reset: 0. 1=Enable 8-bit APIC ID; Core::X86::Msr::APIC_ID[ApicId[31:0]] supports an 8-bit value; an interrupt broadcast in physical destination mode requires that the IntDest[7:0]=1111_1111b (instead of xxxx_1111b); a match in physical destination mode occurs when (IntDest[7:0] == ApicId[7:0]) instead of (IntDest[3:0] == ApicId[3:0]). AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: SeoiEn
field.desc: Reset: 0. 1=Enable SEOI generation when a write to Core::X86::Msr::SpecificEndOfInterrupt is received. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: IerEn
field.desc: Reset: 0. 1=Enable writes to the interrupt enable registers. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: MsrSpecificEndOfInterrupt
register.desc: Specific End Of Interrupt. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: EoiVec
field.desc: End of interrupt vector. Reset: 00h. A write to this field causes an end of interrupt cycle to be performed for the vector specified in this field. The behavior is undefined if no interrupt is pending for the specified interrupt vector. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: InterruptEnable0
register.desc: Interrupt Enable 0. Reset: 0000_0000_FFFF_FFFFh.

field.name: InterruptEnableBits
field.desc: Reset: FFFFh. The interrupt enable bits can be used to enable each of the 256 interrupts. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: InterruptEnable71
register.desc: Interrupt Enable 7..1. Reset: 0000_0000_FFFF_FFFFh.

field.name: InterruptEnableBits
field.desc: Reset: FFFF_FFFFh. The interrupt enable bits can be used to enable each of the 256 interrupts. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: MsrExtendedInterruptLvtEntries
register.desc: Extended Interrupt Local Vector Table. Reset: 0000_0000_0001_0000h.

field.name: Mask
field.desc: Reset: 1. 0=Not masked. 1=Masked. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: DS
field.desc: Interrupt delivery status. Reset: 0. 0=Idle. 1=Send pending. Indicates that the interrupt has not yet been accepted by the core. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write,Volatile : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

field.name: MsgType
field.desc: Message type. Reset: 0h. See2.1.14.2.1.14 [Generalized Local Vector Table]. AccessType: X2APICEN ? Read-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.
