In some cases, certain combinations can result in misleading counts, or the UnitMask value is an ordinal rather than a bit mask. These situations are described where applicable, or should be obvious from the event descriptions. For events where no UnitMask table is shown, the UnitMask is Unused.

EventSelect specifies the event or event duration in a processor unit to be counted by the corresponding PERF_CTR[5:0] register. The events are specified in 2.1.17.4 [Core Performance Monitor Counters]. Some events are Reserved; when a Reserved event is selected, the results are undefined.

register.name: PERF_CTL4
register.desc: Performance Event Select 4. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. See 2.1.17 [Performance Monitor Counters].

field.name: HostGuestOnly
field.desc: Count only host/guest events. Read-write. Reset: 0h. ValidValues: 0h - Count all events, irrespective of guest/host. 1h - Count guest events if [SVME] == 1. 2h - Count host events if [SVME] == 1. 3h - Count all guest and host events if [SVME] == 1.

field.name: EventSelect[11:8]
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Performance event select[11:8].

The events are specified in 2.1.17.4 [Core Performance Monitor Counters]. Some events are Reserved; when a Reserved event is selected, the results are undefined.

register.name: PERF_CTL5
register.desc: Performance Event Select 5. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. See 2.1.17 [Performance Monitor Counters].

field.name: HostGuestOnly
field.desc: Count only host/guest events. Read-write. Reset: 0h. ValidValues: 0h - Count all events, irrespective of guest/host. 1h - Count guest events if [SVME] == 1. 2h - Count host events if [SVME] == 1. 3h - Count all guest and host events if [SVME] == 1.

All events selected by UnitMask are simultaneously monitored. Unless otherwise stated, the UnitMask values shown may be combined (logically ORed) to select any desired combination of the sub-events for a given event. In some cases, certain combinations can result in misleading counts, or the UnitMask value is an ordinal rather than a bit mask. These situations are described where applicable, or should be obvious from the event descriptions. For events where no UnitMask table is shown, the UnitMask is Unused.

field.name: EventSelect[7:0]
field.desc: Event select. Read-write. Reset: 00h. EventSelect[11:0] = {EventSelect[11:8], EventSelect[7:0]}. EventSelect specifies the event or event duration in a processor unit to be counted by the corresponding PERF_CTR[5:0] register. The events are specified in 2.1.17.4 [Core Performance Monitor Counters]. Some events are Reserved; when a Reserved event is selected, the results are undefined.

register.name: ChL3PmcCfg
register.desc: L3 Performance Event Select [5:0]. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. See 2.1.17.5 [L3 Cache Performance Monitor Counters].

field.name: ThreadMask
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Controls which of the 2 threads in the selected core are being counted. In non-SMT mode, thread[0] must be selected. One or more threads must be selected unless otherwise specified by the specific L3PMC event. ValidValues: Bit [0] - Thread[0]. [1] - Thread[1]. [3:2] - Reserved.

field.name: SliceId
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Controls the L3 slice for which events are counted. Unless otherwise noted by the specific L3PMC event, use Core::X86::Msr::ChL3PmcCfg[SliceId] to select an individual slice or Core::X86::Msr::ChL3PmcCfg[EnAllSlices] to select all slices. ValidValues: 7h-0h - <Value> Slice.

field.name: EnAllCores
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. 1=Enable counting L3 events for all cores simultaneously.

field.name: EnAllSlices
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. 1=Enable counting L3 events for all 8 L3 slices simultaneously.

field.name: CoreId
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Controls core for which events are to be counted. See Core::X86::Msr::ChL3PmcCfg[EnAllCores] to count all cores simultaneously. ValidValues: 7h-0h - <Value> CoreId.

field.name: Enable
field.desc: Enable L3 performance counter. Read-write. Reset: 0. 1=Enable.

field.name: UnitMask
field.desc: Event qualification. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Each UnitMask bit further specifies or qualifies the event specified by EventSelect. All events selected by UnitMask are simultaneously monitored. Unless otherwise stated, the UnitMask values shown may be combined (logically ORed) to select any desired combination of the sub-events for a given event. In some cases, certain combinations can result in misleading counts, or the UnitMask value is an ordinal rather than a bit mask. These situations are described where applicable, or should be obvious from the event descriptions. For events where no UnitMask table is shown, the UnitMask is Unused. When selecting an event for which not all UnitMask bits are defined, the undefined UnitMask bits should be set to zero.

field.name: EventSel
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. L3 Event select.

register.name: ChL3Pmc
register.desc: L3 Performance Event Counter [5:0]. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Also can be read via x86 instructions RDPMC ECX=[0F:0A].

field.name: Overflow
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Count overflow bit.

field.name: CountHi
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000h. Bits[47:32] of the count.

field.name: CountLo
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h. Bits[31:0] of the count.

register.name: DF_PERF_CTL
register.desc: Data Fabric Performance Event Select [3:0]. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. See 2.1.17 [Performance Monitor Counters]. The DF Performance Monitors are shared by all cores/threads in the node. See 2.1.11 [Register Sharing].

field.name: EventSelect[13:12]
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Performance event select [13:12].

field.name: EventSelect[11:8]
field.desc: Performance event select. Read-write. Reset: 0h. Performance event select [11:0].

field.name: En
field.desc: Enable performance counter. Read-write. Reset: 0. 1=Performance event counter is enabled.

field.name: UnitMask
field.desc: Event qualification. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Each UnitMask bit further specifies or qualifies the event specified by EventSelect. All events selected by UnitMask are simultaneously monitored.

field.name: EventSelect[7:0]
field.desc: Event select. Read-write. Reset: 00h. This field, along with EventSelect[13:12] and EventSelect[11:8] above, combine to form the 14-bit event select field, EventSelect[13:0]. EventSelect specifies the event or event duration in a processor unit to be counted by the corresponding DF_PERF_CTR[3:0] register. Some events are reserved; when a reserved event is selected, the results are undefined.

register.name: DF_PERF_CTR
register.desc: Data Fabric Performance Event Counter [3:0]. See Core::X86::Msr::DF_PERF_CTL. Also can be read via x86 instructions RDPMC ECX = [09:06]. The DF Performance Monitors are shared by all cores/threads in the node. See 2.1.11 [Register Sharing].

field.name: CTR[47:0]
field.desc: Performance counter value[47:0]. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000h. The current value of the event counter.

register.name: RAPL_PWR_UNIT
register.desc: RAPL Power Unit. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_000A_1003h.

field.name: TU
field.desc: Time Units in seconds. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: Ah. Time information (in Seconds) is based on the multiplier, 1/ 2^TU; where TU is an unsigned integer. Default value is 1010b, indicating time unit is in 976 microseconds increment. ValidValues: Fh-0h - 1/2^<Value> Seconds.

field.name: ESU
field.desc: Energy Status Units. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 10h. Energy information (in Joules) is based on the multiplier, 1/2^ESU; where ESU is an unsigned integer. Default value is 10000b, indicating energy status unit is in 15.3 micro-Joules increment. ValidValues: 1Fh-00h - 1/2^<Value> Joules.

field.name: PU
field.desc: Power Units. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 3h. Power information (in Watts) is based on the multiplier, 1/ 2^PU; where PU is an unsigned integer. Default value is 0011b, indicating power unit is in 1/8 Watts increment. ValidValues: Fh-0h - 1/2^<Value> Watts.

register.name: CORE_ENERGY_STAT
register.desc: Core Energy Status. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: TotalEnergyConsumed
field.desc: Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: PKG_ENERGY_STAT
register.desc: Package Energy Status. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: TotalEnergyConsumed
field.desc: Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: PPIN_CTL
register.desc: Protected Processor Inventory Number Control. Unpredictable.

field.name: PPIN_EN
field.desc: Unpredictable. Reset: X. 0=Reading Core::X86::Msr::PPIN will cause a #GP. 1=Core::X86::Msr::PPIN is accessible using RDMSR. Once set, attempting to write 1 to Core::X86::Msr::PPIN_CTL[Lockout] will cause a #GP.

field.name: Lockout
field.desc: Unpredictable. Reset: X. 0=Writes to Core::X86::Msr::PPIN_CTL are permitted if PPIN_EN == 0. 1=Further Writes to Core::X86::Msr::PPIN_CTL are ignored. Description: Writing 1 to Core::X86::Msr::PPIN_CTL[Lockout] is permitted only if Core::X86::Msr::PPIN_CTL[PPIN_EN] == 0. BIOS should provide an opt-in menu to enable the user to turn on Core::X86::Msr::PPIN_CTL[PPIN_EN] for privileged inventory initialization agent to access Core::X86::Msr::PPIN. After reading Core::X86::Msr::PPIN, the privileged inventory initialization agent should write 00b followed by 01b to Core::X86::Msr::PPIN_CTL to disable further access to MSR_PPIN and prevent unauthorized modification to MSR_PPIN_CTL. Once this bit is written with 1, subsequent writes to this register are ignored, and a reset (warm or cold) is required in order to clear it, which gives BIOS the opportunity to set it again at the next boot.

register.name: PPIN
register.desc: Protected Processor Inventory Number.

field.name: PPIN
field.desc: Reset: Fixed,XXXX_XXXX_XXXX_XXXXh. Protected Processor Inventory Number. AccessType: ({Core::X86::Msr::PPIN_CTL[PPIN_EN] , Core::X86::Msr::PPIN_CTL[Lockout]} == 2h) ? Read,Error-on-write : Error-on-read,Error-on-write.

register.name: SAMP_BR_FROM
register.desc: Sample Branch From. Read-write,Volatile. These MSRs are paired elements of a SAMP_BR_FROM register N and SAMP_BR_TO register N. The first sample is intended to be in register 0 and the last sample in register N-1.

field.name: BranchFrmOffSet
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: XXXX_XXXX_XXXX_XXXXh. The 64 bit Segment offset (RIP) of the Branch Instruction.

register.name: SAMP_BR_TO
register.desc: Sample Branch To. These MSRs are paired elements of a SAMP_BR_FROM register N and SAMP_BR_TO register N. The first sample is intended to be in register 0 and the last sample in register N-1.

field.name: BranchToOffSet
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: XXXX_XXXX_XXXX_XXXXh. The 64 bit Segment offset (RIP) of the Target of the Branch Instruction.

register.name: CPUID_7_Features
register.desc: CPUID Features for CPUID Fn00000007_E[A,B]X. Read-write. Core::X86::Msr::CPUID_7_Features[63:32] provides control over values read from Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEax0; Core::X86::Msr::CPUID_7_Features[31:0] provides control over values read from Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0.

field.name: SHA
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[SHA]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[SHA].

field.name: CLWB
field.desc: Cache line write back. Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[CLWB]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[CLWB].

field.name: CLFSHOPT
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[CLFSHOPT]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[CLFSHOPT].

field.name: SMAP
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[SMAP]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[SMAP].

field.name: ADX
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[ADX]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[ADX].

field.name: RDSEED
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[RDSEED]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[RDSEED].

field.name: PQE
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[PQE]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[PQE].

field.name: PQM
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[PQM]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[PQM].

field.name: INVPCID
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[INVPCID]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[INVPCID].

field.name: BMI2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[BMI2]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[BMI2].

field.name: SMEP
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[SMEP]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[SMEP].

field.name: AVX2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[AVX2]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[AVX2].

field.name: BMI1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[BMI1]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[BMI1].

field.name: FSGSBASE
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[FSGSBASE]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::StructExtFeatIdEbx0[FSGSBASE].

register.name: CPUID_PWR_THERM
register.desc: Thermal and Power Management CPUID Features. Read-write. Core::X86::Msr::CPUID_PWR_THERM provides control over values read from Core::X86::Cpuid::ThermalPwrMgmtEcx.

field.name: EffFreq
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::ThermalPwrMgmtEcx[EffFreq]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::ThermalPwrMgmtEcx[EffFreq].

register.name: CPUID_Features
register.desc: CPUID Features for CPUID Fn00000001_E[C,D]X. Read-write. Core::X86::Msr::CPUID_Features[63:32] provides control over values read from Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx; Core::X86::Msr::CPUID_Features[31:0] provides control over values read from Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEdx.

field.name: RDRAND
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[RDRAND]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[RDRAND].

field.name: F16C
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[F16C]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[F16C].

field.name: AVX
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[AVX]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[AVX].

field.name: OSXSAVE
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[OSXSAVE]. Modifies Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[OSXSAVE] only if CR4[OSXSAVE].

field.name: XSAVE
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[XSAVE]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[XSAVE].

field.name: AES
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[AES]. Modifies Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[AES] only if the reset value is 1.

field.name: POPCNT
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[POPCNT]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[POPCNT].

field.name: MOVBE
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[MOVBE]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[MOVBE].

field.name: X2APIC
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[X2APIC]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[X2APIC].

field.name: SSE42
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[SSE42]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[SSE42].

field.name: SSE41
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[SSE41]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[SSE41].

field.name: PCID
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[PCID]. See Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureIdEcx[PCID].

field.name: CMPXCHG16B

register.name: DR1_ADDR_MASK
register.desc: Address Mask For DR1 Breakpoint. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Support indicated by Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[DataBreakpointExtension].

field.name: AddrMask
field.desc: Mask for DR linear address data breakpoint DR1. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. 1=Exclude bit into address compare. 0=Include bit into address compare. See Core::X86::Msr::DR1_ADDR_MASK. AddrMask[11:0] qualifies the DR1 linear address instruction breakpoint, allowing the DR1 instruction breakpoint on a range of addresses in memory.

register.name: DR2_ADDR_MASK
register.desc: Address Mask For DR2 Breakpoint. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Support indicated by Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[DataBreakpointExtension].

field.name: AddrMask
field.desc: Mask for DR linear address data breakpoint DR2. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. 1=Exclude bit into address compare. 0=Include bit into address compare. See Core::X86::Msr::DR0_ADDR_MASK. AddrMask[11:0] qualifies the DR2 linear address instruction breakpoint, allowing the DR2 instruction breakpoint on a range of addresses in memory.

register.name: DR3_ADDR_MASK
register.desc: Address Mask For DR3 Breakpoint. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Support indicated by Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[DataBreakpointExtension].

field.name: AddrMask
field.desc: Mask for DR linear address data breakpoint DR3. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. 1=Exclude bit into address compare. 0=Include bit into address compare. See Core::X86::Msr::DR0_ADDR_MASK. AddrMask[11:0] qualifies the DR3 linear address instruction breakpoint, allowing the DR3 instruction breakpoint on a range of addresses in memory.

register.name: DR0_ADDR_MASK
register.desc: Address Mask For DR0 Breakpoints. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Support for DR0[31:12] is indicated by Core::X86::Cpuid::FeatureExtIdEcx[DataBreakpointExtension]. See Core::X86::Msr::DR1_ADDR_MASK.

field.name: DR0
field.desc: Mask for DR0 linear address data breakpoint. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. 1=Exclude bit into address compare. 0=Include bit into address compare. See Core::X86::Msr::DR1_ADDR_MASK. This field qualifies the DR0 linear address data breakpoint, allowing the DR0 data breakpoint on a range of addresses in memory. AddrMask[11:0] qualifies the DR0 linear address instruction breakpoint, allowing the DR0 instruction breakpoint on a range of addresses in memory. DR0[31:12] is only valid for data breakpoints. The legacy DR0 breakpoint function is provided by DR0[31:0] == 0000_0000h). The mask bits are active high. DR0 is always used, and it can be used in conjunction with any debug function that uses DR0.

register.name: IBS_FETCH_CTL
register.desc: IBS Fetch Control. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. See 2.1.18 [Instruction Based Sampling (IBS)]. The IBS fetch sampling engine is described as follows: 1) The periodic fetch counter is an internal 20-bit counter: 1.1) The periodic fetch counter[19:4] is set to IbsFetchCnt[19:4] and the periodic fetch counter[3:0] is set according to IbsRandEn when IbsFetchEn is changed from 0 to 1. 1.2) It increments for every fetch cycle that completes when IbsFetchEn == 1 and IbsFetchVal == 0. 1.2.1) The periodic fetch counter is undefined when IbsFetchEn == 0 or IbsFetchVal == 1. 1.3) When IbsFetchCnt[19:4] is read it returns the current value of the periodic fetch counter[19:4]. 2) When the periodic fetch counter reaches {IbsFetchMaxCnt[19:4],0h} and the selected instruction fetch completes or is aborted: 2.1) IbsFetchVal is set to 1. 2.1.1) Drivers can't assume that IbsFetchCnt[19:4] is 0 when IbsFetchVal == 1. 3) The status of the operation is written to the IBS fetch registers (this register, Core::X86::Msr::IBS_FETCH_LINADDR and Core::X86::Msr::IBS_FETCH_PHYSADDR). 4) An interrupt is generated as specified by Core::X86::Msr::IBS_CTL. The interrupt service routine associated with this interrupt is responsible for saving the performance information stored in IBS execution registers.

field.name: IbsFetchL2Miss
field.desc: L2 cache miss for the sampled fetch. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The instruction fetch missed in the L2 Cache. Qualified by (IbsFetchComp == 1).

field.name: IbsRandEn
field.desc: Random instruction fetch tagging enable. Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=Bits[3:0] of the fetch counter are set to 0h when IbsFetchEn is set to start the fetch counter. 1=Bits[3:0] of the fetch counter are randomized when IbsFetchEn is set to start the fetch counter.

field.name: IbsL2TlbMiss
field.desc: Instruction cache L2TLB miss. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The instruction fetch missed in the L2 TLB.

field.name: IbsL1TlbMiss
field.desc: Instruction cache L1TLB miss. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The instruction fetch missed in the L1 TLB.

field.name: IbsL1TlbPgSz
field.desc: Instruction cache L1TLB page size. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0h. Indicates the page size of the translation in the L1 TLB. This field is only valid if IbsPhyAddrValid == 1. ValidValues: 0h - 4 KB. 1h - 2 MB. 2h - 1 GB. 3h - Reserved.

field.name: IbsPhyAddrValid
field.desc: Instruction fetch physical address valid. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The physical address in Core::X86::Msr::IBS_FETCH_PHYSADDR and the IbsL1TlbPgSz field are valid for the instruction fetch.

field.name: IbsIcMiss
field.desc: Instruction cache miss. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The instruction fetch missed in the instruction cache.

field.name: IbsFetchComp
field.desc: Instruction fetch complete. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The instruction fetch completed and the data is available for use by the instruction decoder.

field.name: IbsFetchVal
field.desc: Instruction fetch valid. Read,Write-0-only,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=New instruction fetch data available. When this bit is set, the fetch counter stops counting and an interrupt is generated as specified by Core::X86::Msr::IBS_CTL. This bit must be cleared for the fetch counter to start counting. When clearing this bit, software can write 0000h to IbsFetchCnt[19:4] to start the fetch counter at IbsFetchMaxCnt[19:4].

field.name: IbsFetchEn
field.desc: Instruction fetch enable. Read-write. Reset: 0. 1=Instruction fetch sampling is enabled.

field.name: IbsFetchLat
field.desc: Instruction fetch latency. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000h. Indicates the number of clock cycles from when the instruction fetch was initiated to when the data was delivered to the core. If the instruction fetch is abandoned before the fetch completes, this field returns the number of clock cycles from when the instruction fetch was initiated to when the fetch was abandoned.

field.name: IbsFetchCnt[19:4]
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000h. Provides Read/Write access to bits[19:4] of the periodic fetch counter. Programming this field to a value greater than or equal to IbsFetchMaxCnt[19:4] results in undefined behavior.

field.name: IbsFetchMaxCnt[19:4]
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000h. Specifies bits[19:4] of the maximum count value of the periodic fetch counter. Programming this field to 0000h and setting IbsFetchEn results in undefined behavior. Bits[3:0] of the maximum count are always 0000b.

register.name: IBS_FETCH_LINADDR
register.desc: IBS Fetch Linear Address. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: IbsFetchLinAd
field.desc: Instruction fetch linear address. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Provides the linear address in canonical form for the tagged instruction fetch.

register.name: IBS_FETCH_PHYSADDR
register.desc: IBS Fetch Physical Address.

field.name: IbsFetchPhysAd
field.desc: Instruction fetch physical address. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000h. Provides the physical address for the tagged instruction fetch. The lower 12 bits are not modified by address translation, so they are always the same as the linear address. This field contains valid data only if Core::X86::Msr::IBS_FETCH_CTL[IbsPhyAddrValid] is asserted.

register.name: IBS_OP_CTL
register.desc: IBS Execution Control. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. See 2.1.18 [Instruction Based Sampling (IBS)]. The IBS execution sampling engine is described as follows for IbsOpCntCtl == 1. If IbsOpCntCtl == 1n then references to "periodic op counter" mean "periodic cycle counter". 1) The periodic op counter is an internal 27-bit counter: 1.1) It is set to IbsOpCurCnt[26:0] when IbsOpEn is changed from 0 to 1. 1.2) It increments every dispatched macro-op when IbsOpEn == 1 and IbsOpVal == 0. 1.2.1) The periodic op counter is undefined when IbsOpEn == 0 or IbsOpVal == 1. 1.3) When IbsOpCurCnt[26:0] is read then it returns the current value of the periodic op counter[26:0]. 2) When the periodic op counter reaches IbsOpMaxCnt: 2.1) The next dispatched op is tagged if IbsOpCntCtl == 1. A valid op in the next dispatched line is tagged if IbsOpCntCtl == 0. See IbsOpCntCtl. 2.2) The periodic op counter[26:7] = 0; bits[6:0] is randomized by hardware. 3) The periodic op counter is not modified when a tagged op is flushed. 4) When a tagged op is retired: 4.1) IbsOpVal is set to 1. 4.1.1) Drivers can't assume that IbsOpCurCnt is 0 when IbsOpVal == 1. 5) The status of the operation is written to the IBS execution registers (this register, Core::X86::Msr::IBS_OP_RIP, Core::X86::Msr::IBS_OP_DATA, Core::X86::Msr::IBS_OP_DATA2, Core::X86::Msr::IBS_OP_DATA3, Core::X86::Msr::IBS_DC_LINADDR and Core::X86::Msr::IBS_DC_PHYSADDR). 6) An interrupt is generated as specified by Core::X86::Msr::IBS_CTL. The interrupt service routine associated with this interrupt is responsible for saving the performance information stored in IBS execution registers.

field.name: IbsOpCurCnt[26:0]
field.desc: Periodic op counter current count. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 000_0000h. Returns the current value of the periodic op counter.

field.name: IbsOpMaxCnt[26:20]
field.desc: Periodic op counter maximum count. Read-write. Reset: 00h. See IbsOpMaxCnt[19:4].

field.name: IbsOpCntCtl
field.desc: Periodic op counter count control. Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=Count clock cycles; a 1-of-4 round-robin counter selects an op in the next dispatch line; if the op pointed to by the round-robin counter is invalid, then the next younger valid op is selected. 1=Count dispatched ops; when a roll-over occurs, the counter is preloaded with a pseudorandom 7-bit value between 1 and 127.

field.name: IbsOpVal
field.desc: Op sample valid. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=New instruction execution data available; the periodic op counter is disabled from counting. An interrupt may be generated when this bit is set as specified by Core::X86::Msr::IBS_CTL[LvtOffset].

field.name: IbsOpEn
field.desc: Op sampling enable. Read-write. Reset: 0. 1=Instruction execution sampling enabled.

field.name: IbsOpMaxCnt[19:4]
field.desc: Periodic op counter maximum count. Read-write. Reset: 0000h. IbsOpMaxCnt[26:0] = {IbsOpMaxCnt[26:20], IbsOpMaxCnt[19:4], 0000b}. Specifies maximum count value of the periodic op counter. Bits [3:0] of the maximum count are always 0000b. ValidValues: 0008h-0000h - Reserved. FFFFh-0009h - <Value> *16 Ops.

register.name: IBS_OP_RIP
register.desc: IBS Op RIP. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: IbsOpRip
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. 64-bit Segment offset (RIP) of the instruction that contains the tagged op.

register.name: IBS_OP_DATA
register.desc: IBS Op Data. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: IbsOpMicrocode
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Tagged operation from microcode.

field.name: IbsOpBrnFuse
field.desc: Fused branch op. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Tagged operation was a fused branch op. Support indicated by Core::X86::Cpuid::IbsIdEax[OpBrnFuse].

field.name: IbsRipInvalid
field.desc: RIP is invalid. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Tagged operation RIP is invalid. Support indicated by Core::X86::Cpuid::IbsIdEax[RipInvalidChk].

field.name: IbsOpBrnRet
field.desc: Branch op retired. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Tagged operation was a branch op that retired.

field.name: IbsOpBrnMisp
field.desc: Mispredicted branch op. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Tagged operation was a branch op that was mispredicted. Qualified by IbsOpBrnRet == 1.

field.name: IbsOpBrnTaken
field.desc: Taken branch op. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Tagged operation was a branch op that was taken. Qualified by IbsOpBrnRet == 1.

field.name: IbsOpReturn
field.desc: Return op. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Tagged operation was return op. Qualified by (IbsOpBrnRet == 1).

field.name: IbsTagToRetCtr
field.desc: Op tag to retire count. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000h. This field returns the number of cycles from when the op was tagged to when the op was retired. This field is equal to IbsCompToRetCtr when the tagged op is a NOP.

field.name: IbsCompToRetCtr
field.desc: Op completion to retire count. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000h. This field returns the number of cycles from when the op was completed to when the op was retired.

register.name: IBS_OP_DATA2
register.desc: IBS Op Data 2. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Data is only valid for load operations that miss both the L1 data cache and the L2 cache. If a load operation crosses a cache line boundary, the data returned in this register is the data for the access to the lower cache line.

field.name: RmtNode
field.desc: IBS request destination node. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 0=The request is serviced by the NB in the same node as the core. 1=The request is serviced by the NB in a different node than the core. Valid when NbIbsReqSrc is non-zero.

field.name: DataSrc
field.desc: Northbridge IBS request data source. Read-write. Reset: 0h. ValidValues: 0h - No valid status. 1h - Reserved. 2h - Cache: data returned from shared L3, other L2 on same CCX or other core's cache trough same node. 3h - DRAM: data returned from DRAM. 4h - Cache: other core's cache through remote node. 6h-5h - Reserved. 7h - Other; data returned from MMIO/Config/PCI/APIC.

register.name: IBS_OP_DATA3
register.desc: IBS Op Data 3. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. If a load or store operation crosses a 256-bit boundary, the data returned in this register is the data for the access to the data below the 256-bit boundary.

field.name: IbsTlbRefillLat
field.desc: L1 DTLB refill latency. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000h. The number of cycles from when a L1 DTLB refill is triggered by a tagged op to when the L1 DTLB fill has been completed.

field.name: IbsDcMissLat
field.desc: Data cache miss latency. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000h. Indicates the number of clock cycles from when a miss is detected in the data cache to when the data was delivered to the core. The value returned by this counter is not valid for data cache writes or prefetch instructions.

field.name: IbsOpDcMissOpenMemReqs
field.desc: Outstanding memory requests on DC fill. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 00h. The number of allocated, valid DC MABs when the MAB corresponding to a tagged DC miss op is deallocated. Includes the MAB allocated by the sampled op. 00000b=No information provided.

field.name: IbsOpMemWidth
field.desc: Load/store size in bytes. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0h. Report the number of bytes the load or store is attempting to access. ValidValues: 0h - No information provided. 1h - Byte. 2h - Word. 3h - DW. 4h - QW. 5h - OW. Fh-6h - Reserved.

field.name: IbsSwPf
field.desc: Software prefetch. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The op is a software prefetch.

field.name: IbsL2Miss
field.desc: L2 cache miss for the sampled operation. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The operation missed in the L2, regardless of whether the op initiated the request to the L2.

field.name: IbsDcPhyAddrValid
field.desc: Data cache physical address valid. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The physical address in Core::X86::Msr::IBS_DC_PHYSADDR is valid for the load or store operation.

field.name: IbsDcLinAddrValid
field.desc: Data cache linear address valid. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The linear address in Core::X86::Msr::IBS_DC_LINADDR is valid for the load or store operation.

field.name: DcMissNoMabAlloc
field.desc: DC miss with no MAB allocated. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The tagged load or store operation hit on an already allocated MAB.

field.name: IbsDcLockedOp
field.desc: Locked operation. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Tagged load or store operation is a locked operation.

field.name: IbsDcUcMemAcc
field.desc: UC memory access. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Tagged load or store operation accessed uncacheable memory.

field.name: IbsDcWcMemAcc
field.desc: WC memory access. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Tagged load or store operation accessed write combining memory.

field.name: IbsDcMisAcc
field.desc: Misaligned access. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The tagged load or store operation crosses a 256-bit address boundary.

field.name: IbsDcMiss
field.desc: Data cache miss. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The cache line used by the tagged load or store was not present in the data cache.

field.name: IbsDcPgSz
field.desc: Data cache page size. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0h. Page size information is only availabe when IbsDcPhyAddrValid is set. ValidValues: 0h - 4 KB. 1h - 2 MB. 2h - 1 GB. 3h - Reserved.

field.name: IbsDcL2TlbMiss
field.desc: Data cache L2TLB miss. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The physical address for the tagged load or store operation was not present in the data cache L2TLB.

field.name: IbsDcL1tlbMiss
field.desc: Data cache L1TLB miss. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=The physical address for the tagged load or store operation was not present in the data cache L1TLB.

field.name: IbsStOp
field.desc: Store op. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Tagged operation is a store operation.

field.name: IbsLdOp
field.desc: Load op. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0. 1=Tagged operation is a load operation.

register.name: IBS_DC_LINADDR
register.desc: IBS DC Linear Address. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: IbsDcLinAd
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Provides the linear address in canonical form for the tagged load or store operation. This field contains valid data only if Core::X86::Msr::IBS_OP_DATA3[IbsDcLinAddrValid] is asserted.

register.name: IBS_DC_PHYSADDR
register.desc: IBS DC Physical Address. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h.

field.name: IbsDcPhysAd
field.desc: Load or store physical address. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000h. Provides the physical address for the tagged load or store operation. The lower 12 bits are not modified by address translation, so they are always the same as the linear address. This field contains valid data only if Core::X86::Msr::IBS_OP_DATA3[IbsDcPhyAddrValid] is asserted.

register.name: IBS_CTL
register.desc: IBS Control. Read,Error-on-write.

field.name: LvtOffsetVal
field.desc: Local vector table offset valid. Read,Error-on-write. Reset: X.

field.name: LvtOffset
field.desc: Local vector table offset. Read,Error-on-write. Reset: Xh.

register.name: BP_IBSTGT_RIP
register.desc: IBS Branch Target Address. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Support for this register indicated by Core::X86::Cpuid::IbsIdEax[BrnTrgt].

field.name: IbsBrTarget
field.desc: Read-write,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. The logical address in canonical form for the branch target. Contains a valid target if non-0. Qualified by Core::X86::Msr::IBS_OP_DATA[IbsOpBrnRet] == 1.

register.name: IC_IBS_EXTD_CTL
register.desc: IBS Fetch Control Extended. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Support for this register indicated by Core::X86::Cpuid::IbsIdEax[IbsFetchCtlExtd].

field.name: IbsItlbRefillLat
field.desc: ITLB Refill Latency for the sampled fetch, if there is a reload. Read-only,Volatile. Reset: 0000h. The number of cycles when the fetch engine is stalled for an ITLB reload for the sampled fetch. If there is no reload, the latency is 0.

register.name: Merge
register.desc: Merge. See 2.1.17.3 [Large Increment per Cycle Events].

register.name: FpRetSseAvxOps
register.desc: Retired SSE/AVX FLOPs. Read-write. Reset: 00h. This is a retire-based event. The number of retired SSE/AVX FLOPs. The number of events logged per cycle can vary from 0 to 64. This event requires the use of the MergeEvent since it can count above 15 events per cycle. See 2.1.17.3 [Large Increment per Cycle Events]. It does not provide a useful count without the use of the MergeEvent.

field.name: MacFLOPs
field.desc: Multiply-Accumulate FLOPs. Read-write. Reset: 0. Each MAC operation is counted as 2 FLOPS.

field.name: DivFLOPs
field.desc: Divide/square root FLOPs. Read-write. Reset: 0.

field.name: MultFLOPs
field.desc: Multiply FLOPs. Read-write. Reset: 0.

field.name: AddSubFLOPs
field.desc: Add/subtract FLOPs. Read-write. Reset: 0.

register.name: FpRetiredSerOps
register.desc: Retired Serializing Ops. Read-write. Reset: 00h. The number of serializing Ops retired.

field.name: SseBotRet
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. SSE/AVX bottom-executing ops retired.

field.name: SseCtrlRet
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. SSE/AVX control word mispredict traps.

field.name: X87BotRet
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. x87 bottom-executing ops retired.

field.name: X87CtrlRet
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. x87 control word mispredict traps due to mispredictions in RC or PC, or changes in Exception Mask bits.

register.name: FpDispFaults
register.desc: FP Dispatch Faults. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Floating-point Dispatch Faults.

field.name: YmmSpillFault
field.desc: YMM Spill fault. Read-write. Reset: 0.

field.name: YmmFillFault
field.desc: YMM Fill fault. Read-write. Reset: 0.

field.name: XmmFillFault
field.desc: XMM Fill fault. Read-write. Reset: 0.

field.name: x87FillFault
field.desc: X87 Fill fault. Read-write. Reset: 0.

register.name: LsBadStatus2
register.desc: Bad Status 2. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: StliOther
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Store-to-load conflicts: A load was unable to complete due to a non-forwardable conflict with an older store. Most commonly, a load's address range partially but not completely overlaps with an uncompleted older store. Software can avoid this problem by using same-size and same-alignment loads and stores when accessing the same data. Vector/SIMD code is particularly susceptible to this problem; software should construct wide vector stores by manipulating vector elements in registers using shuffle/blend/swap instructions prior to storing to memory, instead of using narrow element-by-element stores.

register.name: LsLocks
register.desc: Retired Lock Instructions. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: BusLock
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Comparable to legacy bus lock.

register.name: LsRetClClush
register.desc: Retired CLFLUSH Instructions. The number of retired CLFLUSH instructions. This is a non-speculative event.

register.name: LsRetCpuid
register.desc: Retired CPUID Instructions. The number of CPUID instructions retired.

register.name: LsDispatch
register.desc: LS Dispatch. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Counts the number of operations dispatched to the LS unit. Unit Masks events are ADDed.

field.name: LdStDispatch
field.desc: Load-op-Store Dispatch. Read-write. Reset: 0. Dispatch of a single op that performs a load from and store to the same memory address.

field.name: StoreDispatch
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Dispatch of a single op that performs a memory store.

field.name: LdDispatch
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Dispatch of a single op that performs a memory load.

register.name: LsSmiRx
register.desc: SMIs Received. Reset: 00h. Counts the number of SMIs received.

register.name: LsIntTaken
register.desc: Interrupts Taken. Reset: 00h. Counts the number of interrupts taken.

register.name: LsSTLF
register.desc: Store to Load Forward. Number of STLF hits.

register.name: LsStCommitCancel2
register.desc: Store Commit Cancels 2. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: StCommitCancelWcbFull
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A non-cacheable store and the non-cacheable commit buffer is full.

register.name: LsMabAlloc
register.desc: LS MAB Allocates by Type. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Counts when a LS pipe allocates a MAB entry.

field.name: LsMabAllocation
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. ValidValues: 3Eh-00h - Reserved. 3Fh - Load Store Allocations. 40h - Hardware Prefetcher Allocations. 7Eh-41h - Reserved. 7Fh - All Allocations.

register.name: LsDmndFillsFromSys
register.desc: Demand Data Cache Fills by Data Source. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Demand Data Cache Fills by Data Source.

field.name: MemIoRemote
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From DRAM or IO connected in different Node.

field.name: ExtCacheRemote
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From CCX Cache in different Node.

field.name: MemIoLocal
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From DRAM or IO connected in same node.

field.name: ExtCacheLocal
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From cache of different CCX in same node.

field.name: IntCache
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From L3 or different L2 in same CCX.

field.name: LclL2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From Local L2 to the core.

register.name: LsAnyFillsFromSys
register.desc: Any Data Cache Fills by Data Source. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Any Data Cache Fills by Data Source.

field.name: MemIoRemote
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From DRAM or IO connected in different Node.

field.name: ExtCacheRemote
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From CCX Cache in different Node.

field.name: MemIoLocal
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From DRAM or IO connected in same node.

field.name: ExtCacheLocal
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From cache of different CCX in same node.

field.name: IntCache
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From L3 or different L2 in same CCX.

field.name: LclL2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From Local L2 to the core.

register.name: LsL1DTlbMiss
register.desc: L1 DTLB Misses. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: TlbReload1GL2Miss
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. DTLB reload to a 1-G page that also missed in the L2 TLB.

field.name: TlbReload2ML2Miss
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. DTLB reload to a 2-M page that also missed in the L2 TLB.

field.name: TlbReloadCoalescedPageMiss
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. DTLB reload to a coalesced page that also missed in the L2 TLB.

field.name: TlbReload4KL2Miss
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. DTLB reload to a 4-K page that missed the L2 TLB.

field.name: TlbReload1GL2Hit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. DTLB reload to a 1-G page that hit in the L2 TLB.

field.name: TlbReload2ML2Hit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. DTLB reload to a 2-M page that hit in the L2 TLB.

field.name: TlbReloadCoalescedPageHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. DTLB reload to a coalesced page that hit in the L2 TLB.

field.name: TlbReload4KL2Hit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. DTLB reload to a 4-K page that hit in the L2 TLB.

register.name: LsMisalLoads
register.desc: Misaligned loads. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: MA4K
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. The number of 4-KB misaligned (i.e., page crossing) loads.

field.name: MA64
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. The number of 64-B misaligned (i.e., cacheline crossing) loads.

register.name: LsPrefInstrDisp
register.desc: Prefetch Instructions Dispatched. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Software Prefetch Instructions Dispatched (Speculative).

field.name: PREFETCHNTA
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. PrefetchNTA instruction. See docAPM3 PREFETCHlevel.

field.name: PREFETCHW
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. PrefetchW instruction. See docAPM3 PREFETCHW.

field.name: PREFETCH
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. PrefetchT0, T1 and T2 instructions. See docAPM3 PREFETCHlevel.

register.name: LsInefSwPref
register.desc: Ineffective Software Prefetches. Read-write. Reset: 00h. The number of software prefetches that did not fetch data outside of the processor core.

field.name: MabMchCnt
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Software PREFETCH instruction saw a match on an already-allocated miss request buffer.

field.name: DataPipeSwPfDcHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Software PREFETCH instruction saw a DC hit.

register.name: LsSwPfDcFills
register.desc: Software Prefetch Data Cache Fills. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Software Prefetch Data Cache Fills by Data Source.

field.name: MemIoRemote
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From DRAM or IO connected in different Node.

field.name: ExtCacheRemote
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From CCX Cache in different Node.

field.name: MemIoLocal
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From DRAM or IO connected in same node.

field.name: ExtCacheLocal
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From cache of different CCX in same node.

field.name: IntCache
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From L3 or different L2 in same CCX.

field.name: LclL2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From Local L2 to the core.

register.name: LsHwPfDcFills
register.desc: Hardware Prefetch Data Cache Fills. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Hardware Prefetch Data Cache Fills by Data Source.

field.name: MemIoRemote
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From DRAM or IO connected in different Node.

field.name: ExtCacheRemote
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From CCX Cache in different Node.

field.name: MemIoLocal
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From DRAM or IO connected in same node.

field.name: ExtCacheLocal
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From cache of different CCX in same node.

field.name: IntCache
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From L3 or different L2 in same CCX.

field.name: LclL2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. From Local L2 to the core.

register.name: LsAllocMabCount
register.desc: Count of Allocated Mabs. This event counts the in-flight L1 data cache misses (allocated Miss Address Buffers) divided by 4 and rounded down each cycle unless used with the MergeEvent functionality. If the MergeEvent is used, it counts the exact number of outstanding L1 data cache misses. See 2.1.17.3 [Large Increment per Cycle Events].

register.name: LsNotHaltedCyc
register.desc: Cycles not in Halt.

register.name: LsTlbFlush
register.desc: All TLB Flushes. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Requires unit mask 0xFF to engage event for counting.

field.name: All
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. ValidValues: FEh-00h - Reserved. FFh - All TLB Flushes.

register.name: IcCacheFillL2
register.desc: Instruction Cache Refills from L2. The number of 64-byte instruction cache lines fulfilled from the L2 cache.

register.name: IcCacheFillSys
register.desc: Instruction Cache Refills from System. The number of 64-byte instruction cache line fulfilled from system memory or another cache.

register.name: BpL1TlbMissL2TlbHit
register.desc: L1 ITLB Miss, L2 ITLB Hit. The number of instruction fetches that miss in the L1 ITLB but hit in the L2 ITLB.

register.name: BpL1TlbMissL2TlbMiss
register.desc: ITLB Reload from Page-Table walk. Read-write. Reset: 00h. The number of valid fills into the ITLB originating from the LS Page-Table Walker. Tablewalk requests are issued for L1-ITLB and L2-ITLB misses.

field.name: Coalesced4K
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Walk for >4-K Coalesced page.

field.name: IF1G
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Walk for 1-G page.

field.name: IF2M
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Walk for 2-M page.

field.name: IF4K
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Walk to 4-K page.

register.name: BpL2BTBCorrect
register.desc: L2 Branch Prediction Overrides Existing Prediction (speculative).

register.name: BpDynIndPred
register.desc: Dynamic Indirect Predictions. The number of times a branch used the indirect predictor to make a prediction.

register.name: BpDeReDirect
register.desc: Decode Redirects. Reset: 00h. The number of times the instruction decoder overrides the predicted target.

register.name: BpL1TlbFetchHit
register.desc: L1 TLB Hits for Instruction Fetch. Read-write. Reset: 00h. The number of instruction fetches that hit in the L1 ITLB.

field.name: IF1G
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L1 Instruction TLB hit (1-G page size).

field.name: IF2M
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L1 Instruction TLB hit (2-M page size).

field.name: IF4K
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L1 Instruction TLB hit (4-K or 16-K page size).

register.name: IcTagHitMiss
register.desc: IC Tag Hit/Miss Events. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Counts various IC tag related hit and miss events.

field.name: IcAccessTypes
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Instruction Cache accesses. ValidValues: 06h-00h - Reserved. 07h - Instruction Cache Hit. 17h-08h - Reserved. 18h - Instruction Cache Miss. 1Eh-19h - Reserved. 1Fh - All Instruction Cache Accesses.

register.name: OpCacheHitMiss
register.desc: Op Cache Hit/Miss. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Counts Op Cache micro-tag hit/miss events.

field.name: OpCacheAccesses
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. ValidValues: 2h-0h - Reserved. 3h - Op Cache Hit. 4h - Op Cache Miss. 6h-5h - Reserved. 7h - All Op Cache accesses.

register.name: DeOpQueueEmpty
register.desc: Op Queue Empty. Reset: 00h. Cycles where the Op Queue is empty.

register.name: DeSrcOpDisp
register.desc: Source of Op Dispatched From Decoder. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Counts the number of ops dispatched from the decoder classified by op source. See docRevG erratum #1287.

field.name: OpCache
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Count of ops fetched from Op Cache and dispatched.

field.name: x86Decoder
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Count of ops fetched from Instruction Cache and dispatched.

register.name: DeDisOpsFromDecoder
register.desc: Types of Ops Dispatched From Decoder. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Counts the number of ops dispatched from the decoder classified by op type. The UnitMask value encodes which types of ops are counted.

field.name: OpCountingMode
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. 0=Count aligns with IBS count. 1=Count aligns with retire count (PMCx0C1).

field.name: DispOpType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. ValidValues: 03h-00h - Reserved. 04h - Any FP dispatch. 07h-05h - Reserved. 08h - Any Integer dispatch. 1Fh-09h - Reserved.

register.name: DeDisDispatchTokenStalls1
register.desc: Dispatch Resource Stall Cycles 1. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Cycles where a dispatch group is valid but does not get dispatched due to a Token Stall. Also counts cycles when the thread is not selected to dispatch but would have been stalled due to a Token Stall.

field.name: FpFlushRecoveryStall
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. FP Flush recovery stall.

field.name: FPSchRsrcStall
field.desc: FP scheduler resource stall. Read-write. Reset: 0. Applies to ops that use the FP scheduler.

field.name: FpRegFileRsrcStall
field.desc: Floating-point register file resource stall. Read-write. Reset: 0. Applies to all FP ops that have a destination register.

field.name: TakenBrnchBufferRsrc
field.desc: Taken branch buffer resource stall. Read-write. Reset: 0.

field.name: StoreQueueRsrcStall
field.desc: Store Queue resource stall. Read-write. Reset: 0. Applies to all ops with store semantics.

field.name: LoadQueueRsrcStall
field.desc: Load Queue resource stall. Read-write. Reset: 0. Applies to all ops with load semantics.

field.name: IntPhyRegFileRsrcStall
field.desc: Integer Physical Register File resource stall. Read-write. Reset: 0. Integer Physical Register File, applies to all ops that have an integer destination register.

register.name: DeDisDispatchTokenStalls2
register.desc: Dynamic Tokens Dispatch Stall Cycles 2. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Cycles where a dispatch group is valid but does not get dispatched due to a token stall.

field.name: RetireTokenStall
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Insufficient Retire Queue tokens available.

field.name: IntSch3TokenStall
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. No tokens for Integer Scheduler Queue 3 available.

field.name: IntSch2TokenStall
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. No tokens for Integer Scheduler Queue 2 available.

field.name: IntSch1TokenStall
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. No tokens for Integer Scheduler Queue 1 available.

field.name: IntSch0TokenStall
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. No tokens for Integer Scheduler Queue 0 available.

register.name: ExRetInstr
register.desc: Retired Instructions. The number of instructions retired.

register.name: ExRetOps
register.desc: Retired Ops. The number of macro-ops retired.

register.name: ExRetBrn
register.desc: Retired Branch Instructions. The number of branch instructions retired. This includes all types of architectural control flow changes, including exceptions and interrupts.

register.name: ExRetBrnMisp
register.desc: Retired Branch Instructions Mispredicted. The number of retired branch instructions, that were mispredicted.

register.name: ExRetBrnTkn
register.desc: Retired Taken Branch Instructions. The number of taken branches that were retired. This includes all types of architectural control flow changes, including exceptions and interrupts.

register.name: ExRetBrnTknMisp
register.desc: Retired Taken Branch Instructions Mispredicted. The number of retired taken branch instructions that were mispredicted.

register.name: ExRetBrnFar
register.desc: Retired Far Control Transfers. The number of far control transfers retired including far call/jump/return, IRET, SYSCALL and SYSRET, plus exceptions and interrupts. Far control transfers are not subject to branch prediction.

register.name: ExRetNearRet
register.desc: Retired Near Returns. The number of near return instructions (RET or RET Iw) retired.

register.name: ExRetNearRetMispred
register.desc: Retired Near Returns Mispredicted. The number of near returns retired that were not correctly predicted by the return address predictor. Each such mispredict incurs the same penalty as a mispredicted conditional branch instruction.

register.name: ExRetBrnIndMisp
register.desc: Retired Indirect Branch Instructions Mispredicted. The number of indirect branches retired that were not correctly predicted. Each such mispredict incurs the same penalty as a mispredicted conditional branch instruction. Note that only EX mispredicts are counted.

register.name: ExRetMmxFpInstr
register.desc: Retired MMX/FP Instructions. Read-write. Reset: 00h. The number of MMX, SSE or x87 instructions retired. The UnitMask allows the selection of the individual classes of instructions as given in the table. Each increment represents one complete instruction. Since this event includes non-numeric instructions it is not suitable for measuring MFLOPs.

field.name: SseInstr
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. SSE instructions (SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSSE3, SSE4A, SSE41, SSE42, AVX).

field.name: MmxInstr
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. MMX instructions.

field.name: X87Instr
field.desc: X87 instructions. Read-write. Reset: 0.

register.name: ExRetIndBrchInstr
register.desc: Retired Indirect Branch Instructions. The number of indirect branches retired.

register.name: ExRetCond
register.desc: Retired Conditional Branch Instructions.

register.name: ExDivBusy
register.desc: Div Cycles Busy count.

register.name: ExDivCount
register.desc: Div Op Count.

register.name: ExRetMsprdBrnchInstrDirMsmtch
register.desc: Retired Mispredicted Branch Instructions due to Direction Mismatch. The number of retired conditional branch instructions that were not correctly predicted because of a branch direction mismatch.

register.name: ExTaggedIbsOps
register.desc: Tagged IBS Ops. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Counts Op IBS related events.

field.name: IbsCountRollover
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Number of times an op could not be tagged by IBS because of a previous tagged op that has not retired.

field.name: IbsTaggedOpsRet
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Number of Ops tagged by IBS that retired.

field.name: IbsTaggedOps
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Number of Ops tagged by IBS.

register.name: ExRetFusedInstr
register.desc: Retired Fused Instructions. Reset: 00h. Counts retired fused instructions.

register.name: L2RequestG1
register.desc: Requests to L2 Group1. Read-write. Reset: 00h. All L2 Cache Requests (Breakdown 1 - Common).

field.name: RdBlkL
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Data Cache Reads (including hardware and software prefetch).

field.name: RdBlkX
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Data Cache Stores.

field.name: LsRdBlkC_S
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Data Cache Shared Reads.

field.name: CacheableIcRead
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Instruction Cache Reads.

field.name: ChangeToX
field.desc: Data Cache State Change Requests. Read-write. Reset: 0. Request change to writable, check L2 for current state.

field.name: PrefetchL2Cmd
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0.

field.name: L2HwPf
field.desc: L2 Prefetcher. Read-write. Reset: 0. All prefetches accepted by L2 pipeline, hit or miss. Types of PF and L2 hit/miss broken out in a separate perfmon event.

register.name: L2CacheReqStat
register.desc: Core to L2 Cacheable Request Access Status. Read-write. Reset: 00h. L2 Cache Request Outcomes (not including L2 Prefetch).

field.name: LsRdBlkCS
field.desc: Data Cache Shared Read Hit in L2. Read-write. Reset: 0.

field.name: LsRdBlkLHitX
field.desc: Data Cache Read Hit in L2. Read-write. Reset: 0. Modifiable.

field.name: LsRdBlkLHitS
field.desc: Data Cache Read Hit Non-Modifiable Line in L2. Read-write. Reset: 0.

field.name: LsRdBlkX
field.desc: Data Cache Store or State Change Hit in L2. Read-write. Reset: 0.

field.name: LsRdBlkC
field.desc: Data Cache Req Miss in L2 (all types). Read-write. Reset: 0.

field.name: IcFillHitX
field.desc: Instruction Cache Hit Modifiable Line in L2. Read-write. Reset: 0.

field.name: IcFillHitS
field.desc: Instruction Cache Hit Non-Modifiable Line in L2. Read-write. Reset: 0.

field.name: IcFillMiss
field.desc: Instruction Cache Req Miss in L2. Read-write. Reset: 0.

register.name: L2PfHitL2
register.desc: L2 Prefetch Hit in L2. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: L2Prefetches
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. ValidValues: 1Eh-00h - Reserved. 1Fh - Counts requests generated from L2 Hardware Prefetchers. DFh-20h - Reserved. E0h - Counts requests generated from L1 Hardware Prefetchers. FFh-E1h - Reserved.

register.name: L2PfMissL2HitL3
register.desc: L2 Prefetcher Hits in L3. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Counts all L2 prefetches accepted by the L2 pipeline which miss the L2 cache and hit the L3.

field.name: L2PrefetchesMissL2HitL3
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. ValidValues: 1Eh-00h - Reserved. 1Fh - Counts requests generated from L2 Hardware Prefetchers. DFh-20h - Reserved. E0h - Counts requests generated from L1 Hardware Prefetchers. FFh-E1h - Reserved.

register.name: L2PfMissL2L3
register.desc: L2 Prefetcher Misses in L3. Read-write. Reset: 00h. Counts all L2 prefetches accepted by the L2 pipeline which miss the L2 and the L3 caches.

field.name: L2PrefetchesMissL2L3
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. ValidValues: 1Eh-00h - Reserved. 1Fh - Counts requests generated from L2 Hardware Prefetchers. DFh-20h - Reserved. E0h - Counts requests generated from L1 Hardware Prefetchers. FFh-E1h - Reserved.

register.name: L3LookupState
register.desc: All L3 Cache Requests. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: AllL3ReqTyps
field.desc: All L3 Request Types. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

register.name: XiSysFillLatency
register.desc: L3 Cache Miss Latency. Ignores SliceID, EnAllSlices, CoreID, EnAllCores and ThreadMask Each cycle, this event increments by the total number of read requests outstanding from the CCX divided by XiSysFillLatencyDivider. The user can calculate the average system fill latency in cycles by multiplying by XiSysFillLatencyDivider and dividing by the total number of fill requests over the same period (counted by event 0x9A UserMask 0x1F). XiSysFillLatencyDivider is 16 for this product, but may change for future products.

register.name: XiCcxSdpReq1
register.desc: L3 Misses by Request Type. Reset: 00h. Ignores SliceID, EnAllSlices, CoreID, EnAllCores and ThreadMask. Requires unit mask 0xFF to engage event for counting.

register.name: MCA_CTL_LS
register.desc: LS Machine Check Control Thread 0. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. 0=Disables error reporting for the corresponding error. 1=Enables error reporting via machine check exception for the corresponding error. The MCA::LS::MCA_CTL_LS register must be enabled by the corresponding enable bit in Core::X86::Msr::MCG_CTL. Does not affect error detection, correction, or logging.

field.name: STORE_DATA_OTHER
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Store-to-Load Forwarding (STLF) data entry. The error was detected on a store or on a load which consumes STLF data.

field.name: HWA
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A Hardware Assertion (HWA) error was reported.

field.name: SystemReadDataErrorWcb
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A SystemReadDataError error was reported for read data originating from outside the core. The error was detected by a Write Coalescing Buffer (WCB) read-modify-write.

field.name: SystemReadDataErrorScb
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A SystemReadDataError error was reported for read data originating from outside the core. The error was detected by a Store Coalescing Buffer (SCB) read-modify-write.

field.name: SystemReadDataErrorLoad
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A SystemReadDataError error was reported for read data originating from outside the core. The error was detected by a load.

field.name: SCB_POISON
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A poisoned line was detected in a Store Coalescing Buffer (SCB) entry.

field.name: WCB
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Write Coalescing buffer (WCB) entry.

field.name: SCB_DATA
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in the Data field of a Store Coalescing Buffer (SCB) entry.

field.name: SCB_ADDR
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in the Address field of a Store Coalescing Buffer (SCB) entry.

field.name: SCB_STATE
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in the State field of a Store Coalescing Buffer (SCB) entry.

field.name: MAB
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Miss Address Buffer (MAB) entry.

field.name: LDQ
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Load Queue (LDQ) entry.

field.name: STQ
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in an Store Queue (STQ) entry.

field.name: PWC
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Page Walk Cache (PWC) entry.

field.name: L2DTLB
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Level 2 Translation Lookaside Buffer (L2 TLB) entry.

field.name: L1DTLB
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Level 1 Translation Lookaside Buffer (L1 TLB) entry.

field.name: DC_DATA_RMW_2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache data array. This error was detected on a read-modify-write by a store.

field.name: DC_DATA_LOAD_2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache data array. This error was detected on a load.

field.name: DC_TAG_STORE
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache tag array, or a mismatch was detected in the data cache tag meta-data poison bit. The error was detected on a tag read by a store.

field.name: DC_TAG_LOAD
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache tag array, or a mismatch was detected in the data cache tag meta-data poison bit. The error was detected on a tag read by a load.

field.name: DC_TAG_VICTIM
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache tag array, or a mismatch was detected in the data cache tag meta-data poison bit. The error was detected on a tag read by a probe or victimization.

field.name: DC_DATA_RMW
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache data array. This error was detected on a data cache read-modify-write by a store.

field.name: DC_DATA_LOAD
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error or poison consumption was detected on a data cache read by a load. MCA_SYND_LS[0]=0 for a data cache ECC error, and MCA_SYND_LS[0]=1 for poison data originating from outside the core.

field.name: DC_DATA_VICTIM
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache data array. The error was detected on a data cache read by a probe or victimization of a dirty line.

register.name: MCA_STATUS_LS
register.desc: LS Machine Check Status Thread 0. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. Logs information associated with errors.

field.name: Val
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=A valid error has been detected. This bit should be cleared by software after the register has been read. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Overflow
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=An error was detected while the valid bit (Val) was set; at least one error was not logged. Overflow is set independently of whether the existing error is overwritten. See 3.1.3 [Machine Check Errors]. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: UC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was not corrected by hardware. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: En
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=MCA error reporting is enabled for this error, as indicated by the corresponding bit in MCA::LS::MCA_CTL_LS. This bit is a copy of bit in MCA::LS::MCA_CTL_LS for this error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: MiscV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Valid thresholding in MCA::LS::MCA_MISC0_LS. In certain modes, MISC registers are owned by platform firmware and will RAZ when read by non-SMM code. Therefore, it is possible for MiscV=1 and the MISC register to read as all zeros. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: AddrV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS contains address information associated with the error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: PCC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Hardware context held by the processor may have been corrupted. Continued operation of the system may have unpredictable results. The error is not recoverable or survivable, and the system should be reinitialized. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrCoreIdVal
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The ErrCoreId field is valid. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: TCC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Hardware context of the process thread to which the error was reported may have been corrupted. Continued operation of the thread may have unpredictable results. The thread must be terminated. Only meaningful when MCA::LS::MCA_STATUS_LS[PCC]=0. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV54
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bit. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: SyndV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=This error logged information in MCA::LS::MCA_SYND_LS. If MCA::LS::MCA_SYND_LS[ErrorPriority] is the same as the priority of the error in MCA::LS::MCA_STATUS_LS, then the information in MCA::LS::MCA_SYND_LS is associated with the error in MCA::LS::MCA_STATUS_LS. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV47
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: CECC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was a correctable ECC error according to the restrictions of the ECC algorithm. UC indicates whether the error was actually corrected by the processor. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: UECC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was an uncorrectable ECC error according to the restrictions of the ECC algorithm. UC indicates whether the error was actually corrected by the processor. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Deferred
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=A deferred error was created. A deferred error is the result of an uncorrectable data error which did not immediately cause a processor exception; an exception is deferred until the erroneous data is consumed. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Poison
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was the result of attempting to consume poisoned data. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV41
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Scrub
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was the result of a scrub operation. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV38
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrCoreId
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. When ErrCoreIdVal=1 this field indicates which core within the processor is associated with the error; Otherwise this field is reserved. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV30
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: AddrLsb
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. Specifies the least significant valid bit of the address contained in MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS[ErrorAddr]. A value of 0 indicates that MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS[55:0] contains a valid byte address. A value of 6 indicates that MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS[55:6] contains a valid cache line address and that MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS[5:0] are not part of the address and should be ignored by error handling software. A value of 12 indicates that MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS[55:12] contain a valid 4KB memory page and that MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS[11:0] should be ignored by error handling software. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV22
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrorCodeExt
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. Extended Error Code. This field is used to identify the error type for root cause analysis. This field indicates which bit position in MCA::LS::MCA_CTL_LS enables error reporting for the logged error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrorCode
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0000h. Error code for this error. See 3.1.3.3 [Error Codes] for details on decoding this field. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

register.name: MCA_ADDR_LS
register.desc: LS Machine Check Address Thread 0. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS stores an address and other information associated with the error in MCA::LS::MCA_STATUS_LS. The register is only meaningful if MCA::LS::MCA_STATUS_LS[Val]=1 and MCA::LS::MCA_STATUS_LS[AddrV]=1.

field.name: ErrorAddr
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,000_0000_0000_0000h. Unless otherwise specified by an error, contains the address associated with the error logged in MCA::LS::MCA_STATUS_LS. For physical addresses, the most significant bit is given by Core::X86::Cpuid::LongModeInfo [PhysAddrSize].

register.name: MCA_MISC0_LS
register.desc: LS Machine Check Miscellaneous 0 Thread 0. Log miscellaneous information associated with errors.

field.name: Valid
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=A valid CntP field is present in this register. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: CntP
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=A valid threshold counter is present. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: Locked
field.desc: Reset: 0. 1=Writes to this register are ignored. This bit is set by BIOS to indicate that this register is not available for OS use. BIOS should set this bit if ThresholdIntType is set to SMI. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: IntP
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=ThresholdIntType can be used to generate interrupts. 0=ThresholdIntType and interrupt generation are not supported. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::LS::MCA_MISC0_LS[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: LvtOffset
field.desc: Reset: 0h. One per die. For error thresholding interrupts, specifies the address of the LVT entry in the APIC registers as follows: LVT address = (LvtOffset shifted left 4 bits) + 500h (see Core::X86::Apic::ExtendedInterruptLvtEntries). AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::LS::MCA_MISC0_LS[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: CntEn
field.desc: Reset: 0. 1=Count thresholding errors. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::LS::MCA_MISC0_LS[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: ThresholdIntType
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. Specifies the type of interrupt signaled when Ovrflw is set and IntP==1. 00b = No interrupt. 01b = APIC based interrupt (see Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg [ThresholdLvtOffset]). 10b = SMI trigger event. 11b = Reserved. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::LS::MCA_MISC0_LS[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: Ovrflw
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. Set by hardware when ErrCnt transitions from FFEh to FFFh. When this field is set, ErrCnt no longer increments. When this bit is set, the interrupt selected by the ThresholdIntType field is generated. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::LS::MCA_MISC0_LS[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: ErrCnt
field.desc: Reset: Cold,000h. This is written by software to set the starting value of the error counter. This is incremented by hardware when errors are logged. When this counter overflows, it stays at FFFh (no rollover). The threshold value, written by software, is (FFFh - the desired error count (the number of errors necessary in order for an interrupt to be taken)); the desired error count of 0 (a write value of FFFh) is not supported. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::LS::MCA_MISC0_LS[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: BlkPtr
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. 00h=Extended MISC MSR block is not valid. 01h=Extended MSR block is valid.

register.name: MCA_CONFIG_LS
register.desc: LS Machine Check Configuration Thread 0. Reset: 0000_0002_0000_0125h. Controls configuration of the associated machine check bank.

field.name: DeferredIntType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Specifies the type of interrupt signaled when a deferred error is logged. 00b = No interrupt. 01b = APIC based interrupt (see Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg [DeferredLvtOffset]). 10b = SMI trigger event. 11b = Reserved.

field.name: LogDeferredInMcaStat
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Init: BIOS,1. 1=Log deferred errors in MCA::LS::MCA_STATUS_LS and MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS in addition to MCA::LS::MCA_DESTAT_LS and MCA::LS::MCA_DEADDR_LS. 0=Only log deferred errors in MCA::LS::MCA_DESTAT_LS and MCA::LS::MCA_DEADDR_LS. This bit does not affect logging of deferred errors in MCA::LS::MCA_SYND_LS, MCA::LS::MCA_MISC0_LS.

field.name: McaXEnable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Init: BIOS,1. Check: 1. 1=Software has acknowledged support for the MCAX feature set. 0=Software has not acknowledged support for the MCAX feature set. All uncorrected and fatal errors will cause an ErrorEvent packet to be generated. Deferred error interrupts are configured via Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg.

field.name: McaLsbInStatusSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=MCA::LS::MCA_CONFIG_LS[McaLsbInStatusSupported] indicates that AddrLbc is located in McaStatus registers.

field.name: DeferredIntTypeSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=MCA::LS::MCA_CONFIG_LS[DeferredIntType] controls the type of interrupt generated on a deferred error. Deferred errors are supported in this bank only if MCA::LS::MCA_CONFIG_LS[DeferredErrorLoggingSupported]=1.

field.name: DeferredErrorLoggingSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=Deferred errors are supported in this MCA bank, and MCA::LS::MCA_CONFIG_LS[LogDeferredInMcaStat] controls the logging behavior of these errors. MCA::LS::MCA_DESTAT_LS and MCA::LS::MCA_DEADDR_LS are supported in this MCA bank. 0=Deferred errors are not supported in this bank.

field.name: McaX
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=This bank provides Machine Check Architecture Extensions. Up to 4 additional MISC registers (MISC1-MISC4) are supported. MCA::LS::MCA_MISC0_LS[BlkPtr] indicates the presence of the additional MISC registers, but is not used to determine their MSR numbers. Deferred error interrupt type is specifiable by MCA bank. MCA::LS::MCA_STATUS_LS[TCC] is present.

register.name: MCA_IPID_LS
register.desc: LS IP Identification Thread 0. Reset: 0010_00B0_0000_0000h. The MCA::LS::MCA_IPID_LS register is used by software to determine what IP type and revision is associated with the MCA bank.

field.name: McaType
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0010h. The McaType of the MCA bank within this IP.

field.name: InstanceIdHi
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. The HI value instance ID of this IP. This is initialized to a unique ID per instance of this register.

field.name: HardwareID
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0B0h. The Hardware ID of the IP associated with this MCA bank.

field.name: InstanceId
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. The instance ID of this IP. This is initialized to a unique ID per instance of this register.

register.name: MCA_SYND_LS
register.desc: LS Machine Check Syndrome Thread 0. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. Logs physical location information associated with error in MCA::LS::MCA_STATUS_LS Thread 0.

field.name: Syndrome
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,00h. Contains the syndrome, if any, associated with the error logged in MCA::LS::MCA_STATUS_LS. The low-order bit of the syndrome is stored in bit 0, and the syndrome has a length specified by MCA::LS::MCA_SYND_LS[Length]. The Syndrome field is only valid when MCA::LS::MCA_SYND_LS[Length] is not 0.

field.name: ErrorPriority
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0h. Encodes the priority of the error logged in MCA::LS::MCA_SYND_LS. 3'b000 = No error; 3'b001 = Reserved; 3'b010 = Corrected Error; 3'b011 = Deferred Error; 3'b100 = Uncorrected Error; 3'b101 = Fatal Error; all others reserved.

field.name: Length
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,00h. Specifies the length in bits of the syndrome contained in MCA::LS::MCA_SYND_LS[Syndrome]. A value of 0 indicates that there is no valid syndrome in MCA::LS::MCA_SYND_LS. For example, a syndrome length of 9 means that MCA::LS::MCA_SYND_LS[Syndrome] bits [8:0] contains a valid syndrome.

field.name: ErrorInformation
