field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0_0000h. Contains error-specific information about the location of the error. Decoding is available in Table 45 [MCA_SYND_LS].

register.name: MCA_DESTAT_LS
register.desc: LS Machine Check Deferred Error Status Thread 0. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. Holds status information for the first deferred error seen in this bank.

field.name: Val
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0. 1=A valid error has been detected (whether it is enabled or not).

field.name: Overflow
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0. 1=An error was detected while the valid bit (Val) was set; at least one error was not logged. Overflow is set independently of whether the existing error is overwritten. (See the section on overwrite priorities.).

field.name: RESERV4
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Cold,0h.

field.name: AddrV
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0. 1=MCA::LS::MCA_DEADDR_LS contains address information associated with the error.

field.name: RESERV3
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Cold,0h.

field.name: SyndV
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0. 1=This error logged information in MCA::LS::MCA_SYND_LS. If MCA::LS::MCA_SYND_LS[ErrorPriority] is the same as the priority of the error in MCA::LS::MCA_STATUS_LS, then the information in MCA::LS::MCA_SYND_LS is associated with the error in MCA::LS::MCA_DESTAT_LS.

field.name: RESERV2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Cold,00h.

field.name: Deferred
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0. 1=A deferred error was created. A deferred error is the result of an uncorrectable data error which did not immediately cause a processor exception; poison is created and an exception is deferred until the poison data is consumed.

field.name: RESERV1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Cold,0000h.

field.name: AddrLsb
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,00h. Specifies the least significant valid bit of the address contained in MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS[ErrorAddr]. A value of 0 indicates that MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS[55:0] contains a valid byte address. A value of 6 indicates that MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS[55:6] contains a valid cache line address and that MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS[5:0] are not part of the address and should be ignored by error handling software. A value of 12 indicates that MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS[55:12] contain a valid 4KB memory page and that MCA::LS::MCA_ADDR_LS[11:0] should be ignored by error handling software.

field.name: RESERV0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Cold,0h.

field.name: ErrorCodeExt
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,00h. Logs an extended error code when an error is detected. This model-specific field is used in conjunction with ErrorCode? to identify the error sub-type for root cause analysis.

field.name: ErrorCode
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0000h. Error code for this error.

register.name: MCA_DEADDR_LS
register.desc: LS Deferred Error Address Thread 0. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. The MCA::LS::MCA_DEADDR_LS register stores the address associated with the error in MCA::LS::MCA_DESTAT_LS. The register is only meaningful if MCA::LS::MCA_DESTAT_LS[Val]=1 and MCA::LS::MCA_DESTAT_LS[AddrV]=1. The lowest valid bit of the address is defined by MCA::LS::MCA_DESTAT_LS[AddrLsb].

field.name: ErrorAddr
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,000_0000_0000_0000h. Contains the address, if any, associated with the error logged in MCA::LS::MCA_DESTAT_LS. The lowest-order valid bit of the address is specified in MCA::LS::MCA_DESTAT_LS[AddrLsb].

register.name: MCA_CTL_MASK_LS
register.desc: LS Machine Check Control Mask Thread 0. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Inhibit detection of an error source.

field.name: STORE_DATA_OTHER
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Store-to-Load Forwarding (STLF) data entry. The error was detected on a store or on a load which consumes STLF data.

field.name: HWA
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A Hardware Assertion (HWA) error was reported.

field.name: SystemReadDataErrorWcb
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Init: BIOS,1. A SystemReadDataError error was reported for read data originating from outside the core. The error was detected by a Write Coalescing Buffer (WCB) read-modify-write.

field.name: SystemReadDataErrorScb
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Init: BIOS,1. A SystemReadDataError error was reported for read data originating from outside the core. The error was detected by a Store Coalescing Buffer (SCB) read-modify-write.

field.name: SystemReadDataErrorLoad
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Init: BIOS,1. A SystemReadDataError error was reported for read data originating from outside the core. The error was detected by a load.

field.name: SCB_POISON
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A poisoned line was detected in a Store Coalescing Buffer (SCB) entry.

field.name: WCB
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Write Coalescing buffer (WCB) entry.

field.name: SCB_DATA
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in the Data field of a Store Coalescing Buffer (SCB) entry.

field.name: SCB_ADDR
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in the Address field of a Store Coalescing Buffer (SCB) entry.

field.name: SCB_STATE
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in the State field of a Store Coalescing Buffer (SCB) entry.

field.name: MAB
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Miss Address Buffer (MAB) entry.

field.name: LDQ
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Load Queue (LDQ) entry.

field.name: STQ
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in an Store Queue (STQ) entry.

field.name: PWC
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Page Walk Cache (PWC) entry.

field.name: L2DTLB
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Level 2 Translation Lookaside Buffer (L2 TLB) entry.

field.name: L1DTLB
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. A parity error was detected in a Level 1 Translation Lookaside Buffer (L1 TLB) entry.

field.name: DC_DATA_RMW_2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache data array. This error was detected on a read-modify-write by a store.

field.name: DC_DATA_LOAD_2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache data array. This error was detected on a load.

field.name: DC_TAG_STORE
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache tag array, or a mismatch was detected in the data cache tag meta-data poison bit. The error was detected on a tag read by a store.

field.name: DC_TAG_LOAD
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache tag array, or a mismatch was detected in the data cache tag meta-data poison bit. The error was detected on a tag read by a load.

field.name: DC_TAG_VICTIM
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache tag array, or a mismatch was detected in the data cache tag meta-data poison bit. The error was detected on a tag read by a probe or victimization.

field.name: DC_DATA_RMW
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache data array. This error was detected on a data cache read-modify-write by a store.

field.name: DC_DATA_LOAD
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error or poison consumption was detected on a data cache read by a load. MCA_SYND_LS[0]=0 for a data cache ECC error, and MCA_SYND_LS[0]=1 for poison data originating from outside the core.

field.name: DC_DATA_VICTIM
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. An ECC error was detected in the data cache data array. The error was detected on a data cache read by a probe or victimization of a dirty line.

register.name: MCA_CTL_IF
register.desc: IF Machine Check Control Thread 0. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. 0=Disables error reporting for the corresponding error. 1=Enables error reporting via machine check exception for the corresponding error. The MCA::IF::MCA_CTL_IF register must be enabled by the corresponding enable bit in Core::X86::Msr::MCG_CTL. Does not affect error detection, correction, or logging.

field.name: CtMceError
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. CT MCE.

field.name: BsrParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. BSR Parity Error.

field.name: L2TlbMultiHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L2-TLB Multi-Hit.

field.name: L1TlbMultiHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L1-TLB Multi-Hit.

field.name: HwAssert
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Hardware Assertion Error.

field.name: SystemReadDataError
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L2 Cache Error Response.

field.name: L2RespPoison
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L2 Cache Response Poison Error. Error is the result of consuming poison data.

field.name: L2BtbMultiHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. BP L2-BTB Multi-Hit Error.

field.name: L1BtbMultiHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. BP L1-BTB Multi-Hit Error.

field.name: IcUtagParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Ic MicroTag Parity Error.

field.name: RSVD_8
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Reserved. Will never trigger.

field.name: L2ItlbParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L2-TLB Parity Error.

field.name: L1ItlbParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L1-TLB Parity Error.

field.name: RSVD_5
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Reserved. Will never trigger.

field.name: DqParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. PRQ Parity Error.

field.name: DataParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. IC Data Array Parity Error.

field.name: TagParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. IC Full Tag Parity Error.

field.name: TagMultiHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. IC Full Tag Multi-hit Error.

field.name: OcUtagParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Op Cache Microtag Parity Error. Parity errors on PA and other relevant uTag fields are reported, independent of any utag probing. The parity error way and index are logged.

register.name: MCA_STATUS_IF
register.desc: IF Machine Check Status Thread 0. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. Logs information associated with errors.

field.name: Val
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=A valid error has been detected. This bit should be cleared by software after the register has been read. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Overflow
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=An error was detected while the valid bit (Val) was set; at least one error was not logged. Overflow is set independently of whether the existing error is overwritten. See 3.1.3 [Machine Check Errors]. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: UC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was not corrected by hardware. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: En
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=MCA error reporting is enabled for this error, as indicated by the corresponding bit in MCA::IF::MCA_CTL_IF. This bit is a copy of bit in MCA::IF::MCA_CTL_IF for this error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: MiscV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Valid thresholding in MCA::IF::MCA_MISC0_IF. In certain modes, MISC registers are owned by platform firmware and will RAZ when read by non-SMM code. Therefore, it is possible for MiscV=1 and the MISC register to read as all zeros. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: AddrV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=MCA::IF::MCA_ADDR_IF contains address information associated with the error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: PCC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Hardware context held by the processor may have been corrupted. Continued operation of the system may have unpredictable results. The error is not recoverable or survivable, and the system should be reinitialized. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrCoreIdVal
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The ErrCoreId field is valid. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: TCC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Hardware context of the process thread to which the error was reported may have been corrupted. Continued operation of the thread may have unpredictable results. The thread must be terminated. Only meaningful when MCA::IF::MCA_STATUS_IF[PCC]=0. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV54
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bit. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: SyndV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=This error logged information in MCA::IF::MCA_SYND_IF. If MCA::IF::MCA_SYND_IF[ErrorPriority] is the same as the priority of the error in MCA::IF::MCA_STATUS_IF, then the information in MCA::IF::MCA_SYND_IF is associated with the error in MCA::IF::MCA_STATUS_IF. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV47
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: CECC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was a correctable ECC error according to the restrictions of the ECC algorithm. UC indicates whether the error was actually corrected by the processor. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: UECC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was an uncorrectable ECC error according to the restrictions of the ECC algorithm. UC indicates whether the error was actually corrected by the processor. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Deferred
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=A deferred error was created. A deferred error is the result of an uncorrectable data error which did not immediately cause a processor exception; an exception is deferred until the erroneous data is consumed. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Poison
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was the result of attempting to consume poisoned data. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV41
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Scrub
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was the result of a scrub operation. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV38
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrCoreId
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. When ErrCoreIdVal=1 this field indicates which core within the processor is associated with the error; Otherwise this field is reserved. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV30
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: AddrLsb
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. Specifies the least significant valid bit of the address contained in MCA::IF::MCA_ADDR_IF[ErrorAddr]. A value of 0 indicates that MCA::IF::MCA_ADDR_IF[55:0] contains a valid byte address. A value of 6 indicates that MCA::IF::MCA_ADDR_IF[55:6] contains a valid cache line address and that MCA::IF::MCA_ADDR_IF[5:0] are not part of the address and should be ignored by error handling software. A value of 12 indicates that MCA::IF::MCA_ADDR_IF[55:12] contain a valid 4KB memory page and that MCA::IF::MCA_ADDR_IF[11:0] should be ignored by error handling software. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV22
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrorCodeExt
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. Extended Error Code. This field is used to identify the error type for root cause analysis. This field indicates which bit position in MCA::IF::MCA_CTL_IF enables error reporting for the logged error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrorCode
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0000h. Error code for this error. See 3.1.3.3 [Error Codes] for details on decoding this field. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

register.name: MCA_ADDR_IF
register.desc: IF Machine Check Address Thread 0. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. MCA::IF::MCA_ADDR_IF stores an address and other information associated with the error in MCA::IF::MCA_STATUS_IF. The register is only meaningful if MCA::IF::MCA_STATUS_IF[Val]=1 and MCA::IF::MCA_STATUS_IF[AddrV]=1.

field.name: ErrorAddr
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,000_0000_0000_0000h. Unless otherwise specified by an error, contains the address associated with the error logged in MCA::IF::MCA_STATUS_IF. For physical addresses, the most significant bit is given by Core::X86::Cpuid::LongModeInfo [PhysAddrSize].

register.name: MCA_MISC0_IF
register.desc: IF Machine Check Miscellaneous 0 Thread 0. Log miscellaneous information associated with errors.

field.name: Valid
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=A valid CntP field is present in this register. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: CntP
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=A valid threshold counter is present. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: Locked
field.desc: Reset: 0. 1=Writes to this register are ignored. This bit is set by BIOS to indicate that this register is not available for OS use. BIOS should set this bit if ThresholdIntType is set to SMI. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: IntP
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=ThresholdIntType can be used to generate interrupts. 0=ThresholdIntType and interrupt generation are not supported. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::IF::MCA_MISC0_IF[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: LvtOffset
field.desc: Reset: 0h. One per die. For error thresholding interrupts, specifies the address of the LVT entry in the APIC registers as follows: LVT address = (LvtOffset shifted left 4 bits) + 500h (see Core::X86::Apic::ExtendedInterruptLvtEntries). AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::IF::MCA_MISC0_IF[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: CntEn
field.desc: Reset: 0. 1=Count thresholding errors. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::IF::MCA_MISC0_IF[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: ThresholdIntType
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. Specifies the type of interrupt signaled when Ovrflw is set and IntP==1. 00b = No interrupt. 01b = APIC based interrupt (see Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg [ThresholdLvtOffset]). 10b = SMI trigger event. 11b = Reserved. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::IF::MCA_MISC0_IF[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: Ovrflw
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. Set by hardware when ErrCnt transitions from FFEh to FFFh. When this field is set, ErrCnt no longer increments. When this bit is set, the interrupt selected by the ThresholdIntType field is generated. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::IF::MCA_MISC0_IF[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: ErrCnt
field.desc: Reset: Cold,000h. This is written by software to set the starting value of the error counter. This is incremented by hardware when errors are logged. When this counter overflows, it stays at FFFh (no rollover). The threshold value, written by software, is (FFFh - the desired error count (the number of errors necessary in order for an interrupt to be taken)); the desired error count of 0 (a write value of FFFh) is not supported. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::IF::MCA_MISC0_IF[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: BlkPtr
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. 00h=Extended MISC MSR block is not valid. 01h=Extended MSR block is valid.

register.name: MCA_CONFIG_IF
register.desc: IF Machine Check Configuration Thread 0. Reset: 0000_0002_0000_0121h. Controls configuration of the associated machine check bank.

field.name: DeferredIntType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Specifies the type of interrupt signaled when a deferred error is logged. 00b = No interrupt. 01b = APIC based interrupt (see Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg [DeferredLvtOffset]). 10b = SMI trigger event. 11b = Reserved.

field.name: McaXEnable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Init: BIOS,1. Check: 1. 1=Software has acknowledged support for the MCAX feature set. 0=Software has not acknowledged support for the MCAX feature set. All uncorrected and fatal errors will cause an ErrorEvent packet to be generated. Deferred error interrupts are configured via Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg.

field.name: McaLsbInStatusSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=MCA::IF::MCA_CONFIG_IF[McaLsbInStatusSupported] indicates that AddrLbc is located in McaStatus registers.

field.name: DeferredIntTypeSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=MCA::IF::MCA_CONFIG_IF[DeferredIntType] controls the type of interrupt generated on a deferred error. Deferred errors are supported in this bank only if MCA::IF::MCA_CONFIG_IF[DeferredErrorLoggingSupported]=1.

field.name: DeferredErrorLoggingSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0. 1=Deferred errors are supported in this MCA bank, and the LogDeferredInMcaStat field in this register controls the logging behavior of these errors. MCA_DESTAT and MCA_DEADDR are supported in this MCA bank. 0=Deferred errors are not supported in this bank.

field.name: McaX
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=This bank provides Machine Check Architecture Extensions. Up to 4 additional MISC registers (MISC1-MISC4) are supported. MCA::IF::MCA_MISC0_IF[BlkPtr] indicates the presence of the additional MISC registers, but is not used to determine their MSR numbers. Deferred error interrupt type is specifiable by MCA bank. MCA::IF::MCA_STATUS_IF[TCC] is present.

register.name: MCA_IPID_IF
register.desc: IF IP Identification Thread 0. Reset: 0001_00B0_0000_0000h. The MCA::IF::MCA_IPID_IF register is used by software to determine what IP type and revision is associated with the MCA bank.

field.name: McaType
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0001h. The McaType of the MCA bank within this IP.

field.name: InstanceIdHi
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. The HI value instance ID of this IP. This is initialized to a unique ID per instance of this register.

field.name: HardwareID
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0B0h. The Hardware ID of the IP associated with this MCA bank.

field.name: InstanceId
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. The instance ID of this IP. This is initialized to a unique ID per instance of this register.

register.name: MCA_SYND_IF
register.desc: IF Machine Check Syndrome Thread 0. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. Logs physical location information associated with error in MCA::IF::MCA_STATUS_IF Thread 0.

field.name: Syndrome
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0. Contains the syndrome, if any, associated with the error logged in MCA::IF::MCA_STATUS_IF. The low-order bit of the syndrome is stored in bit 0, and the syndrome has a length specified by MCA::IF::MCA_SYND_IF[Length]. The Syndrome field is only valid when MCA::IF::MCA_SYND_IF[Length] is not 0.

field.name: ErrorPriority
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0h. Encodes the priority of the error logged in MCA::IF::MCA_SYND_IF. 3'b000 = No error; 3'b001 = Reserved; 3'b010 = Corrected Error; 3'b011 = Deferred Error; 3'b100 = Uncorrected Error; 3'b101 = Fatal Error; all others reserved.

field.name: Length
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,00h. Specifies the length in bits of the syndrome contained in MCA::IF::MCA_SYND_IF[Syndrome]. A value of 0 indicates that there is no valid syndrome in MCA::IF::MCA_SYND_IF. For example, a syndrome length of 9 means that MCA::IF::MCA_SYND_IF[Syndrome] bits [8:0] contains a valid syndrome.

field.name: ErrorInformation
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0_0000h. Contains error-specific information about the location of the error. Decoding is available in Table 48 [MCA_SYND_IF].

register.name: MCA_CTL_MASK_IF
register.desc: IF Machine Check Control Mask Thread 0. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Inhibit detection of an error source.

field.name: CtMceError
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. CT MCE.

field.name: BsrParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. BSR Parity Error.

field.name: L2TlbMultiHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Init: BIOS,1. L2-TLB Multi-Hit.

field.name: L1TlbMultiHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L1-TLB Multi-Hit.

field.name: HwAssert
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Hardware Assertion Error.

field.name: SystemReadDataError
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L2 Cache Error Response.

field.name: L2RespPoison
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L2 Cache Response Poison Error. Error is the result of consuming poison data.

field.name: L2BtbMultiHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Init: BIOS,1. BP L2-BTB Multi-Hit Error.

field.name: L1BtbMultiHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. BP L1-BTB Multi-Hit Error.

field.name: IcUtagParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Ic MicroTag Parity Error.

field.name: RSVD_8
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Reserved. Will never trigger.

field.name: L2ItlbParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L2-TLB Parity Error.

field.name: L1ItlbParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L1-TLB Parity Error.

field.name: RSVD_5
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Reserved. Will never trigger.

field.name: DqParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. PRQ Parity Error.

field.name: DataParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. IC Data Array Parity Error.

field.name: TagParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. IC Full Tag Parity Error.

field.name: TagMultiHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. IC Full Tag Multi-hit Error.

field.name: OcUtagParity
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Op Cache Microtag Parity Error. Parity errors on PA and other relevant uTag fields are reported, independent of any utag probing. The parity error way and index are logged.

register.name: MCA_CTL_L2
register.desc: L2 Machine Check Control Thread 0. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. 0=Disables error reporting for the corresponding error. 1=Enables error reporting via machine check exception for the corresponding error. The MCA::L2::MCA_CTL_L2 register must be enabled by the corresponding enable bit in Core::X86::Msr::MCG_CTL. Does not affect error detection, correction, or logging.

field.name: Hwa
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Hardware Assert Error.

field.name: Data
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L2M Data Array ECC Error.

field.name: Tag
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L2M Tag or State Array ECC Error.

field.name: MultiHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L2M Tag Multiple-Way-Hit error.

register.name: MCA_STATUS_L2
register.desc: L2 Machine Check Status Thread 0. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. Logs information associated with errors.

field.name: Val
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=A valid error has been detected. This bit should be cleared by software after the register has been read. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Overflow
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=An error was detected while the valid bit (Val) was set; at least one error was not logged. Overflow is set independently of whether the existing error is overwritten. See 3.1.3 [Machine Check Errors]. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: UC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was not corrected by hardware. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: En
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=MCA error reporting is enabled for this error, as indicated by the corresponding bit in MCA::L2::MCA_CTL_L2. This bit is a copy of bit in MCA::L2::MCA_CTL_L2 for this error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: MiscV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Valid thresholding in MCA::L2::MCA_MISC0_L2. In certain modes, MISC registers are owned by platform firmware and will RAZ when read by non-SMM code. Therefore, it is possible for MiscV=1 and the MISC register to read as all zeros. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: AddrV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2 contains address information associated with the error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: PCC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Hardware context held by the processor may have been corrupted. Continued operation of the system may have unpredictable results. The error is not recoverable or survivable, and the system should be reinitialized. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrCoreIdVal
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The ErrCoreId field is valid. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: TCC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Hardware context of the process thread to which the error was reported may have been corrupted. Continued operation of the thread may have unpredictable results. The thread must be terminated. Only meaningful when MCA::L2::MCA_STATUS_L2[PCC]=0. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV54
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bit. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: SyndV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=This error logged information in MCA::L2::MCA_SYND_L2. If MCA::L2::MCA_SYND_L2[ErrorPriority] is the same as the priority of the error in MCA::L2::MCA_STATUS_L2, then the information in MCA::L2::MCA_SYND_L2 is associated with the error in MCA::L2::MCA_STATUS_L2. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV47
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: CECC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was a correctable ECC error according to the restrictions of the ECC algorithm. UC indicates whether the error was actually corrected by the processor. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: UECC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was an uncorrectable ECC error according to the restrictions of the ECC algorithm. UC indicates whether the error was actually corrected by the processor. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Deferred
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=A deferred error was created. A deferred error is the result of an uncorrectable data error which did not immediately cause a processor exception; an exception is deferred until the erroneous data is consumed. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Poison
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was the result of attempting to consume poisoned data. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV41
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Scrub
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was the result of a scrub operation. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV38
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrCoreId
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. When ErrCoreIdVal=1 this field indicates which core within the processor is associated with the error; Otherwise this field is reserved. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV30
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: AddrLsb
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. Specifies the least significant valid bit of the address contained in MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2[ErrorAddr]. A value of 0 indicates that MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2[54:0] contains a valid byte address. A value of 6 indicates that MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2[54:6] contains a valid cache line address and that MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2[5:0] are not part of the address and should be ignored by error handling software. A value of 12 indicates that MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2[54:12] contain a valid 4KB memory page and that MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2[11:0] should be ignored by error handling software. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV22
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrorCodeExt
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. Extended Error Code. This field is used to identify the error type for root cause analysis. This field indicates which bit position in MCA::L2::MCA_CTL_L2 enables error reporting for the logged error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrorCode
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0000h. Error code for this error. See 3.1.3.3 [Error Codes] for details on decoding this field. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

register.name: MCA_ADDR_L2
register.desc: L2 Machine Check Address Thread 0. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2 stores an address and other information associated with the error in MCA::L2::MCA_STATUS_L2. The register is only meaningful if MCA::L2::MCA_STATUS_L2[Val]=1 and MCA::L2::MCA_STATUS_L2[AddrV]=1.

field.name: ErrorAddr
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,00_0000_0000_0000h. Unless otherwise specified by an error, contains the address associated with the error logged in MCA::L2::MCA_STATUS_L2. For physical addresses, the most significant bit is given by Core::X86::Cpuid::LongModeInfo [PhysAddrSize].

register.name: MCA_MISC0_L2
register.desc: L2 Machine Check Miscellaneous 0 Thread 0. Log miscellaneous information associated with errors.

field.name: Valid
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=A valid CntP field is present in this register. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: CntP
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=A valid threshold counter is present. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: Locked
field.desc: Reset: 0. 1=Writes to this register are ignored. This bit is set by BIOS to indicate that this register is not available for OS use. BIOS should set this bit if ThresholdIntType is set to SMI. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: IntP
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=ThresholdIntType can be used to generate interrupts. 0=ThresholdIntType and interrupt generation are not supported. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::L2::MCA_MISC0_L2[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: LvtOffset
field.desc: Reset: 0h. One per die. For error thresholding interrupts, specifies the address of the LVT entry in the APIC registers as follows: LVT address = (LvtOffset shifted left 4 bits) + 500h (see Core::X86::Apic::ExtendedInterruptLvtEntries). AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::L2::MCA_MISC0_L2[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: CntEn
field.desc: Reset: 0. 1=Count thresholding errors. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::L2::MCA_MISC0_L2[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: ThresholdIntType
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. Specifies the type of interrupt signaled when Ovrflw is set and IntP==1. 00b = No interrupt. 01b = APIC based interrupt (see Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg [ThresholdLvtOffset]). 10b = SMI trigger event. 11b = Reserved. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::L2::MCA_MISC0_L2[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: Ovrflw
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. Set by hardware when ErrCnt transitions from FFEh to FFFh. When this field is set, ErrCnt no longer increments. When this bit is set, the interrupt selected by the ThresholdIntType field is generated. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::L2::MCA_MISC0_L2[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: ErrCnt
field.desc: Reset: Cold,000h. This is written by software to set the starting value of the error counter. This is incremented by hardware when errors are logged. When this counter overflows, it stays at FFFh (no rollover). The threshold value, written by software, is (FFFh - the desired error count (the number of errors necessary in order for an interrupt to be taken)); the desired error count of 0 (a write value of FFFh) is not supported. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::L2::MCA_MISC0_L2[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: BlkPtr
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. 00h=Extended MISC MSR block is not valid. 01h=Extended MSR block is valid.

register.name: MCA_CONFIG_L2
register.desc: L2 Machine Check Configuration Thread 0. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0125h. Controls configuration of the associated machine check bank.

field.name: DeferredIntType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Specifies the type of interrupt signaled when a deferred error is logged. 00b = No interrupt. 01b = APIC based interrupt (see Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg [DeferredLvtOffset]). 10b = SMI trigger event. 11b = Reserved.

field.name: LogDeferredInMcaStat
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Init: BIOS,1. 1=Log deferred errors in MCA::L2::MCA_STATUS_L2 and MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2 in addition to MCA::L2::MCA_DESTAT_L2 and MCA::L2::MCA_DEADDR_L2. 0=Only log deferred errors in MCA::L2::MCA_DESTAT_L2 and MCA::L2::MCA_DEADDR_L2. This bit does not affect logging of deferred errors in MCA::L2::MCA_SYND_L2, MCA::L2::MCA_MISC0_L2.

field.name: McaXEnable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Init: BIOS,1. Check: 1. 1=Software has acknowledged support for the MCAX feature set. 0=Software has not acknowledged support for the MCAX feature set. All uncorrected and fatal errors will cause an ErrorEvent packet to be generated. Deferred error interrupts are configured via Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg.

field.name: McaLsbInStatusSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=MCA::L2::MCA_CONFIG_L2[McaLsbInStatusSupported] indicates that AddrLbc is located in McaStatus registers.

field.name: DeferredIntTypeSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=MCA::L2::MCA_CONFIG_L2[DeferredIntType] controls the type of interrupt generated on a deferred error. Deferred errors are supported in this bank only if MCA::L2::MCA_CONFIG_L2[DeferredErrorLoggingSupported]=1.

field.name: DeferredErrorLoggingSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=Deferred errors are supported in this MCA bank, and MCA::L2::MCA_CONFIG_L2[LogDeferredInMcaStat] controls the logging behavior of these errors. MCA::L2::MCA_DESTAT_L2 and MCA::L2::MCA_DEADDR_L2 are supported in this MCA bank. 0=Deferred errors are not supported in this bank.

field.name: McaX
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=This bank provides Machine Check Architecture Extensions. Up to 4 additional MISC registers (MISC1-MISC4) are supported. MCA::L2::MCA_MISC0_L2[BlkPtr] indicates the presence of the additional MISC registers, but is not used to determine their MSR numbers. Deferred error interrupt type is specifiable by MCA bank. MCA::L2::MCA_STATUS_L2[TCC] is present.

register.name: MCA_IPID_L2
register.desc: L2 IP Identification Thread 0. Reset: 0002_00B0_0000_0000h. The MCA::L2::MCA_IPID_L2 register is used by software to determine what IP type and revision is associated with the MCA bank.

field.name: McaType
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0002h. The McaType of the MCA bank within this IP.

field.name: InstanceIdHi
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. The HI value instance ID of this IP. This is initialized to a unique ID per instance of this register.

field.name: HardwareID
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0B0h. The Hardware ID of the IP associated with this MCA bank.

field.name: InstanceId
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. The instance ID of this IP. This is initialized to a unique ID per instance of this register.

register.name: MCA_SYND_L2
register.desc: L2 Machine Check Syndrome Thread 0. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. Logs physical location information associated with error in MCA::L2::MCA_STATUS_L2 Thread 0.

field.name: Syndrome
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0_0000h. Contains the syndrome, if any, associated with the error logged in MCA::L2::MCA_STATUS_L2. The low-order bit of the syndrome is stored in bit 0, and the syndrome has a length specified by MCA::L2::MCA_SYND_L2[Length]. The Syndrome field is only valid when MCA::L2::MCA_SYND_L2[Length] is not 0.

field.name: ErrorPriority
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0h. Encodes the priority of the error logged in MCA::L2::MCA_SYND_L2. 3'b000 = No error; 3'b001 = Reserved; 3'b010 = Corrected Error; 3'b011 = Deferred Error; 3'b100 = Uncorrected Error; 3'b101 = Fatal Error; all others reserved.

field.name: Length
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,00h. Specifies the length in bits of the syndrome contained in MCA::L2::MCA_SYND_L2[Syndrome]. A value of 0 indicates that there is no valid syndrome in MCA::L2::MCA_SYND_L2. For example, a syndrome length of 9 means that MCA::L2::MCA_SYND_L2[Syndrome] bits [8:0] contains a valid syndrome.

field.name: ErrorInformation
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0_0000h. Contains error-specific information about the location of the error. Decoding is available in Table 51 [MCA_SYND_L2].

register.name: MCA_DESTAT_L2
register.desc: L2 Machine Check Deferred Error Status Thread 0. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. Holds status information for the first deferred error seen in this bank.

field.name: Val
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0. 1=A valid error has been detected (whether it is enabled or not).

field.name: Overflow
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0. 1=An error was detected while the valid bit (Val) was set; at least one error was not logged. Overflow is set independently of whether the existing error is overwritten. (See the section on overwrite priorities.).

field.name: RESERV4
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Cold,0h.

field.name: AddrV
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0. 1=MCA::L2::MCA_DEADDR_L2 contains address information associated with the error.

field.name: RESERV3
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Cold,0h.

field.name: SyndV
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0. 1=This error logged information in MCA::L2::MCA_SYND_L2. If MCA::L2::MCA_SYND_L2[ErrorPriority] is the same as the priority of the error in MCA::L2::MCA_STATUS_L2, then the information in MCA::L2::MCA_SYND_L2 is associated with the error in MCA::L2::MCA_DESTAT_L2.

field.name: RESERV2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Cold,00h.

field.name: Deferred
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0. 1=A deferred error was created. A deferred error is the result of an uncorrectable data error which did not immediately cause a processor exception; poison is created and an exception is deferred until the poison data is consumed.

field.name: RESERV1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Cold,0000h.

field.name: AddrLsb
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,00h. Specifies the least significant valid bit of the address contained in MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2[ErrorAddr]. A value of 0 indicates that MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2[54:0] contains a valid byte address. A value of 6 indicates that MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2[54:6] contains a valid cache line address and that MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2[5:0] are not part of the address and should be ignored by error handling software. A value of 12 indicates that MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2[54:12] contain a valid 4KB memory page and that MCA::L2::MCA_ADDR_L2[11:0] should be ignored by error handling software.

field.name: RESERV0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: Cold,0h.

field.name: ErrorCodeExt
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,00h. Logs an extended error code when an error is detected. This model-specific field is used in conjunction with ErrorCode? to identify the error sub-type for root cause analysis.

field.name: ErrorCode
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0000h. Error code for this error.

register.name: MCA_DEADDR_L2
register.desc: L2 Deferred Error Address Thread 0. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. The MCA::L2::MCA_DEADDR_L2 register stores the address associated with the error in MCA::L2::MCA_DESTAT_L2. The register is only meaningful if MCA::L2::MCA_DESTAT_L2[Val]=1 and MCA::L2::MCA_DESTAT_L2[AddrV]=1. The lowest valid bit of the address is defined by MCA::L2::MCA_DESTAT_L2[AddrLsb].

field.name: ErrorAddr
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,00_0000_0000_0000h. Contains the address, if any, associated with the error logged in MCA::L2::MCA_DESTAT_L2. The lowest-order valid bit of the address is specified in MCA::L2::MCA_DESTAT_L2[AddrLsb].

register.name: MCA_CTL_MASK_L2
register.desc: L2 Machine Check Control Mask Thread 0. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Inhibit detection of an error source.

field.name: Hwa
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Init: BIOS,1. Hardware Assert Error.

field.name: Data
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L2M Data Array ECC Error.

field.name: Tag
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L2M Tag or State Array ECC Error.

field.name: MultiHit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. L2M Tag Multiple-Way-Hit error.

register.name: MCA_CTL_DE
register.desc: DE Machine Check Control Thread 0. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. 0=Disables error reporting for the corresponding error. 1=Enables error reporting via machine check exception for the corresponding error. The MCA::DE::MCA_CTL_DE register must be enabled by the corresponding enable bit in Core::X86::Msr::MCG_CTL. Does not affect error detection, correction, or logging.

field.name: HwAssertMca
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Hardware Assertion MCA Error.

field.name: OCBQ
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Micro-op buffer parity error.

field.name: UcSeq
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Patch RAM sequencer parity error.

field.name: UcDat
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Patch RAM data parity error.

field.name: Faq
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Fetch address FIFO parity error.

field.name: Idq
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Instruction dispatch queue parity error.

field.name: UopQ
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Micro-op Queue parity error.

field.name: Ibq
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. IBB Register File parity error.

field.name: OcDat
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Micro-op cache DATA Array parity error.

field.name: OcTag
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Micro-op cache TAG Array parity error.

register.name: MCA_STATUS_DE
register.desc: DE Machine Check Status Thread 0. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. Logs information associated with errors.

field.name: Val
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=A valid error has been detected. This bit should be cleared by software after the register has been read. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Overflow
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=An error was detected while the valid bit (Val) was set; at least one error was not logged. Overflow is set independently of whether the existing error is overwritten. See 3.1.3 [Machine Check Errors]. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: UC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was not corrected by hardware. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: En
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=MCA error reporting is enabled for this error, as indicated by the corresponding bit in MCA::DE::MCA_CTL_DE. This bit is a copy of bit in MCA::DE::MCA_CTL_DE for this error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: MiscV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Valid thresholding in MCA::DE::MCA_MISC0_DE. In certain modes, MISC registers are owned by platform firmware and will RAZ when read by non-SMM code. Therefore, it is possible for MiscV=1 and the MISC register to read as all zeros. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: AddrV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=MCA::DE::MCA_ADDR_DE contains address information associated with the error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: PCC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Hardware context held by the processor may have been corrupted. Continued operation of the system may have unpredictable results. The error is not recoverable or survivable, and the system should be reinitialized. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrCoreIdVal
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The ErrCoreId field is valid. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: TCC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Hardware context of the process thread to which the error was reported may have been corrupted. Continued operation of the thread may have unpredictable results. The thread must be terminated. Only meaningful when MCA::DE::MCA_STATUS_DE[PCC]=0. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV54
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bit. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: SyndV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=This error logged information in MCA::DE::MCA_SYND_DE. If MCA::DE::MCA_SYND_DE[ErrorPriority] is the same as the priority of the error in MCA::DE::MCA_STATUS_DE, then the information in MCA::DE::MCA_SYND_DE is associated with the error in MCA::DE::MCA_STATUS_DE. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV47
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: CECC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was a correctable ECC error according to the restrictions of the ECC algorithm. UC indicates whether the error was actually corrected by the processor. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: UECC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was an uncorrectable ECC error according to the restrictions of the ECC algorithm. UC indicates whether the error was actually corrected by the processor. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Deferred
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=A deferred error was created. A deferred error is the result of an uncorrectable data error which did not immediately cause a processor exception; an exception is deferred until the erroneous data is consumed. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Poison
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was the result of attempting to consume poisoned data. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV41
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Scrub
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was the result of a scrub operation. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV38
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrCoreId
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. When ErrCoreIdVal=1 this field indicates which core within the processor is associated with the error; Otherwise this field is reserved. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV30
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: AddrLsb
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. Specifies the least significant valid bit of the address contained in MCA::DE::MCA_ADDR_DE[ErrorAddr]. A value of 0 indicates that MCA::DE::MCA_ADDR_DE[54:0] contains a valid byte address. A value of 6 indicates that MCA::DE::MCA_ADDR_DE[54:6] contains a valid cache line address and that MCA::DE::MCA_ADDR_DE[5:0] are not part of the address and should be ignored by error handling software. A value of 12 indicates that MCA::DE::MCA_ADDR_DE[54:12] contain a valid 4KB memory page and that MCA::DE::MCA_ADDR_DE[11:0] should be ignored by error handling software. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV22
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrorCodeExt
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. Extended Error Code. This field is used to identify the error type for root cause analysis. This field indicates which bit position in MCA::DE::MCA_CTL_DE enables error reporting for the logged error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrorCode
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0000h. Error code for this error. See 3.1.3.3 [Error Codes] for details on decoding this field. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

register.name: MCA_ADDR_DE
register.desc: DE Machine Check Address Thread 0. Read-only. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. MCA::DE::MCA_ADDR_DE stores an address and other information associated with the error in MCA::DE::MCA_STATUS_DE. The register is only meaningful if MCA::DE::MCA_STATUS_DE[Val]=1 and MCA::DE::MCA_STATUS_DE[AddrV]=1.

field.name: ErrorAddr
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. Contains the address, if any, associated with the error logged in MCA::DE::MCA_STATUS_DE.

register.name: MCA_MISC0_DE
register.desc: DE Machine Check Miscellaneous 0 Thread 0. Log miscellaneous information associated with errors.

field.name: Valid
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=A valid CntP field is present in this register. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: CntP
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=A valid threshold counter is present. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: Locked
field.desc: Reset: 0. 1=Writes to this register are ignored. This bit is set by BIOS to indicate that this register is not available for OS use. BIOS should set this bit if ThresholdIntType is set to SMI. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: IntP
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=ThresholdIntType can be used to generate interrupts. 0=ThresholdIntType and interrupt generation are not supported. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::DE::MCA_MISC0_DE[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: LvtOffset
field.desc: Reset: 0h. One per die. For error thresholding interrupts, specifies the address of the LVT entry in the APIC registers as follows: LVT address = (LvtOffset shifted left 4 bits) + 500h (see Core::X86::Apic::ExtendedInterruptLvtEntries). AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::DE::MCA_MISC0_DE[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: CntEn
field.desc: Reset: 0. 1=Count thresholding errors. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::DE::MCA_MISC0_DE[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: ThresholdIntType
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. Specifies the type of interrupt signaled when Ovrflw is set and IntP==1. 00b = No interrupt. 01b = APIC based interrupt (see Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg [ThresholdLvtOffset]). 10b = SMI trigger event. 11b = Reserved. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::DE::MCA_MISC0_DE[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: Ovrflw
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. Set by hardware when ErrCnt transitions from FFEh to FFFh. When this field is set, ErrCnt no longer increments. When this bit is set, the interrupt selected by the ThresholdIntType field is generated. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::DE::MCA_MISC0_DE[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: ErrCnt
field.desc: Reset: Cold,000h. This is written by software to set the starting value of the error counter. This is incremented by hardware when errors are logged. When this counter overflows, it stays at FFFh (no rollover). The threshold value, written by software, is (FFFh - the desired error count (the number of errors necessary in order for an interrupt to be taken)); the desired error count of 0 (a write value of FFFh) is not supported. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::DE::MCA_MISC0_DE[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: BlkPtr
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. 00h=Extended MISC MSR block is not valid. 01h=Extended MSR block is valid.

register.name: MCA_CONFIG_DE
register.desc: DE Machine Check Configuration Thread 0. Reset: 0000_0002_0000_0121h. Controls configuration of the associated machine check bank.

field.name: DeferredIntType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Specifies the type of interrupt signaled when a deferred error is logged. 00b = No interrupt. 01b = APIC based interrupt (see Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg [DeferredLvtOffset]). 10b = SMI trigger event. 11b = Reserved.

field.name: McaXEnable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Init: BIOS,1. Check: 1. 1=Software has acknowledged support for the MCAX feature set. 0=Software has not acknowledged support for the MCAX feature set. All uncorrected and fatal errors will cause an ErrorEvent packet to be generated. Deferred error interrupts are configured via Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg.

field.name: McaLsbInStatusSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=MCA::DE::MCA_CONFIG_DE[McaLsbInStatusSupported] indicates that AddrLbc is located in McaStatus registers.

field.name: DeferredIntTypeSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=MCA::DE::MCA_CONFIG_DE[DeferredIntType] controls the type of interrupt generated on a deferred error. Deferred errors are supported in this bank only if MCA::DE::MCA_CONFIG_DE[DeferredErrorLoggingSupported]=1.

field.name: DeferredErrorLoggingSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0. 1=Deferred errors are supported in this MCA bank, and the LogDeferredInMcaStat field in this register controls the logging behavior of these errors. MCA_DESTAT and MCA_DEADDR are supported in this MCA bank. 0=Deferred errors are not supported in this bank.

field.name: McaX
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=This bank provides Machine Check Architecture Extensions. Up to 4 additional MISC registers (MISC1-MISC4) are supported. MCA::DE::MCA_MISC0_DE[BlkPtr] indicates the presence of the additional MISC registers, but is not used to determine their MSR numbers. Deferred error interrupt type is specifiable by MCA bank. MCA::DE::MCA_STATUS_DE[TCC] is present.

register.name: MCA_IPID_DE
register.desc: DE IP Identification Thread 0. Reset: 0003_00B0_0000_0000h. The MCA::DE::MCA_IPID_DE register is used by software to determine what IP type and revision is associated with the MCA bank.

field.name: McaType
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0003h. The McaType of the MCA bank within this IP.

field.name: InstanceIdHi
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. The HI value instance ID of this IP. This is initialized to a unique ID per instance of this register.

field.name: HardwareID
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0B0h. The Hardware ID of the IP associated with this MCA bank.

field.name: InstanceId
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. The instance ID of this IP. This is initialized to a unique ID per instance of this register.

register.name: MCA_SYND_DE
register.desc: DE Machine Check Syndrome Thread 0. Read-write,Volatile. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. Logs physical location information associated with error in MCA::DE::MCA_STATUS_DE Thread 0.

field.name: Syndrome
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0. Contains the syndrome, if any, associated with the error logged in MCA::DE::MCA_STATUS_DE. The low-order bit of the syndrome is stored in bit 0, and the syndrome has a length specified by MCA::DE::MCA_SYND_DE[Length]. The Syndrome field is only valid when MCA::DE::MCA_SYND_DE[Length] is not 0.

field.name: ErrorPriority
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0h. Encodes the priority of the error logged in MCA::DE::MCA_SYND_DE. 3'b000 = No error; 3'b001 = Reserved; 3'b010 = Corrected Error; 3'b011 = Deferred Error; 3'b100 = Uncorrected Error; 3'b101 = Fatal Error; all others reserved.

field.name: Length
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,00h. Specifies the length in bits of the syndrome contained in MCA::DE::MCA_SYND_DE[Syndrome]. A value of 0 indicates that there is no valid syndrome in MCA::DE::MCA_SYND_DE. For example, a syndrome length of 9 means that MCA::DE::MCA_SYND_DE[Syndrome] bits [8:0] contains a valid syndrome.

field.name: ErrorInformation
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,0_0000h. Contains error-specific information about the location of the error. Decoding is available in Table 54 [MCA_SYND_DE].

register.name: MCA_CTL_MASK_DE
register.desc: DE Machine Check Control Mask Thread 0. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. Inhibit detection of an error source.

field.name: HwAssertMca
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Hardware Assertion MCA Error.

field.name: OCBQ
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Micro-op buffer parity error.

field.name: UcSeq
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Patch RAM sequencer parity error.

field.name: UcDat
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Patch RAM data parity error.

field.name: Faq
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Fetch address FIFO parity error.

field.name: Idq
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Instruction dispatch queue parity error.

field.name: UopQ
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Micro-op Queue parity error.

field.name: Ibq
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. IBB Register File parity error.

field.name: OcDat
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Micro-op cache DATA Array parity error.

field.name: OcTag
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Micro-op cache TAG Array parity error.

register.name: MCA_CTL_EX
register.desc: EX Machine Check Control Thread 0. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000_0000_0000h. 0=Disables error reporting for the corresponding error. 1=Enables error reporting via machine check exception for the corresponding error. The MCA::EX::MCA_CTL_EX register must be enabled by the corresponding enable bit in Core::X86::Msr::MCG_CTL. Does not affect error detection, correction, or logging.

field.name: RETMAP
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Retire Map parity error.

field.name: SPECMAP
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Spec Map parity error.

field.name: HWA
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Hardware Assertion Error.

field.name: BBQ
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Branch Buffer Queue (BBQ) parity error.

field.name: SQ
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Scheduler Queue parity error.

field.name: STATQ
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Retire status queue parity error.

field.name: RETDISP
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Retire Dispatch Queue parity error.

field.name: CHKPTQ
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Checkpoint Queue (AKA Map_DispQ) parity error.

field.name: PLDAL
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. EX payload parity error.

field.name: PLDAG
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Address generator payload parity error.

field.name: IDRF
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Immediate displacement register file parity error.

field.name: FRF
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Flag register file (FRF) parity error.

field.name: PRF
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Physical register file (PRF) parity error.

field.name: WDT
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Watchdog Timeout.

register.name: MCA_STATUS_EX
register.desc: EX Machine Check Status Thread 0. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. Logs information associated with errors.

field.name: Val
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=A valid error has been detected. This bit should be cleared by software after the register has been read. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Overflow
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=An error was detected while the valid bit (Val) was set; at least one error was not logged. Overflow is set independently of whether the existing error is overwritten. See 3.1.3 [Machine Check Errors]. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: UC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was not corrected by hardware. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: En
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=MCA error reporting is enabled for this error, as indicated by the corresponding bit in MCA::EX::MCA_CTL_EX. This bit is a copy of bit in MCA::EX::MCA_CTL_EX for this error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: MiscV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Valid thresholding in MCA::EX::MCA_MISC0_EX. In certain modes, MISC registers are owned by platform firmware and will RAZ when read by non-SMM code. Therefore, it is possible for MiscV=1 and the MISC register to read as all zeros. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: AddrV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=MCA::EX::MCA_ADDR_EX contains address information associated with the error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: PCC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Hardware context held by the processor may have been corrupted. Continued operation of the system may have unpredictable results. The error is not recoverable or survivable, and the system should be reinitialized. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrCoreIdVal
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The ErrCoreId field is valid. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: TCC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=Hardware context of the process thread to which the error was reported may have been corrupted. Continued operation of the thread may have unpredictable results. The thread must be terminated. Only meaningful when MCA::EX::MCA_STATUS_EX[PCC]=0. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV54
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bit. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: SyndV
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=This error logged information in MCA::EX::MCA_SYND_EX. If MCA::EX::MCA_SYND_EX[ErrorPriority] is the same as the priority of the error in MCA::EX::MCA_STATUS_EX, then the information in MCA::EX::MCA_SYND_EX is associated with the error in MCA::EX::MCA_STATUS_EX. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV47
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: CECC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was a correctable ECC error according to the restrictions of the ECC algorithm. UC indicates whether the error was actually corrected by the processor. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: UECC
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was an uncorrectable ECC error according to the restrictions of the ECC algorithm. UC indicates whether the error was actually corrected by the processor. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Deferred
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=A deferred error was created. A deferred error is the result of an uncorrectable data error which did not immediately cause a processor exception; an exception is deferred until the erroneous data is consumed. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Poison
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was the result of attempting to consume poisoned data. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV41
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: Scrub
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. 1=The error was the result of a scrub operation. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV38
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrCoreId
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. When ErrCoreIdVal=1 this field indicates which core within the processor is associated with the error; Otherwise this field is reserved. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV30
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: AddrLsb
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. Specifies the least significant valid bit of the address contained in MCA::EX::MCA_ADDR_EX[ErrorAddr]. A value of 0 indicates that MCA::EX::MCA_ADDR_EX[55:0] contains a valid byte address. A value of 6 indicates that MCA::EX::MCA_ADDR_EX[55:6] contains a valid cache line address and that MCA::EX::MCA_ADDR_EX[5:0] are not part of the address and should be ignored by error handling software. A value of 12 indicates that MCA::EX::MCA_ADDR_EX[55:12] contain a valid 4KB memory page and that MCA::EX::MCA_ADDR_EX[11:0] should be ignored by error handling software. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: RESERV22
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. MCA_STATUS Register Reserved bits. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrorCodeExt
field.desc: Reset: Cold,00h. Extended Error Code. This field is used to identify the error type for root cause analysis. This field indicates which bit position in MCA::EX::MCA_CTL_EX enables error reporting for the logged error. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

field.name: ErrorCode
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0000h. Error code for this error. See 3.1.3.3 [Error Codes] for details on decoding this field. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read,Write-0-only,Error-on-write-1.

register.name: MCA_ADDR_EX
register.desc: EX Machine Check Address Thread 0. Reset: Cold,0000_0000_0000_0000h. MCA::EX::MCA_ADDR_EX stores an address and other information associated with the error in MCA::EX::MCA_STATUS_EX. The register is only meaningful if MCA::EX::MCA_STATUS_EX[Val]=1 and MCA::EX::MCA_STATUS_EX[AddrV]=1.

field.name: ErrorAddr
field.desc: Read-write, Volatile . Reset: Cold,000_0000_0000_0000h. Unless otherwise specified by an error, contains the address associated with the error logged in MCA::EX::MCA_STATUS_EX. For physical addresses, the most significant bit is given by Core::X86::Cpuid::LongModeInfo [PhysAddrSize].

register.name: MCA_MISC0_EX
register.desc: EX Machine Check Miscellaneous 0 Thread 0. Log miscellaneous information associated with errors.

field.name: Valid
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=A valid CntP field is present in this register. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: CntP
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=A valid threshold counter is present. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: Locked
field.desc: Reset: 0. 1=Writes to this register are ignored. This bit is set by BIOS to indicate that this register is not available for OS use. BIOS should set this bit if ThresholdIntType is set to SMI. AccessType: Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: IntP
field.desc: Reset: 1. 1=ThresholdIntType can be used to generate interrupts. 0=ThresholdIntType and interrupt generation are not supported. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::EX::MCA_MISC0_EX[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: LvtOffset
field.desc: Reset: 0h. One per die. For error thresholding interrupts, specifies the address of the LVT entry in the APIC registers as follows: LVT address = (LvtOffset shifted left 4 bits) + 500h (see Core::X86::Apic::ExtendedInterruptLvtEntries). AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::EX::MCA_MISC0_EX[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: CntEn
field.desc: Reset: 0. 1=Count thresholding errors. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::EX::MCA_MISC0_EX[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: ThresholdIntType
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0h. Specifies the type of interrupt signaled when Ovrflw is set and IntP==1. 00b = No interrupt. 01b = APIC based interrupt (see Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg [ThresholdLvtOffset]). 10b = SMI trigger event. 11b = Reserved. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::EX::MCA_MISC0_EX[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: Ovrflw
field.desc: Reset: Cold,0. Set by hardware when ErrCnt transitions from FFEh to FFFh. When this field is set, ErrCnt no longer increments. When this bit is set, the interrupt selected by the ThresholdIntType field is generated. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::EX::MCA_MISC0_EX[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: ErrCnt
field.desc: Reset: Cold,000h. This is written by software to set the starting value of the error counter. This is incremented by hardware when errors are logged. When this counter overflows, it stays at FFFh (no rollover). The threshold value, written by software, is (FFFh - the desired error count (the number of errors necessary in order for an interrupt to be taken)); the desired error count of 0 (a write value of FFFh) is not supported. AccessType: (Core::X86::Msr::HWCR [McStatusWrEn] | !MCA::EX::MCA_MISC0_EX[Locked]) ? Read-write : Read-only.

field.name: BlkPtr
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. 00h=Extended MISC MSR block is not valid. 01h=Extended MSR block is valid.

register.name: MCA_CONFIG_EX
register.desc: EX Machine Check Configuration Thread 0. Reset: 0000_0002_0000_0121h. Controls configuration of the associated machine check bank.

field.name: DeferredIntType
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Specifies the type of interrupt signaled when a deferred error is logged. 00b = No interrupt. 01b = APIC based interrupt (see Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg [DeferredLvtOffset]). 10b = SMI trigger event. 11b = Reserved.

field.name: McaXEnable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Init: BIOS,1. Check: 1. 1=Software has acknowledged support for the MCAX feature set. 0=Software has not acknowledged support for the MCAX feature set. All uncorrected and fatal errors will cause an ErrorEvent packet to be generated. Deferred error interrupts are configured via Core::X86::Msr::McaIntrCfg.

field.name: McaLsbInStatusSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=MCA::EX::MCA_CONFIG_EX[McaLsbInStatusSupported] indicates that AddrLbc is located in McaStatus registers.

field.name: DeferredIntTypeSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=MCA::EX::MCA_CONFIG_EX[DeferredIntType] controls the type of interrupt generated on a deferred error. Deferred errors are supported in this bank only if MCA::EX::MCA_CONFIG_EX[DeferredErrorLoggingSupported]=1.

field.name: DeferredErrorLoggingSupported
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0. 1=Deferred errors are supported in this MCA bank, and the LogDeferredInMcaStat field in this register controls the logging behavior of these errors. MCA_DESTAT and MCA_DEADDR are supported in this MCA bank. 0=Deferred errors are not supported in this bank.

field.name: McaX
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. 1=This bank provides Machine Check Architecture Extensions. Up to 4 additional MISC registers (MISC1-MISC4) are supported. MCA::EX::MCA_MISC0_EX[BlkPtr] indicates the presence of the additional MISC registers, but is not used to determine their MSR numbers. Deferred error interrupt type is specifiable by MCA bank. MCA::EX::MCA_STATUS_EX[TCC] is present.

register.name: MCA_IPID_EX
register.desc: EX IP Identification Thread 0. Reset: 0005_00B0_0000_0000h. The MCA::EX::MCA_IPID_EX register is used by software to determine what IP type and revision is associated with the MCA bank.

field.name: McaType
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0005h. The McaType of the MCA bank within this IP.

field.name: InstanceIdHi
