register.name: irq_pin_assertion_register
register.desc: FCH::IOAPIC::irq_pin_assertion_register. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. Write to this register will trigger an interrupt associated with the redirection table entry referenced by the IRQ number. Currently the redirection table has 24 entries. Write with IRQ number greater than 17H has no effect.

field.name: input_irq
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. IRQ number for the requested interrupt.

register.name: eoi_register
register.desc: FCH::IOAPIC::eoi_register. Write-only. Reset: 0000_0000h. Write to this register will clear the remote IRR bit in the redirection table entry found matching the interrupt vector. This provides an alternate mechanism other than PCI special cycle for EOI to reach IOXAPIC.

field.name: vector
field.desc: Write-only. Reset: 00h. Interrupt vector.

register.name: shdwsyscnt_l
register.desc: FCH::SSC::shdwsyscnt_l. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: shdwsyscnt_l
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. Lower 32-bits of the Shadow ARM System Counter.

register.name: shdwsyscnt_h
register.desc: FCH::SSC::shdwsyscnt_h. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. SW should read ShdwSysCnt_L first then read this register to ensure the value of ShdwSysCnt_L and ShdwSysCnt_H are consistent.

field.name: shdwsyscnt_h
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. Upper 32-bits of the Shadow ARM System Counter.

register.name: shdwsysalarm_l
register.desc: FCH::SSC::shdwsysalarm_l. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: shdwsysalarm_l
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. Lower 32-bits of the Shadow ARM System Alarm.

register.name: shdwsysalarm_h
register.desc: FCH::SSC::shdwsysalarm_h. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: shdwsysalarm_h
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. Upper 32-bits of the Shadow ARM System Alarm.

register.name: shdwsysctrl
register.desc: FCH::SSC::shdwsysctrl. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: cnt48m100mrun
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 0: 48MHz counter to measure 100MHz SSC is stopped. 1: 48MHz counter to measure 100MHz SSC is running.

field.name: shdwsyscnttesten
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 0: Normal function mode 1: Simulation mode. In this simulation mode, the counter increment during sleep mode is decreased and RtcClk has to be 3.23369MHz.

field.name: shdwsyscntalarmen
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: Read/Write. 0: ShdwSysAlarm is disabled. 1: ShdwSysAlarm is enabled, only valid when ShdwSysCntRun is 1.

field.name: shdwsyscntrun
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 0: ShdwSysCnt is stopped. Note that it is simply stopped, not reset. 1: ShdwSysCnt is running.

register.name: shdwsyssts
register.desc: FCH::SSC::shdwsyssts. Read-write,Read,Write-1-to-clear. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: shdwsysalarmsts
field.desc: Read-write,Read,Write-1-to-clear. Reset: 0. HW set this bit to 1 when shadow system counter alarm fires, SW write 1 to clear this bit.

register.name: shdwrtctimer
register.desc: FCH::SSC::shdwrtctimer. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: shdwrtctmr
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h. Description: Shadow registers of SecureRtcTimer in secure register block. Read only.

register.name: num100mtickwithin1ms
register.desc: FCH::SSC::num100mtickwithin1ms. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: num100mtickwithin1ms
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00_0000h. Description: value of Num100MtickWithin1Ms. Num100MtickWithin1Ms contains the number of ticks running at 100M Ref Clk within 1ms <_period defined by Cnt48Mperiod at addr = 0x28_>.

register.name: cnt48mperiod
register.desc: FCH::SSC::cnt48mperiod. Read-write. Reset: 0000_BB80h.

field.name: cnt48mperiod
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0_BB80h. Description: Define the period of 48MHz counter, 20-bit is used. 0xBB80= 48000, 48000/48M = 1.0ms Max=0xFFFFF = 1048575, 1048575/48M = 21.845ms.

register.name: s0i3entrytime_l
register.desc: FCH::SSC::s0i3entrytime_l. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: s0i3entrytime_l
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h. Description: Lower 32-bits of the S0I3 entry time. <_counted as 48M Clk_> S0i3 entry time is measured when LDT_RSTB goes low.

register.name: s0i3entrytime_h
register.desc: FCH::SSC::s0i3entrytime_h. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: s0i3entrytime_h
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h. Description: Upper 32-bits of the S0I3 entry time. <_counted as 48M Clk_> S0i3 entry time is measured when LDT_RSTB goes low.

register.name: s0i3exittime_l
register.desc: FCH::SSC::s0i3exittime_l. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: s0i3exittime_l
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h. Description: Lower 32-bits of the S0I3 Exit time. <_counted as 48M Clk_> S0i3 exit time is measured when LDT_RSTB goes high.

register.name: s0i3exittime_h
register.desc: FCH::SSC::s0i3exittime_h. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: s0i3exittime_h
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h. Description: Upper 32-bits of the S0I3 Exit time. <_counted as 48M Clk_> S0i3 exit time is measured when LDT_RSTB goes high.

register.name: vendor_id
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::vendor_id. Read-only. Reset: 1022h.

field.name: vendor_id
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1022h. Vendor Identifier. The vendor ID is 0x1022. This is the former ATI vendor ID which is now owned by AMD. AMD officially has two vendor IDs.

register.name: device_id
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::device_id. Read-only. Reset: 790Eh.

field.name: device_id
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 790Eh. Device Identifier. This 16-bit field is assigned by the device manufacturer and identifies the type of device. The device ID is selected by internal e-fuses.

register.name: command
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::command. Read-write. Reset: 000Fh. Command Register: The PCI specification defines this register to control a PCI device's ability to generate and respond to PCI cycles. Writes to this register, except bit 6, have no effect. Bits[3:0]=0fh and are read-only.

field.name: fast_back_to_back_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Fast Back-to-back Enable. The SB only acts as a master to a single device, so this functionality is not needed. This bit is always 0.

field.name: serr_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. SERR# Enable. If set to 1, the SB asserts SERR# when it detects an address parity error. SERR# is not asserted if this bit is 0.

field.name: stepping_control
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Wait Cycle Enable. The SB does not need to insert a wait state between the address and data on the AD lines. This bit is always 0.

field.name: parity_error_response
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. PERR# <_Response_> Detection Enable bit. If set to 1, The SB asserts PERR# when it is the agent receiving data AND it detects a parity error. PERR# is not asserted if this bit is 0.

field.name: vga_palette_snoop
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. VGA Palette Snoop Enable. The SB <_Southbridge_> does not need to snoop VGA palette cycles. This bit is always 0.

field.name: memory_write_and_invalidate_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Memory Write and Invalidate Enable. Not implemented. This bit is always 0.

field.name: special_cycles
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Special Cycle Recognition Enable. This feature is not implemented and this bit is always 1.

field.name: bus_master
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: Bus Master Enable. 1: Enable 0: Disable.

field.name: memory_space
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Memory Access Enable. This function is not implemented. This bit is always 1.

field.name: io_space
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. I/O Access Enable. This bit controls access to the I/O space registers. When this bit is 1, it enables access to the legacy IDE ports, and PCI bus master IDE I/O registers are enabled.

register.name: status
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::status. Read-write. Reset: 0220h. Status Register: The PCI specification defines this register to record status information for PCI related events. This is a read/write register. However, writes can only reset bits. A bit is reset when the register is written and the data in the corresponding bit location is a 1.

field.name: detected_parity_error
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Detected Parity Error. This bit is set to 1 when the SB detects a parity error.

field.name: signaled_system_error
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. SERR# status. This bit is set to 1 when the SB detects a PCI address parity error.

field.name: received_master_abort
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Received Master Abort Status. Set to 1 when the SB acts as a PCI master and aborts a PCI bus memory cycle. Cleared by writing a 1 to this bit.

field.name: received_target_abort
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Received Target Abort. Set to 1 when an SB generated PCI cycle <_the SB is the PCI master_> is aborted by a PCI target. Cleared by writing a 1 to it.

field.name: signaled_target_abort
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Signaled Target Abort. Read-only, Set to 1 when the SB signals Target Abort.

field.name: device_select_timing
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1h. Description: DEVSEL# Timing. Read-only. Indicates DEVSEL# timing when performing a positive decode. Since DEVSEL# is asserted to meet the medium timing, these bits are encoded as 01b.

field.name: master_data_parity_error
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Data Parity Reported. Set to 1 if the SB detects PERR# asserted while acting as PCI master <_whether PERR# was driven by the SB or not._>.

field.name: capabilities_list
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Read-only. When reg0x78[1] <_Msi On_> is 1, this bit reads 1 when reg0x78[1] <_Msi On_> is 0, this bit reads 0.

register.name: revision_id_class_code
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::revision_id_class_code. Read-only. Reset: 0601_0051h. Revision ID/Class Code Register: This read only register contains the device's revision information and generic function. Since SB is an ISA bridge, its assigned class code is 060100h.

field.name: class_code
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 06_0100h. Class Code.

field.name: revision_id
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 51h. These bits are hardwired to 11h to indicate the revision level of the chip design.

register.name: cache_line_size
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::cache_line_size. Read-only. Reset: 00h. Cache Line Size Register: This register specifies the system cache line size. This register is not implemented.

field.name: cache_line_size
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Cache Line Size.

register.name: latency_timer
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::latency_timer. Read-only. Reset: 00h. Latency Timer Register: This register specifies the value of the Latency Timer in units of PCICLKs.

field.name: latency_timer
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Latency Timer.

register.name: header_type
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::header_type. Read-only. Reset: 80h. Header Type Register: This register identifies the type of the predefined header in the configuration space. Since THE SB is a multifunction device, the most significant bit is set.

field.name: header_type
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 80h. Header Type.

register.name: bist
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::bist. Read-only. Reset: 00h. Built-in Self Test Register: This register is used for control and status for Built-in Self Test. LPC has no BIST modes.

field.name: bist
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. BIST.

register.name: pci_control
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::pci_control. Read-write. Reset: 1Ch.

field.name: ecsemaphore
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: Read only. 1: SPI rom is locked by External EC. 0: SPI rom is not locked by External EC.

field.name: biossemaphore
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: This bit is writeable by Host to block EC Rom access. 1: SPI rom is locked by Host. 0: SPI rom is not locked by Host. Wehnever the Host want to lock down Rom access then The host should keep writting the bit to 1 untill read back to 1.

field.name: extromsharingen
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: 1: support Rom sharing in External mode. 0: disable Rom sharing in External mode.

field.name: vwromsharingen
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: 1: support Rom sharing in Virtual write mode. 0: disable Rom sharing in Virtual write mode.

field.name: legacy_dma_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Setting it to 1 enables LPC DMA cycle. Note: 32-bit DMA is not supported. Transfer size: Channels 0-3: 8 bits, channels 5-7: 16 bits.

register.name: io_port_decode_enable
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::io_port_decode_enable. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. This register controls the decoding of parallel, serial, audio, MID, and MSS, FDC, game, KBC, ACPI micro-controller, & Ad-lib ports. Writing 1 to a bit enables the corresponding I/O range.

field.name: ad_libport_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for Ad-Lib port, 388-389h.

field.name: acpi_micro_controller_port_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for ACPI Micro-Controller port, 62 & 66h.

field.name: kbcport_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for KBC port, 60 & 64h.

field.name: gameport_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for Game port, 200-20fh.

field.name: fdcport_enable_1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for FDC port, 370-377h.

field.name: fdcport_enable_0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for FDC port, 3f0-3f7h.

field.name: mss_port_enable_3
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for MSS port, f40-f47h.

field.name: mss_port_enable_2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for MSS port, e80-e87h.

field.name: mss_port_enable_1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for MSS port, 604-60bh.

field.name: mss_port_enable_0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for MSS port, 530-537h.

field.name: midiport_enable_3
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for MIDI port, 330-331h.

field.name: midiport_enable_2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for MIDI port, 320-321h.

field.name: midiport_enable_1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for MIDI port, 310-311h.

field.name: midiport_enable_0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for MIDI port, 300-301h.

field.name: audioport_enable_3
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for audio port, 280-293h.

field.name: audioport_enable_2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for audio port, 260-273h.

field.name: audioport_enable_1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for audio port, 240-253h.

field.name: audioport_enable_0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for audio port, 230-233h <_range 220-22fh needs to be enabled using bits 0 and 1_>.

field.name: serialport_enable_7
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for serial port, 3e8-3efh.

field.name: serialport_enable_6
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for serial port, 338-33fh.

field.name: serialport_enable_5
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for serial port, 2e8-2efh.

field.name: serialport_enable_4
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for serial port, 238-23fh.

field.name: serialport_enable_3
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for serial port, 228-22fh.

field.name: serialport_enable_2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for serial port, 220-227h.

field.name: serialport_enable_1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for serial port, 2f8-2ffh.

field.name: serialport_enable_0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for serial port, 3f8-3ffh.

field.name: parallelport_enable_5
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for parallel port, 7bc-7bfh.

field.name: parallelport_enable_4
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for parallel port, 3bc-3bfh.

field.name: parallelport_enable_3
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for parallel port, 678-67fh.

field.name: parallelport_enable_2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for parallel port, 278-27fh.

field.name: parallelport_enable_1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for parallel port, 778-77fh.

field.name: parallelport_enable_0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for parallel port, 378-37fh.

register.name: io_mem_port_decode_enable
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::io_mem_port_decode_enable. Read-write. Reset: 0000_FF00h. This register controls the decoding of Super I/O configuration, alternate Super I/O configuration, wide generic ports, ROM1 & ROM2 ports, and memory port. Writing a 1 to a bit enables the corresponding IO/ROM/Memory range.

field.name: wide_io2_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Wide IO port 2 <_defined in registers 90/91h_> enable.

field.name: wide_io1_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Wide IO port 1 <_defined in registers 66/67h_> enable.

field.name: io_port_enable_6
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for IO port FD60h-FD6Fh.

field.name: io_port_enable_5
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for IO port 4700h-470Bh.

field.name: io_port_enable_4
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for Iport 80h. Sw should always set the bit to 0.

field.name: mem_port_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for 4K byte memory range defined in reg0x4C.

field.name: io_port_enable_3
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for IO port 580h-5BFh.

field.name: io_port_enable_2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for IO port 500h-53Fh.

field.name: io_port_enable_1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for IO port 480h-4BFh.

field.name: io_port_enable_0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for IO port 400h-43Fh.

field.name: sync_timeout_count
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: FFh. Sync Timeout Count. This is the number of LPC clocks that the state machine will wait when LPC data = sync before aborting the cycle <_when Sync Timeout Counter Enable is set_>.

field.name: sync_timeout_counter_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. LPC sync timeout counter enabled when set to 1 otherwise the counter is disabled. This counter is used to avoid a deadlock condition if an LPC device drives sync forever. Timeout count is programmed in register0x49h. Write 0 to this bit if an LPC device is extremely slow & takes more than 255 LPC clocks to complete a cycle.

field.name: rtc_io_range_port_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for RTC I/O range 70h~73h.

field.name: memoryrangeport_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for LPC memory target range, see registers 60h- 63h.

field.name: wide_generic_io_port_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for WideGenericPort, see registers 64h-65h.

field.name: alternate_super_io_configuration_port_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for AlternateSuperI/OConfigPort, 4e-4fh.

field.name: super_io_configuration_port_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Port enable for SuperI/OConfigPort, 2e-2fh.

register.name: memoryrange
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::memoryrange. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: base_address
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0_0000h. This register defines a 4K byte memory range from {Base Address, 000h} to {Base Address, FFFh}. The range is enabled by reg0x4A[4] <_Mem port enable_>.

register.name: rom_protect_0
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::rom_protect_0. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: rombase
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0_0000h. Description: Protected range is defined below: {RomBase, 12'h0} ~ {RomBase, 12'h0} + <_Range+1_> *4K <_or 64K if bit 8 is 1_> Note: To support 64MB ROM, RomBase has been changed to use a Unified Mapped Addressfor all three ROMAddressRange <_LPC bridge, PCI 0x68:6B, 0x6C:6F, 0xA8:AF_> definition as shown below. Hardware will then further convert the Unified Mapped Address into physical address at the flash ROM ROM1 mapping to Unified Address: 000F_FFFF: 000E_8000 FFFF_FFFF:FFFE_8000 ROM2 mapping to Unified Address: FFFF_FFFF:FF00_0000 FFFF_FFFF:FF00_0000 ROM3 mapping to Unified Address: FD_03FF_FFFF:FD_0000_0000 FFFF_FFFF:FC00_0000 Examples of : 1: To set write and read protection for 16K space at the unified address mapping of FFF8_3FFF: FFF8_0000 Set this register to 0xFFF8_0603: 2: To set write and read protection for 512K space at the unified address mapping of FC07_FFFF: FC00_0000 Set this register to 0xFC00_0707:

field.name: write_protect
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When this bit is set, the memory range defined by this register is write-protected. Writing to the range has no effect.

field.name: read_protect
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When this bit is set, the memory range defined by this register is read-protected. Reading any location in the range returns FFFF_FFFFh.

field.name: rangeunit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 0: 4K 1: 64K.

field.name: range
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: Specify the protected range in either 4KB or 64KB unit depending on setting on bit 8. Note: the protection definition is remapped to be within a 4GB range. BIOS should make sure resultant {RomBase, 12'h0} + <_Range+1_> *4K <_or 64K if bit 8 is 1_> is within a valid range and will not cross FFFF_FFFF.

register.name: rom_protect_1
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::rom_protect_1. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: rombase
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0_0000h. Description: Protected range is defined below: {RomBase, 12'h0} ~ {RomBase, 12'h0} + <_Range+1_> *4K <_or 64K if bit 8 is 1_> Note: please refer to offsetx50 bit[31:12] for more information.

field.name: write_protect
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When this bit is set, the memory range defined by this register is write-protected. Writing to the range has no effect.

field.name: read_protect
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When this bit is set, the memory range defined by this register is read-protected. Reading any location in the range returns FFFF_FFFFh.

field.name: rangeunit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 0: 4K 1: 64K.

field.name: range
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: Specify the protected range in either 4KB or 64KB unit depending on setting on bit 8. Note: the protection definition is remapped to be within a 4GB range. BIOS should make sure resultant {RomBase, 12'h0} + <_Range+1_> *4K <_or 64K if bit 8 is 1_> is within a valid range and will not cross FFFF_FFFF.

register.name: rom_protect_2
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::rom_protect_2. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: rombase
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0_0000h. Description: Protected range is defined below: {RomBase, 12'h0} ~ {RomBase, 12'h0} + <_Range+1_> *4K <_or 64K if bit 8 is 1_> Note: please refer to offsetx50 bit[31:12] for more information.

field.name: write_protect
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When this bit is set, the memory range defined by this register is write-protected. Writing to the range has no effect.

field.name: read_protect
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When this bit is set, the memory range defined by this register is read-protected. Reading any location in the range returns FFFF_FFFFh.

field.name: rangeunit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 0: 4K 1: 64K.

field.name: range
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: Specify the protected range in either 4KB or 64KB unit depending on setting on bit 8. Note: the protection definition is remapped to be within a 4GB range. BIOS should make sure resultant {RomBase, 12'h0} + <_Range+1_> *4K <_or 64K if bit 8 is 1_> is within a valid range and will not cross FFFF_FFFF.

register.name: rom_protect_3
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::rom_protect_3. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: rombase
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0_0000h. Description: Protected range is defined below: {RomBase, 12'h0} ~ {RomBase, 12'h0} + <_Range+1_> *4K <_or 64K if bit 8 is 1_> Note: please refer to offsetx50 bit[31:12] for more information.

field.name: write_protect
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When this bit is set, the memory range defined by this register is write-protected. Writing to the range has no effect.

field.name: read_protect
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When this bit is set, the memory range defined by this register is read-protected. Reading any location in the range returns FFFF_FFFFh.

field.name: rangeunit
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 0: 4K 1: 64K.

field.name: range
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: Specify the protected range in either 4KB or 64KB unit depending on setting on bit 8. Note: the protection definition is remapped to be within a 4GB range. BIOS should make sure resultant {RomBase, 12'h0} + <_Range+1_> *4K <_or 64K if bit 8 is 1_> is within a valid range and will not cross FFFF_FFFF.

register.name: pci_memory_start_address_for_lpc_target_cycles
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::pci_memory_start_address_for_lpc_target_cycles. Read-write. Reset: 0000h.

field.name: memory_start_address
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000h. Description: 16-bit starting address of the LPC target <_memory_> range. This register contains the upper 16 bits of the starting address of the LPC memory target range. The lower 16 bits of the starting address are considered 0's. This range can be enabled/disabled using reg0x48[5] <_Memory Range Port Enable_>.

register.name: pci_memory_end_address_for_lpc_target_cycles
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::pci_memory_end_address_for_lpc_target_cycles. Read-write. Reset: 0000h.

field.name: memory_end_address
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000h. Description: 16-bit END address of the LPC target <_memory_> range. This register contains the upper 16 bits of the ending address of the LPC memory target range. The lower 16 bits of the end address are considered 1's. This range can be enabled/disabled using reg0x48[5] <_Memory Range Port Enable_>.

register.name: pci_io_base_address_for_widegenericport
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::pci_io_base_address_for_widegenericport. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. This register contains two 16-bits of I/O base address for LPC I/O <_wide generic port_> target range. The limit address is found by adding 512 to the base address.

field.name: io_base_address_1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000h. 16-bit PCI I/O base address for wide generic port range. 512byte wide range. This function is enabled byreg0x4B[0] <_0x48[24]_> <_Super IO Configuration Port Enable_>.When Alternative Wide Io Range Enable <_0x74[2]_> is set to 1, the range is 16 bytes otherwise, the range is 512 bytes.

field.name: io_base_address_0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000h. 16-bit PCI I/O base address for wide generic port range. 16/512byte wide range. This function is enabled by reg0x48[2] <_Wide Generic IO Port Enable_>. When Alternative Wide Io Range Enable <_0x74[0]_> is set to 1, the range is 16 bytes otherwise, the range is 512 bytes.

register.name: romaddressrange_1_start_address
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::romaddressrange_1_start_address. Read-write. Reset: 0000h.

field.name: rom_start_address_1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000h. 16-bit starting address of the ROM <_memory_> address range 1. Default is set to 1M below 1M. This register contains the upper 16 bits of the starting address of the ROM address range 1. The lower 16 bits of the starting address are considered 0's.

register.name: romaddressrange_1_end_address
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::romaddressrange_1_end_address. Read-write.

field.name: rom_end_address_1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXb. 16-bit END address of the ROM <_memory_> address range 1. This register contains the upper 16 bits of the ending address of the ROM address range 1. The lower 16 bits of the end address are considered 1's.

register.name: romaddressrange_2_start_address
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::romaddressrange_2_start_address. Read-write.

field.name: rom_start_address_2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXb. 16-bit starting address of the ROM <_memory_> address range 2. Default is set to 16M below 4GB. This register contains the upper 16 bits of the starting address of the ROM address range 2. The lower 16 bits of the starting address are considered 0's.

register.name: romaddressrange_2_end_address
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::romaddressrange_2_end_address. Read-write.

field.name: rom_end_address_2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXb. 16-bit END address of the ROM <_memory_> address range 2. This register contains the upper 16 bits of the ending address of the ROM address range 2. The lower 16 bits of the end address are considered 1's.

register.name: firmware_hub_select
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::firmware_hub_select. Read-write. Reset: 0023_4567h.

register.name: alternative_wide_io_range_enable
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::alternative_wide_io_range_enable. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: alternative_wide_io_2_range_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Similar to bit[0], but it applies to I/O address defined in reg0x90~91.

field.name: alternative_wide_io_1_range_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Similar to bit[0], but it applies to I/O address defined in reg0x66~67.

field.name: alternative_wide_io_range_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Wide I/O range is usually 512 bytes. With this bit set, the range changes to 16 bytes only. To use this feature, address in reg0x64~65 must be aligned to 16 bytes, i.e., bits[3:0] must be 0. If the address is not aligned to 16 bytes, the I/O range is from address[15:0] to {address[15:4], 0xF}.

register.name: miscellaneous_control_bits
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::miscellaneous_control_bits. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0291h.

field.name: ldrq0_pu_en
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 1: Enable the pull-up of LDRQ0 pad 0: Disable the pull-up of LDRQ0 pad.

field.name: ldrq0_pd_en
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: 1: Enable the pull-down of LDRQ0 pad 0: Disable the pull-down of LDRQ0 pad.

field.name: msi_hidden
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 1: Host can change the value of PCI_Reg 80h 0: Host can not change the value of PCI_Reg 80h.

field.name: allowhostindma
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: 1: allow Host to access Lpc if acpi has not give gnt to lpc during dma transfer. 0: Dma hold LPc even acpi has not give gnt to lpc during dma transfer.

field.name: gatewrongrx
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Set to 1 to allow AltrxByteCount to be 0.

field.name: gatespiaccessdis
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Set to 1 to pass rom access to spi even it is strapped as lpc.

field.name: smmwriteromen
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Enable Rom access in SMM mode.

field.name: ldrq1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Enable LDRQ1# on LPC bus if set to 1.

field.name: ldrq0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Enable LDRQ0# on LPC bus if set to 1.

field.name: no_hog
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: 1:when enabled, the internal bus will not be locked by LPC bridge during a slave access <_eg. LPC DMA fetch_>. 0: If not set, LPC may hold the internal bus during a DMA transfer.

register.name: tpm_trusted_platform_module
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::tpm_trusted_platform_module. Read-write. Reset: 03h. any tpm cycle above is decoded only when the cycle is started by ALinkBridge. Access from bus master devices is not allowed.

field.name: widertpmen
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Set to 1 to force logic to decode FED4xxxx as TPM cycles instead of FED4_0xxx, FED4_1xxx, FED4_2xxx, FED4_3xxx, and FED4_4xxx.

field.name: tmkbc_sel
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Select which one of the four sets of tmkbc registers <_specified in the registers 84h, 88h, and 8Ch_> to be accessed.

field.name: tmkbc_set
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Write once bit. Once set, all tmkbc address/remap registers cannot be changed until the next reset.

field.name: tmkbc_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Enable bit for the TMKBC function.

field.name: tpm_legacy
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When set to 1, it enables decoding of legacy tpm addresses, i.e., I/O addresses 7E/7F and EE/EF will be decoded.

field.name: tpm_amd
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: When set to 1, the following cycles covert into TPM cycle. 0xFD_F920_0000~0xFD_F923_FFFF --> 0x4028 0xFD_F928_0000~0xFD_F928_0003 --> 0x4020 0xFD_F928_0004~0xFD_F928_0007 --> 0x4024~0x4027.

field.name: tpm12_en
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: When set to 1 then the follow memory cycles pass to TPM device. 0xFED4_0xxx 0xFED4_1xxx 0xFED4_2xxx 0xFED4_3xxx 0xFED4_4xxx.

register.name: lpcclkcntl
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::lpcclkcntl. Read-write.

field.name: tpmbufferen
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 1: Enable TPM buffer. 0: Disable TPM buffer.

field.name: tpmpfetchen
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 1: Enable TPM burst read. 0: Disable TPM burst read.

field.name: lpcclk1isgpio
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: RW. 1: Treat LpcClk1IsGpio as GPIO 0: Treat LpcClk1IsGpio as LpcClk1.

field.name: gpiolpcclk1out
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. RW, control GpioLpcClk1 output value.

field.name: gpiolpcclk1oeb
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: RW. 1: disable GpioLpcClk1 output 0: enable GpioLpcClk1 output.

field.name: gpiolpcclk1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: X. Read only. Status of LpcClk1 port.

register.name: tmkbc_baseaddrlow
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::tmkbc_baseaddrlow. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: tmkbc_baseaddrlow
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 000_0000h. Description: This register defines the lower 32 bit memory address used for the TMKBC function. There are actually four sets of such mapping. The selection is controlled by reg0x7C[6:5] <_Tmkbc_sel_>. 00: set 0 01: set 1 10: set 2 11: set 3.

field.name: maskbits13thru8
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: Defines whether address bits [13:8] are masked. 1:Masked 0:No mask.

field.name: maskbits12thru8
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: Defines whether address bits [12:8] are masked. 1:Masked 0:No mask.

field.name: maskbits11thru8
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: Defines whether address bits [11:8] are masked. 1:Masked 0:No mask.

field.name: maskbits10thru8
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: Defines whether address bits[10:8] are masked <_"masked" means bits[10:8] are don't care_>. 1:Masked 0:No mask.

field.name: addr64
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: Defines whether the address is 32 or 64 bits. 1:The address is 64 bits 0:The address is 32 bits.

register.name: tmkbc_baseaddrhigh
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::tmkbc_baseaddrhigh. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: tmkbc_baseaddrhigh
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. Description: This register defines the upper 32 bit memory address used for the TMKBC function. This register has no meaning if bit 2 of 84h is set to 0. There are actually four sets of such mapping. The selection is controlled by reg0x7C[6:5] <_Tmkbc_sel_>. 00: set 0 01: set 1 10: set 2 11: set 3.

register.name: tmkbc_remap
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::tmkbc_remap. Read-write. Reset: 0000h.

field.name: tmkbc_remap
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: This register defines the remap address [15:8] on the LPC bus. There are actually four sets of such mapping. The selection is controlled by reg0x7C[6:5] <_Tmkbc_sel_>. 00: set 0 01: set 1 10: set 2 11: set 3.

register.name: wide_io_2
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::wide_io_2. Read-write. Reset: 0000h.

field.name: io_base_address_2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000h. 16-bit PCI I/O base address for wide generic port range. 512 byte wide range. This function is enabled by reg0x4B[1] <_Wide_io2_enable_>. When Alternative Wide Io 1 Range Enable <_0x74[3]_> is set to 1, the range is 16 bytes otherwise, it is defined as 512 bytes.

register.name: ec_lpc_cntrl
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::ec_lpc_cntrl. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: gec_shadowrom_address
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::gec_shadowrom_address. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: spi_base_addr
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::spi_base_addr. Read-write. Reset: FEC1_0012h.

field.name: spi_espi_baseaddr
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: FE_C100h. Description: This register defines the MMIO base address for the SPI ROM controller and eSPI host controller: SPI BAR = {SPI_BaseAddr[31:8],8'b0} HFP BAR = {SPI_BaseAddr[31:8],8'b0} + 0x0000_1000 eSPI BAR = {SPI_BaseAddr[31:8],8'b0} + 0x0001_0000 There bits will be write protected when PSP_RegxFC[8] <_Security_Mem_RegxFC[8]_> =1.

field.name: pspspimmiosel
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: 0: Spi Mmio register space map to Host spi_reg 1: Spi Mmio register space map to PSP psp_reg.

field.name: routetpm2spi
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When set, TPM cycles are routed to SPI bus with TPM_SPI_CS# asserted <_note TCG has not finalized the SPI TPM specification yet_>.

field.name: abortenable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. LPC Abort enable.

field.name: spiromenable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. When this bit is set and chip is strapped to SPI Rom, SPI Rom is enabled, otherwise SPI Rom is disabled.

field.name: altspicsenable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Alternative SPI CS enable.

register.name: ec_portaddress
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::ec_portaddress. Read-write. Reset: 012Eh.

register.name: pci_bar_64mb_rom3_low
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::pci_bar_64mb_rom3_low. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: bar_64mb_31_26
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Base address [31:26] of 64MB BIOS ROM space.

register.name: pci_bar_64mb_rom3_high
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::pci_bar_64mb_rom3_high. Read-only. Reset: 0000_00FDh.

field.name: bar_64mb_63_32
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0000_00FDh. Base address [63:32] of 64MB BIOS ROM space.

register.name: romdmasrcaddr
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::romdmasrcaddr. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: romdmadstaddr
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::romdmadstaddr. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: romdmacontrol
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::romdmacontrol. Read-write. Reset: 0000h.

register.name: eccontrol
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::eccontrol. Read-write. Reset: 6Dh.

register.name: hostcontrol
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::hostcontrol. Read-write. Reset: 03h.

field.name: lpcbuspullupen
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: 0: Disable LAD[3:0] internal pull-up 0: Enable LAD internal Pull-up.

field.name: prefetchenspifromhost
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. This is for performance enhancement purpose. When set, SPI controller will prefetch from the flash on behalf of the host.

register.name: ecromwroffset
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::ecromwroffset. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: ecromrdoffset
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::ecromrdoffset. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: clientromprotect
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::clientromprotect. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: autoromcfg
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::autoromcfg. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: clkgatecntrl
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::clkgatecntrl. Read-only. Reset: 86h.

field.name: lpcclkrunen
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1.

field.name: spionclkrun
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 1. Description: 0: Spi request can assert ClkRun# 1: Spi request doesn't assert ClkRun#.

field.name: clkgatecntrl
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 2h. Description: These two bits control whether the LPC module will allow clock gating to the internal 66Mhz core clock. 00: Disable the clock gating function 01: Wait 16 clocks before allowing clock gating to the LPC module 10: Wait 64 clocks before allowing clock gating to the LPC module 11: Wait 256 clocks before allowing clock gating to the LPC module.

register.name: clkpincntrl
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::clkpincntrl. Read-write. Reset: FFh.

field.name: lclk1en
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: 0: LPCCLK1 will be forced stop 1: LPCCLK1 will be functioning with CLKRUN protocol.

field.name: lclk0en
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: 0: LPCCLK0 will be forced stop 1: LPCCLK0 will be functioning with CLKRUN protocol.

register.name: clkrunovrid
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::clkrunovrid. Read-write. Reset: FFh.

field.name: lclk1clkrunovrid
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: 1: LPCCLK1 will be functioning with CLKRUN protocol 0: LPCCLK1 will be forced running.

field.name: lclk0clkrunovrid
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: 1: LPCCLK0 will be functioning with CLKRUN protocol 0: LPCCLK0 will be forced running.

register.name: clkrunen
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::clkrunen. Read-write. Reset: 08h.

field.name: clkrunen
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 1: ClkRun function is enabled and lclk0/lclk1 can be stopped. 0: ClkRun function is disabled and lclk0/lclk1 can be running all the time. Note: Should be set to 1 for mobile platforms for energy savings.

field.name: clkrundlycounter
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 08h. Specify the amount of the clk to be extended before stopping the lclk0/lclk1.

register.name: clkrunoption
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::clkrunoption. Read-write. Reset: 42h.

field.name: minassertion
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 4h. Specify the minimum time of ClkRun# assertion. Its unit is 30ns.

field.name: extendclkrunb
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 2h. Description: Bit[0]: 0: Delay 30ns before capturing ClkRun# input. 1: Delay 60ns before capturing ClkRun# input. Bit[1]: 0: PAD LPC_CLKRUN_L will drive 1 clock high before tri-state 1: PAD LPC_CLKRUN_L is open drain.

register.name: pci_misccntrl
register.desc: FCH::LPCPCICFG::pci_misccntrl. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

register.name: spi_cntrl0_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi_cntrl0_register. Reset: 0FC0_0000h.

field.name: spibusy
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: Read-only. 0: SPI bus is idle 1: SPI bus is busy Note: When SpiBusy=1, ROM access is going on, SpiReadMode[2:0] should not be changed and ExecuteOpCode should not be set to 1 to start new operation.

field.name: spireadmode_2_1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Description: These two bits and bit 18 in the same register Specify the Spi read mode: 000: Normal read <_up to 33M_> 001: Reserved 010: Dual-io <_1-1-2_> 011: Quad-io <_1-1-4_> 100: Dual-io <_1-2-2_> 101: Quad-io <_1-4-4_> 110: Normal read <_up to 66M_> 111: Fast read Note: before change SpiReadMode[2:0], software should read bit[31]: SpiBusy=0 to make sure no SPI operation is going on.

field.name: spiclkgate
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Set to 1 to skip the 8th spiclk at the end data when doing read.

field.name: spihostaccessromen
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Description: This is a clear-once protection bit once it is cleared to 0 it cannot be set back to 1. Once cleared to 0, some SPI registers cannot be written. Note: bit[23] and bit[22] were used for other function also, but those function were removed, so they are used for write-protection for certain registers only. Following registers are write-protect when bit[23:22] != 2'b11, 0x04[31:0] 0x08[31:0] 0x0C[31:24] 0x14[31:0] 0x18[31:0] 0x1D[1:0] 0x30[4:0] 0x40[31:0] 0x50[31:8] 0x54[31:8] 0x5C[7:0] 0x60[31:26] 0x64[31:0].

field.name: spiaccessmacromen
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. This is a clear-once protection bit once it is cleared to 0 it cannot be set back to 1. Once cleared to 0, some SPI registers cannot be written.

field.name: illegalaccess
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0. Description: Read Only. 0. Legal Index mode Access 1. Illegal Index mode Access.

field.name: spireadmode_0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: It is bit 0 of SpiReadMode to specify the spi read mode. Please see the definition of SpiReadMode in bit [30:29] below. Note: before change SpiReadMode[2:0], software should read bit[31]: SpiBusy=0 to make sure no SPI operation is going on.

register.name: spi_restrictedcmd_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi_restrictedcmd_register. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: restrictedcmd3
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Same as RestrictedCmd0.

field.name: restrictedcmd2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Same as RestrictedCmd0.

field.name: restrictedcmd1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Same as RestrictedCmd0.

field.name: restrictedcmd0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: This defines a restricted command code. If SPI_Opcode matches this register and address[23:19]! =0, 'Execute' bit of Index mode cannot be written, and the Index mode transfer will not occur. Note when either SpiAccessRomEn and/or SpiHostAccessRomEn bit are cleared, these registers become read-only and cannot be changed any more.

register.name: spi_restrictedcmd2_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi_restrictedcmd2_register. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: restrictedcmdwoaddr2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Same as RestrictedCmd0 except this command does not have address.

field.name: restrictedcmdwoaddr1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Same as RestrictedCmd0 except this command does not have address.

field.name: restrictedcmdwoaddr0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Same as RestrictedCmd0 except this command does not have address.

field.name: restrictedcmd4
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Same as RestrictedCmd0.

register.name: spi_cntrl1_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi_cntrl1_register. Read-write. Reset: 0200_0000h. When either bit[22] and/or bit[23] of offset 00h are cleared, bit[31:24] of Host_Mem_Reg:0Ch become read-only and cannot be changed any more.

field.name: bytecommand
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 02h. Specify the Byte Programmand Op Code.

register.name: spi_cmdvalue2_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi_cmdvalue2_register. Read-write. Reset: 020A_0B03h. When either bit[22] and/or bit[23] of offset 00h are cleared, bit[31:0] of Host_Mem_Reg:18h become read-only and cannot be changed any more.

field.name: bytewr
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 02h. This is used to compare against the opcode sent out by the Index mode. This is a predefined value to decode for the BYTEWR <_byte write_> command.

field.name: pagewr
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0Ah. This is used to compare against the opcode sent out by the Index mode. This is a predefined value to decode for the PAGEWR <_page write_> command.

field.name: fread
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0Bh. This is used to compare against the opcode sent out by the Index mode. This is a predefined value to decode for the Fread <_fast read_> command.

field.name: read_cmd
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 03h. This is used to compare against the opcode sent out by the Index mode. This is a predefined value to decode for the Read <_Read byte_> command.

register.name: alt_spi_cs_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::alt_spi_cs_register. Read-write. Reset: 08h. When either bit[22] and/or bit[23] of offset 00h are cleared, bit[1:0] of Host_Mem_Reg:1Dh become read-only and cannot be changed any more.

field.name: spiprotectlock
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Once set, bits 3, 4, and 5 are no longer writeable.

field.name: spiprotecten0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1. Enable SPI read/write protection base on PCI reg 0x50, 0x54, 0x58, 0x5C.

field.name: altspicsen
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Description: These two bits select the alternate SPI_CS# for BIOS_ROM 00b BIOS ROM select SPI_CS1_L 01b BIOS ROM select SPI_CS2_L 10b BIOS ROM select SPI_CS3_L 11b -- reserved.

register.name: spiindexaddr_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spiindexaddr_register. Reset: 00h.

register.name: spiindexdata_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spiindexdata_register. Reset: 00h.

register.name: spi100enable_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi100enable_register. Read-write. Reset: 3133_0700h.

field.name: normspeed
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 3h. Description: Configure the SPI bus normal speed in new SPI100 engine. If the command is not using TpmSpeed and FastSpeed, it will use NormSpeed. The speed definition: 000: 66.66MHz 001: 33.33MHz 010: 22.22MHz 011: 16.66MHz 100: 100MHz 101: 800KHz.

field.name: fastspeednew
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 1h. Description: Configure the SPI bus speed for the following command in new SPI100 engine: FAST READ DDR READ <_1-1-2_> QDR READ <_1-1-4_> DPR READ <_1-2-2_> QPR READ <_1-4-4_> The speed definition: 000: 66.66MHz 001: 33.33MHz 010: 22.22MHz 011: 16.66MHz 100: 100MHz 101: 800KHz.

field.name: altspeednew
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 3h. Description: Configure the SPI bus speed for the AltOpCode mode in new SPI100 engine The speed definition: 000: 66.66MHz 001: 33.33MHz 010: 22.22MHz 011: 16.66MHz 100: 100MHz 101: 800KHz.

field.name: tpmspeed
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 3h. Description: Configure the SPI bus speed for TPM read and write in new SPI100 engine. The speed definition: 000: 66.66MHz 001: 33.33MHz 010: 22.22MHz 011: 16.66MHz 100: 100MHz 101: 800KHz.

field.name: spicsdlysel
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 7h. Description: 0000: 0 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 0001: 1 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 0010: 2 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 0011: 3 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 0100: 4 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 0101: 5 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 0110: 6 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 0111: 7 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 1000: 8 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 1001: 9 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 1010: 10 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 1011: 11 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 1100: 12 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 1101: 13 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 1110: 14 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time. 1111: 15 SPI clock cycles of SpiCs# de-assertion time.

field.name: usespi100
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 0: Use old SPI100 engine <_verified in SB900_> 1: Use new SPI100 engine <_new since ERIE, support 100MHz_>.

register.name: spi100precyc0_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi100precyc0_register. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: spi100precyc1_config_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi100precyc1_config_register. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: spi100_host_prefetch_config_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi100_host_prefetch_config_register. Reset: 0000h. Prefetch Mechanism: Whenever there is read from Host, we will start to prefetch ROM data into the Host Prefetch Buffer. The prefetch will start from the first address requested by Host, and finish when any of the following happens: We have reached the maximum prefetch size defined in HostPrefetchSize and HostPrefOn64ByteBoundary register. When Host requests an address that is not already prefetched and not going to be prefetched shortly, we stop current prefetch action and re-start a new prefetch with the first address being the current address requested by Host. When there is a ROM-Write or AltOpCode request from Host, the on-going prefetch will be terminated, and the prefetch buffer will be flushed. When there is a TPM-Write, TPM-Read, USB-Read or EC-Read request, the on-going prefetch will be halted. The contents of prefetch buffer will be preserved so that Host can access them later. Prefetch Prediction: We have logics to predict how soon the data in the current requested address will be fetched into the buffer. There are two algorithms in place. And depends on the setting of HostWillHitEn and HostHitSoonEn, we use either one of them: "Will Hit" algorithm We say the current requested address will be fetched "shortly" if this equation is true: Current Requested Adress <= <_First Prefetched Address + HostPrefetchSize_> "Hit Soon" algorithm We say the current requested address will be fetched "shortly" if this equation is true: Current Requested Adress <= <_Last Prefetched Adress + HostHitRange_> Of course, the current requested address also has to be equal to or larger than the first prefetched address. If current requested address meet the criteria, the on-going prefetch will continue and Host will wait until the prefetch buffer received the requested data. With the register default values: Start prefetch only when host request on 64 byte boundary. Otherwise, get 1~4 bytes from ROM depends on byte-enable. When there is a ROM-write or a miss, flush the prefetch buffer immediately <_including on-going prefetch action_>. Once a 64 byte prefetch begins, we don't stop it until all 64 bytes are fetched, unless a ROM-write or a miss happens. "Miss" means the requested address is not in the range of the 64 bytes being prefetched.

register.name: tpm_spi_di_timeout_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::tpm_spi_di_timeout_register. Read-write. Reset: 8000h.

field.name: tpm_di_to_cnt
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 10h. SPI_DI_TO_counter is using 125ms Rtc8HzClk to increment, and these bits specify the timeout value. Due to synchronization, the actual delay is N*125ms to <_N+1_>*125ms. Default=16 for 2 seconds to 2.125 seconds. In spec, TIMEOUT_B is specified at 2 seconds.

field.name: tpm_di_to_status
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When bit[9]=1 and TPM_DI_TO_counter reaches timeout, this bit is set to '1'. Software can write '1' to clear it., write '0' has no effect.

field.name: tpm_di_to_enable
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When set to 1, if SPI_DI=0 during TPM access wait window, internal TPM_DI_TO_counter will start counting, once it reaches timeout value, internal SPI_DI will be forced to '1' to terminate TPM access, read cycle will return all '1' data, write cycle will be discarded.

register.name: rom2_addr_override_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::rom2_addr_override_register. Read-write. Reset: 14C0h. NOTE1: When either bit[22] and/or bit[23] of offset 00h are cleared, bit[4:0] of Host_Mem_Reg:30h become read-only and cannot be changed any more. NOTE2: 'ROM Address' used in this register description will be used as LPC ROM Address when LPC ROM is used, LPC ROM address does not use XOR. If SPI ROM is used, SPI ROM address [31:24] will be after XOR with offset x5C[7:0], SPIROM_page[31:24].

field.name: r2msk25
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When set to '1', R2VAL25 <_bit[1]_> will replace ROM Address[25] value when ROM2 range <_defined by LPC PCIx6C[31:0]_> is used to access ROM.

field.name: r2msk24
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When set to '1', R2VAL24 <_bit[0]_> will replace ROM Address[24] value when ROM2 range <_defined by LPC PCIx6C[31:0]_> is used to access ROM.

field.name: r2val25
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: When bit[3]=1, this bit will replace ROM Address[25] value when ROM2 range <_defined by LPC PCIx6C[31:0]_> is used to access ROM. When bit[3]=0, ROM Address[25] will be derived from Host Address.

field.name: r2val24
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: When bit[2]=1, this bit will replace ROM Address[24] value when ROM2 range <_defined by LPC PCIx6C[31:0]_> is used to access ROM. When bit[2]=0, ROM Address[24] will be derived from Host Address.

register.name: spi100_dummy_cycle_config_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi100_dummy_cycle_config_register. Read-write. Reset: 4608h. The cycle count for mode bits shall be included in the Dummy Cycle Config Register. For example, if ROM vendor requires 4 cycles of dummy bits and 2 cycles of mode bits in QPR Read, we should put 6 in QPR_DummyCyc[3:0] register. As a result, FCH will send 1 byte of mode bits and 2 bytes of dummy bits through Dout, Din, WP# and HOLD# pins. NOTE: For IndexMode, the dummy byte should be programmed into the IndexMode FIFO. The SPI100 Dummy Cycle Config Registers are not for IndexMode dummy bytes.

field.name: dpr_dummycyc
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 4h. Configure dummy cycle count for DPR READ <_1-2-2_> command. The cycle count shall include the cycle counts for both mode bits and dummy bits. <_See NOTE_>.

field.name: qpr_dummycyc
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 6h. Configure dummy cycle count for QPR READ <_1-4-4_> command. The cycle count shall include the cycle counts for both mode bits and dummy bits. <_See NOTE_>.

field.name: fastread_dummycyc
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 08h. Configure dummy cycle count for FAST READ command.

register.name: spi100_rx_timing_config0_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi100_rx_timing_config0_register. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: flushhostprefetch
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. If SW wants to fluxh the SPI100 Host prefetch buffer, SW can set this bit and then clear it.

register.name: spi100_rx_timing_config1_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi100_rx_timing_config1_register. Reset: 0000_0000h.

register.name: spi100_rx_timing_config2_register
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi100_rx_timing_config2_register. Read-write. Reset: 8800h.

register.name: ddrcmdcode
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::ddrcmdcode. Read-write. Reset: 3Bh.

field.name: ddr_cmd
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 3Bh. Command code of DDR <_1-1-2_> it is shadow register of SPI Extend register DDR_CMD.

register.name: qdrcmdcode
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::qdrcmdcode. Read-write. Reset: 6Bh.

field.name: qdr_cmd
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 6Bh. Command code of QDR <_1-1-4_> it is shadow register of SPI Extend register QDR_CMD.

register.name: dprcmdcode
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::dprcmdcode. Read-write. Reset: BBh.

field.name: dpr_cmd
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: BBh. Command code of DPR <_1-2-2_> it is shadow register of SPI Extend register DPR_CMD.

register.name: qprcmdcode
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::qprcmdcode. Read-write. Reset: EBh. When either bit[22] and/or bit[23] of offset 00h are cleared, bit[31:0] of Host_Mem_Reg:40h become read-only and cannot be changed any more.

field.name: qpr_cmd
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: EBh. Command code of QPR <_1-4-4_> it is shadow register of SPI Extend register QPR_CMD.

register.name: modebyte
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::modebyte. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: modebyte
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Whenever DPR_CMD or QPR_CMD is used, ModeByte is also sent out onto the SPI stream it is shadow register of SPI Extend register ModeByte.

register.name: cmdcode
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::cmdcode. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: spi_opcode
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. When software uses the alternate program method to communicate with the SPI ROM, this register contains the OPCODE <_command code_>.

register.name: cmdtrigger
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::cmdtrigger. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: execute
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: Write 1 to execute the transaction in the alternate program registers. Writing 0 has no effect. When the transaction is complete, this bit will return 0. If the command is an illegal command, the bit cannot be set and thereby cannot execute. Note: before set Execute=1 software should read Host_Mem_Regx4C[31]: SpiBusy=0 to make sure no SPI operation is going on.

register.name: txbytecount
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::txbytecount. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: txbytecount
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Number of bytes to be sent to SPI ROM in Index mode. This number does not include SPI_OpCode specified in offset 0x45.

register.name: rxbytecount
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::rxbytecount. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.name: rxbytecount
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Number of bytes to be received from the SPI ROM in Index mode.

register.name: spistatus
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spistatus. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: spibusy
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0. Description: 1: Spi bus is busy 0:Spi busy is idle.

field.name: donebytecount
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Indicate how many bytes has been received or sent in previous spi transaction.

register.name: addr32ctrl0
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::addr32ctrl0. Read-write. Reset: 0C13_1200h. When either bit[22] and/or bit[23] of offset 00h are cleared, bit[31:0] of Host_Mem_Reg:50h become read-only and cannot be changed any more.

field.name: freadcmd_addr_32
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0Ch. Fast Read Command Code of 32-bit address mode.

field.name: readcmd_addr_32
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 13h. Read Command Code of 32-bit address mode.

field.name: bytewrcmd_addr_32
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 12h. Byte Program Command Code of 32-bit address mode.

field.name: spi_rom_addr_32
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 0: 24-bit address SPI ROM 1: 32-bit address SPI ROM This bit can only be changed when Host_Mem_Regx00[31]: SpiBusy =0, since this bit will affect Host Address to SPI ROM address mapping.

register.name: addr32ctrl1
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::addr32ctrl1. Read-write. Reset: ECBC_6C3Ch. When either bit[22] and/or bit[23] of offset 00h are cleared, bit[31:0] of Host_Mem_Reg:54h become read-only and cannot be changed any more.

field.name: qpr_cmd_addr_32
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: ECh. QPR <_1-4-4_> Command Code of 32-bit address mode.

field.name: dpr_cmd_addr_32
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: BCh. DPR <_1-2-2_> Command Code of 32-bit address mode.

field.name: qdr_cmd_addr_32
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 6Ch. QDR <_1-1-4_> Command Code of 32-bit address mode.

field.name: ddr_cmd_addr_32
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 3Ch. DDR <_1-1-2_> Command Code of 32-bit address mode.

register.name: addr32ctrl2
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::addr32ctrl2. Read-write. Reset: 0000_4608h.

field.name: dpr_dummycyc_addr_32
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 4h. Configure dummy cycle count for DPR READ <_1-2-2_> command in 32-bit address mode <_24-bit address mode at 0x32[15:12]_>.

field.name: qpr_dummycyc_addr_32
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 6h. Configure dummy cycle count for QPR READ <_1-4-4_> command in 32-bit address mode <_24-bit address mode at 0x32[11:8]_>.

field.name: fastread_dummycyc_addr_32
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 08h. Configure dummy cycle count for FAST READ command in 32-bit address mode <_24-bit address mode at 0x32[4:0]_>.

register.name: addr32ctrl3
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::addr32ctrl3. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. When either bit[22] and/or bit[23] of offset 00h are cleared, bit[7:0] of Host_Mem_Reg:5Ch become read-only and cannot be changed any more.

field.name: spirom_page_31_24
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: Used for 32-bit address mode, produce SPIROMAddr[31:24] use XOR. SPIROMAddr[31:24] = SPI_ROM_page[31:24] ^ HostMemAddr[31:24] These bits can only be changed when Host_Mem_Regx00[31]: SpiBusy =0, since these bits will affect Host Address to SPI ROM address mapping.

register.name: bar_64mb_rom3_low
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::bar_64mb_rom3_low. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h. When either bit[22] and/or bit[23] of offset 00h are cleared, bit[31:26] of Host_Mem_Reg:60h become read-only and cannot be changed any more.

field.name: bar_64mb_31_26
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Specify Base address [31:26] of 64MB BIOS ROM space.

register.name: bar_64mb_rom3_high
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::bar_64mb_rom3_high. Read-write. Reset: 0000_00FDh. When either bit[22] and/or bit[23] of offset 00h are cleared, bit[31:0] of Host_Mem_Reg:64h become read-only and cannot be changed any more.

field.name: bar_64mb_63_32
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0000_00FDh. Specify Base address [63:32] of 64MB BIOS ROM space.

register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::fifo0. Read-write. Reset: 00h.

field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Data FiFo byte N which used in command mode to send or receive data. These FIFO can only be written in Byte, but they can be read in Double-word, Word, or Byte.

register.name: spi_misccntrl
register.desc: FCH::LPCHOSTSPIREG::spi_misccntrl. Read-write. Reset: 0000h.

field.name: psp_own_spi
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Read Only. Value of PSPxFC[5].

field.name: hfp_own_spi
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Read Only. Value of HFPxFC[5].

field.name: en_map_spi_bx_to_cfg
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. When set to 1, write/read to SPIxB0 xBF will be map to write/read LPC PCICFGxB0 xBF. This will allow processor to do SPI DMA Copy <_controlled by LPC PCICFGxB0 xB9_> through SPI Register space.

field.name: romcp_new_scheme
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: Set to '1' to allow direct or Index mode memory read to SPI ROM to be 'inserted' between SPI DMA Copy cache line transfer If this bit is '0', software should not do any SPI Memory read during SPI DMA Copy operation.

field.name: hbios_spimutex
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 0: Host-BIOS doesn't own SpiMutex 1: Host-BIOS owns SpiMutex Host-BIOS can't write the bit to 1 if the SpiMutex is owned by PSP <_PSPxFC[5]=1_> or Host-FP <_HFPxFC[5]=1_>.

register.name: DN_TXHDR_0th
register.desc: FCH::ITF::ESPI::DN_TXHDR_0th. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: DNCMD_HDATA2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on SW_CMD_TYPE Indipendent channel command selected: Reserved, Always be 00h Peripheral selected : Length[7:0] VW selected: Reserved, Always be 00h OOB selected: Length[7:0] FLASH selected: Length[7:0].

field.name: DNCMD_HDATA1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on SW_CMD_TYPE Independent channel command selected: Addres [7:0] of SET_CONFIGURATION/GET_CONFIGURATION Bit[1:0] needs to be 00. Note: In-Band command. These bits are ignored. Peripheral selected [23:20]: Tag [19:16]: Length[11:8] VW selected: Reserved, Always be 00h OOB selected: [23:20]: Tag [19:16]: Length[11:8] FLASH selected: [23:20]: Tag [19:16]: Length[11:8].

field.name: DNCMD_HDATA0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on SW_CMD_TYPE. Independent command selected: Address [15:8] of SET_CONFIGURATION/GET_CONFIGURATION. [15:12]:0h [11:8]: address[11:8] Note: In-Band command. These bits are ignored. Peripheral selected: a) SW programs this byte to be Message cycle type(0001xxxy) to instruct the eSPI controller send down peripheral message with data(8 bytes+data byte N) or without data(total 8 bytes) b) SW programs this byte to be 'nsuccessful completion to instruct the eSPI controller send down unsuccessful completion VW selected: It indicates the Virtual Wire Count will be send down. Bit[5:0] represends how many Virtual Wire groups to be commnunicated in the same packets. NOTE: In the current design, it is limited to 16 groups(bit[5:4]=00) to save the registers needed. Only 8 indexes are defined In current eSPI spec v0.7. OOB selected: SW programs this byte to be 0x21 to instruct the eSPI controller send send down Tunneled SMBUS message to slave SW prgrames this byte to be CycleType for Flash Completion, including Cpl/Unsuccessful Cpl/CplD.

field.name: SLAVE_SEL
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. 00: Slave0. others: Reserved.

field.name: DNCMD_STATUS
field.desc: Read,Write-0-only. Reset: 0. Description: RW0C. Set: The bit needs to be set last by Software after all eSPI specific registers are all programmed to inform the protocoal layer to send down command or packet. Clear: Hardware will automatically clear this bit after the packet is sent down.

field.name: DNCMD_TYPE
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. Description: TX Command Type: 000: Set Configuration (Independent command) 001: Get Configuration (Independent command) 010: In-band RESET command (Independent command) 011: Peripheral Unsuccesful Cpl down stream 100: Peripheral Channel message down stream 101: VW Channel down stream 110: OOB Channel down stream 111: Flash Channel Cpl/CplD/Unsuccesful Cpl down stream.

register.name: DN_TXHDR_1
register.desc: FCH::ITF::ESPI::DN_TXHDR_1. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: DNCMD_HDATA6
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on SW_CMD_TYPE Indipendent channel command selected: data[31:24] Peripheral selected : Message specific byte 2 VW selected: Reserved, Always be 00h OOB selected: Reserved, Always be 00h FLASH selected: Reserved, Always be 00h.

field.name: DNCMD_HDATA5
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on SW_CMD_TYPE Indipendent channel command selected: data[23:16] Peripheral selected : Message specific byte 1 VW selected: Reserved, Always be 00h OOB selected: SMBus Byte Count. Need to program not greater than 128bytes FLASH selected: Reserved, Always be 00h.

field.name: DNCMD_HDATA4
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on SW_CMD_TYPE Indipendent channel command selected: data[15:8] Peripheral selected : Message specific byte 0 VW selected: Reserved, Always be 00h OOB selected: SMBus Command Op Code. FLASH selected: Reserved, Always be 00h.

field.name: DNCMD_HDATA3
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on SW_CMD_TYPE Indipendent channel command selected: data[7:0] Peripheral selected : Message code [7:0] VW selected: Reserved, Always be 00h OOB selected: SMBus Slave Address. Bit[0] needs to program to 1 FLASH selected: Reserved, Always be 00h.

register.name: DN_TXHDR_2
register.desc: FCH::ITF::ESPI::DN_TXHDR_2. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: DNCMD_HDATA7
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on SW_CMD_TYPE Indipendent channel command selected: Reserved, Always be 00h Peripheral selected : Message specific byte 3 VW selected: Reserved, Always be 00h OOB selected: Reserved, Always be 00h FLASH selected: Reserved, Always be 00h.

register.name: DN_TXDATA_PORT
register.desc: FCH::ITF::ESPI::DN_TXDATA_PORT. Read-write. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: DN_TXDATA_B3
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on SW_CMD_TYPE Indipendent channel command selected: Reserved, Always be 00h Peripheral selected : Message Data DWn[31:24] VW selected: VW Index Group 2n+1 data OOB selected: OOB Message DWn[31:24] FLASH selected: Flash Cpl Data DWn[31:24].

field.name: DN_TXDATA_B2
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on SW_CMD_TYPE Indipendent channel command selected: Reserved, Always be 00h Peripheral selected : Message Data DWn[23:16] VW selected: VW Index Group 2n+1 OOB selected: OOB Message DWn[23:16] FLASH selected: Flash Cpl Data DWn[23:16].

field.name: DN_TXDATA_B1
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on SW_CMD_TYPE Indipendent channel command selected: Reserved, Always be 00h Peripheral selected : Message Data DWn[15:8] VW selected: VW Index Group 2n Data OOB selected: OOB Message DWn[15:8] FLASH selected: Flash Cpl Data DWn[15:8].

field.name: DN_TXDATA_B0
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on SW_CMD_TYPE Indipendent channel command selected: Reserved, Always be 00h Peripheral selected : Message Data DWn[7:0] VW selected: VW Index Group 2n OOB selected: OOB Message DWn[7:0] FLASH selected: Flash Cpl Data DWn[7:0].

register.name: UP_RXHDR_0
register.desc: FCH::ITF::ESPI::UP_RXHDR_0. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: UPCMD_HDATA2
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Description: RX_LOW_LEN: This field store the Length[7:0] from GET_FLASH_NP/GET_OOB [31:24] Length[7:0].

field.name: UPCMD_HDATA1
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Description: RX_TAG_LEN: This field store the Tag and Length[11:8] which from eSPI packet received by GET_FLASH_NP/GET_OOB [23:20], Tag [19:16] Length[11:8].

field.name: UPCMD_HDATA0
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Cycle Type This field store the cycle type from GET_FLASH_NP and GET_OOB.

field.name: SLAVE_SEL
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0h. Description: Slave N Received selected 00: The upstream packet is from Slave0 Others: Reserved.

field.name: UPCMD_STATUS
field.desc: Read,Write-1-to-clear. Reset: 0. Description: RW0C Valid bit Status: This bit will be set after received OOB message packet or Flash request packet, and eSPI will not send down another GET_OOB or GET_FLASH_NP before the Valid bit cleared by SW. This bit can be cleared by SW writing 1 to this field.

field.name: UPCMD_TYPE
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0h. Description: 000: Flash Channel Request (GET_FLASH_NP) 001: Upstream OOB message (GET_OOB) Others: Reserved.

register.name: UP_RXHDR_1
register.desc: FCH::ITF::ESPI::UP_RXHDR_1. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: UPCMD_HDATA6
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on RX_REQ_TYPE OOB selected: Reserved FLASH selected: Addess[7:0].

field.name: UPCMD_HDATA5
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on RX_REQ_TYPE OOB selected: SMBus Byte Count FLASH selected: Addess[15:8].

field.name: UPCMD_HDATA4
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on RX_REQ_TYPE OOB selected: SMBus Command Opcode FLASH selected: Addess[23:16].

field.name: UPCMD_HDATA3
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Description: The definition for this field is depended on RX_REQ_TYPE OOB selected SMBus Slave Address FLASH selected Addess[31:24].

field.name: ALERT_MODE_SEL
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0. Description: 0b: IO bit1 pin is used to signal the Alert event. 1b: A dedicated Alert# pin is used to signal the Alert event. Note: This bit can only be 0 in a single master-single slave topology. For single master-multiple slave topology, this bit must be programmed to 1.

field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 0h. I/O Mode Select. 00 Single I/O 01 Dual I/O 10 Quad I/O 11 Reserved.

field.name: CLK_FREQ_SEL

field.name: IRQ_STS
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 0h. Description: IRQ Satus: IRQ status which specified by IRQ selection: Bits Status 000 IRQ keep 0 unchanged 001 IRQ keep 1 unchanged 010 IRQ changed from 1 to 0 (Clear) 011 IRQ changed from 0 to 1 (Set) 100 IRQ changed from 0->1->0(High pulse) 101 IRQ changed from 1->0->1(Low pulse) 110 IRQ changed from 1->1->0 or 1->0->0 or 0->0->0 111 IRQ changed from 0->0->1 or 0->1->1 or 1->1->1.

field.name: IRQ_SEL
field.desc: Read-write. Reset: 00h. Description: This field determine Slave N Received Virtual Wires Register bit[7:5] output which IRQ status. Bits Selection 00000: IRQ0 00001 IRQ1 .... 10111:IRQ23 others:Reserved.

register.name: SLAVE0_RXVW_DATA
register.desc: FCH::ITF::ESPI::SLAVE0_RXVW_DATA. Read-only. Reset: 0000_0000h.

field.name: GRP3
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Description: Group3 Virtual Wire Data Register: When VW MISC CONTRL register bit3 set, eSPI master will check each received VW Index, if the received Index matches with Group3 Virtual Wire Index Selection Register, eSPI Master will update this field with the new received value.

field.name: GRP2
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Description: Group2 Virtual Wire Data Register: When VW MISC CONTRL register bit2 set, eSPI master will check each received VW Index, if the received Index matches with Group2 Virtual Wire Index Selection Register, eSPI Master will update this field with the new received value.

field.name: GRP1
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Description: Group1 Virtual Wire Data Register: When VW MISC CONTRL register bit1 set, eSPI master will check each received VW Index, if the received Index matches with Group1 Virtual Wire Index Selection Register, eSPI Master will update this field with the new received value.

field.name: GRP0
field.desc: Read-only. Reset: 00h. Description: Group0 Virtual Wire Data Register: When VW MISC CONTRL register bit0 set, eSPI master will check each received VW Index, if the received Index matches with Group0 Virtual Wire Index Selection Register, eSPI Master will update this field with the new received value.
